item.title: Redux 工具包：createAsyncThunk | Redux Toolkit: createAsyncThunk
item.description:
• 还原
  • 创建AsyncThunk

大家好！ 希望你们都做得很好。 在这篇博客中，我正在写关于 createAsyncThunk 的文章，但在深入探讨之前，我将解释 Redux 和 Redux 工具包。

什么是Redux ？

什么时候使用 Redux ？

通常，当您的应用程序大规模增长时，您很难管理状态。 现在你可以寻找像 Redux 这样的工具。 在一个地方管理状态很容易。

根据官方文档，该开发人员面临三个主要问题。

  • 现在让我们了解 Redux Toolkit

    什么是 Redux 工具包？

    配置存储（）

  • 创建减速器（）
  • 创建动作（）
  • 创建切片（）
  • 创建实体适配器（）
  • 创建选择器（）

您可以阅读有关Redux Toolkit的更多信息

现在您对 Redux 和 Redux Toolkit 有了基本的了解。 让我们了解 createAsyncThunk()

什么是 createAsyncThunk() ？

这允许延迟操作，包括使用 Promise。 关联

根据官方文档

这抽象了处理异步请求生命周期的标准推荐方法。

基本上， createAsyncThunk() 是带有三个参数的函数

  • 类型
  • 有效载荷创建者
  • 选项

让我们一一了解

类型：

一个字符串值生成一个常量动作类型。 它代表异步操作的生命周期。

  • 待定：'数据/getData/待定'
  • 已完成：“数据/getData/已完成”
  • 拒绝：'数据/getData/拒绝'

有效载荷创建者

一个有两个参数的函数

参数

调度 createAsyncThunk 函数时，可以传递给 thunk 创建者的单个值。 信号：这可用于取消请求。

  • 额外：设置时给予 thunk 中间件的“额外参数”（如果可用）

    • 选项

    它是一个带有选项字段的对象。

    使用 createAsyncThunk() 的主要原因。 它基于三种状态生成承诺生命周期操作类型。

    • 待定：'数据/getData/待定'
    • 已完成：“数据/getData/已完成”
    • 拒绝：'数据/getData/拒绝'

    让我们用一个例子来研究

    第1步：

    我们必须创建一个切片并使用 createAsyncThunk 函数进行 API 提取调用

    import { createAsyncThunk } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'; export const getAllData = createAsyncThunk( 'data/getData', async () => { return axios.get(`https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts`); } ); const initialState = { loading: false, data: [], error: "" } const dataSlice = createSlice({ name: 'data', initialState, extraReducers: { [getAllData.pending] = (state) => { state.loading = true; }, [getAllData.fulfilled] = (state, action) => { state.loading = 'Fulfilled'; state.data = action.payload; }, [getAllData.rejected] = (state) => { state.loading = false; state.error = "Error occurred" } } export default dataSlice.reducer;

    • 在 createAsyncThunk 调度数据/getData/pending 生命周期操作类型的初始调用上。

    • 如果我们的数据获取成功，data/getData/pending 动作类型就会被调度。

    • 如果分派了 data/getData/rejected，则 createAsyncThunk 函数返回一个包含错误的被拒绝承诺。

    • 在这个 extraReducer 中，我们对状态进行更改

    希望这个博客将给出 createAsyncThunk 的基本概述。

    感谢您的阅读。



    原文:
    • Redux
    • Redux Toolkit
    • createAsyncThunk

    Hello Everyone! Hope you all are doing great. 😊

    In this Blog I'm writing about createAsyncThunk but before diving into this I'll explain Redux and Redux toolkit.

    What is Redux?

    Redux is open source JavaScript library to managing state in JavaScript applications runs in different platform such as client side, server side. Redux commonly use with JavaScript libraries such as Reactjs, Angular etc. With redux all states of a application states kept in a store, which can be share to all component of your application.

    When use Redux ?

    In general, When your application grows at large scale, where managing states is difficult to you. Now you can look for tools like Redux. It's makes easy to managing states at one place.

    Problem with Redux ?

    According to official docs, There are three major problem with That developers are facing.

    • “Configuring a Redux store is too complicated”
    • “I have to add a lot of packages to get Redux to do anything useful”
    • “Redux requires too much boilerplate code”

    Now Let's Understand Redux Toolkit

    What is Redux Toolkit?

    Redux Toolkit is abstraction version of Redux.

    Redux toolkit is the new standard way to write redux logic which is solve our three major problem, which is mentioned above with Redux.

    Features comes with Redux Toolkit

    • configureStore()
    • createReducer()
    • createAction()
    • createSlice()
    • createAsyncThunk()
    • createEntityAdapter()
    • createSelector()

    You can read more about Redux Toolkit

    Now You have a basic knowledge of Redux and Redux Toolkit. Let's understand createAsyncThunk()

    What is createAsyncThunk() ?

    Redux Thunk is middleware that allows you to return functions, rather than just actions, within Redux. This allows for delayed actions, including working with promises. link

    According to the official docs

    This abstracts the standard recommended approach for handling async request lifecycles.

    createAsyncThunk is a middleware to perform asynchronous operations such as fetching an API, This package included by Default with Redux Toolkit.
    Basically, createAsyncThunk() is function which is take three parameter

    • type
    • payloadCreator
    • options

    Let's understand one by one

    type:

    "data/getData" (reducerName/actionType)

    A string value generate a constant action type. It represent the life cycle of a async operations.

    • pending: 'data/getData/pending'
    • fulfilled: 'data/getData/fulfilled'
    • rejected: 'data/getData/rejected'

    payloadCreator

    A function with two parameter

    arg

    A single value that can be passed to the thunk creator, when createAsyncThunk function dispatched. (we'll see through an example.)

    thunkAPI

    ThunkAPI is an object, contains all the parameter that can be passed to redux thunk function.

    • dispatch: for dispatching different actions.
    • getState: to access the redux store from within the callback
    • requestId: this is a unique id redux-toolkit generates for each request
    • signal: this can be used to cancel request.
    • rejectWithValue: It is a utility function that can returns to the action creator a defined payload, in case of error.
    • extra: the “extra argument” given to the thunk middleware on setup, if available

    Options

    It is an object with options field.

    The main reason to use createAsyncThunk(). It generates promise life cycle action types based on three states.

    • pending: 'data/getData/pending'
    • fulfilled: 'data/getData/fulfilled'
    • rejected: 'data/getData/rejected'

    Let's Examine with an example

    Step1:

    We have to create a slice and make a API fetch call with createAsyncThunk function

    import { createAsyncThunk } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';

export const getAllData = createAsyncThunk(
  'data/getData',
  async () => {
    return axios.get(`https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts`);
  }
);

const initialState = {
    loading: false,
    data: [],
    error: ""
}

const dataSlice = createSlice({
    name: 'data',
    initialState,
    extraReducers: {
      [getAllData.pending] = (state) => {
        state.loading = true;
      },

      [getAllData.fulfilled] = (state, action) => {
        state.loading = 'Fulfilled';
        state.data = action.payload;
      },

      [getAllData.rejected] = (state) => {
        state.loading = false;
        state.error = "Error occurred"
      }
    }

export default dataSlice.reducer;

    • On the Initial Call of the createAsyncThunk dispatch data/getData/pending lifecycle action type.

    • If our data fetch is successful, the data/getData/pending action type gets dispatched.

    • In case of data/getData/rejected is dispatched, then createAsyncThunk function return a rejected promise containing with an error.

    • In this extraReducer, we make our changes to the states

    Hope This blog will give basic overview of createAsyncThunk .

    Thank you for reading.
item.author: pritamkr_63

item.title: 为什么您需要 SEO 代理？ | Why Do You Need Proxies for SEO?
item.description:
代理具有许多有用的功能，因此不将这些功能应用于互联网营销的主要驱动力之一——SEO 是错误的。 在本文中，我们将探讨如何利用代理来增强营销策略并以安全可靠的方式充分利用在线形象。

什么是搜索引擎优化？

让我们快速回顾一下基础知识。 搜索引擎优化，或简称 SEO，是增加从搜索引擎访问网站的访问者数量以及这些访问者作为潜在客户的质量的过程。 最终目标是在相关用户的搜索请求的建议站点列表中出现更高的位置。 为了吸引更多的无偿流量，营销人员需要跟随搜索引擎算法的趋势，但更重要的是，要时刻关注客户行为的变化——他们使用的搜索词或关键词，以及竞争对手对此的反应。 获得这些知识并利用它来优化网络内容是 SEO 代理发挥作用的时候。

为什么代理对 SEO 至关重要？

代理是用户和目标网站之间的中介。 它们充当缓冲区，匿名化客户的 IP 地址并提供更安全的互联网使用方式。 有几种类型的代理，例如，数据中心或住宅代理。 所有代理的最终目标是用户的安全和隐私，类似于 VPN，但不完全相同。 您可以在我们之前的博客中了解更多关于它们之间的差异。 代理可以通过多种方式应用于 SEO。 让我们一一介绍。


网页抓取：
跟踪竞争对手的活动是经营业务的基本要素。 然而，手动组装这些信息并保持更新可能是一项艰巨的任务。 此外，重复访问竞争对手的网站可能会引发他们的怀疑，甚至您的 IP 地址被封锁。 使用带有代理的网络抓取工具可能是最终的解决方案。 它允许从竞争对手的网站自动收集请求的信息，例如价格、特价、标题标签、元描述和关键字。 分析此类数据可以增强您的营销策略。 搜索引擎优化代理将隐藏爬虫的身份，而情报收集活动将不会被注意到。

测试和检查结果：
对网站进行调整不是一次性的活动。 搜索引擎优化是一个持续的过程，涉及测试不同的假设并检查它们对排名的影响。 代理允许您跟踪此类结果并了解竞争对手的表现（关键字、排名、点击次数和流量。）

对于来自不同位置的用户，某些 Web 内容的显示可能会有所不同。 促销没有限制：
广告是营销策略的重要组成部分。 但是，频繁开展促销活动确实需要大量带宽。 搜索引擎优化代理可以帮助实现最大的结果，而不会有被阻止 IP 的风险。

竞争对手分析：
即使不使用网络抓取技术，竞争对手分析过程的匿名性也应该是最重要的。 对他们的关键词、标题、反向链接策略和视觉效果的彻底审查将需要对他们的网站进行大量和长时间的访问。 通过不断更改您的 IP，SEO 的轮换代理可以确保不会发现研究人员的存在，并且他们的真实 IP 保持畅通。

社交媒体管理：
社交媒体允许品牌与受众建立并保持联系，同时也将他们转化为客户。 许多企业需要管理多个社交媒体帐户并发送大量消息。 这可能会引发平台的怀疑和封锁。 它们允许从不同的地理位置访问，绕过社交媒体平台设置的任何限制。

创建高质量的反向链接：
反向链接，有时称为入站、传入或单向链接，是 SEO 的重要驱动力之一。 对于搜索引擎来说，从一个网站到另一个网站的这种链接是有价值的认可的象征。 背书人的影响力越大，这种联系的价值就越高。 还考虑了与特定主题的链接的相关性。 高质量的代言会导致高有机搜索引擎排名。 代理可以帮助以安全有效的方式为反向链接机会抓取信息。

提高页面加载速度：
没有人喜欢等待。 搜索引擎也不希望他们的用户这样做，并优先考虑加载速度更快的网站的排名。 首次访问时，它们使用缓存技术存储部分数据。 因此，任何后续请求都会加载得更快。 并且该网站可以在搜索引擎排名中出现更高的位置。

为您的业务使用基于地理的 IP 地址向特定受众进行宣传：
如前所述，SEO 代理允许从位于特定区域的用户的角度查看搜索引擎结果和其他网站。 因此，企业可以确保他们的 SEO 工作与目标领域相关。 此外，公司可以在其活动中专门针对特定位置的 IP 地址。 这使得无论位置如何都能在国际上发展业务。

鉴于在线竞争的激烈程度，弄清楚如何让搜索引擎为您的利益服务对于任何规模的企业都极为重要。 也就是说，公司应该付出很多努力来提高他们的搜索引擎排名，超越他们的竞争对手，并为他们的网站带来高质量的流量。 代理可以帮助您，并将您的 SEO 优化工作推向新的高度，同时还为您提供匿名性。



原文:

Proxies have many useful features, so it would be a mistake not to apply those to one of the main drivers of Internet marketing – SEO. In this article, we’ll explore how proxies can be utilised for enhancing marketing strategies and making the most of the online presence in a safe and secure way.

What Is SEO?

Let’s quickly recap the basics. Search Engine Optimization, or simply SEO, is a process of increasing the number of visitors coming to the website from the search engine and the quality of those visitors as potential customers. The ultimate goal is to appear higher up in the list of the proposed sites for the relevant user’s search request. In order to attract more unpaid traffic, marketers need to follow trends of search engine algorithms but, more importantly, stay attuned to the changes in the customers’ behaviour – the search terms or keywords they use, and how competitors are reacting to that. Gaining such knowledge and leveraging it for the optimisation of the web content is when proxies for SEO come into play.

Why Proxies are Essential for SEO?

Proxies are intermediaries between a user and a targeted website. They act as a buffer, anonymising their clients’ IP addresses and providing a safer way to use the Internet. There are several types of proxies, for example, datacenter or residential proxies. The ultimate goal of all the proxies is the security and privacy of their users, similar to VPN, but not exactly the same. You can read more about their differences in our earlier blog. There are numerous ways how proxies can be applied for SEO. Let’s go through them one by one.


Web Scraping:
Keeping track of competitors’ activities is an essential element of running the business. However, assembling such information manually and keeping it updated might be a laborious task. Furthermore, repetitive access to the rival’s websites can trigger their suspicion and even your IP address blockage. Using web scraping tools with proxies can be an ultimate solution. It allows automated collection of the requested information from the competitors’ sites, such as prices, special offers, title tags, meta descriptions and keywords. Analysis of such data can enhance your marketing strategy. SEO proxies will keep the scraper’s identity hidden and the intelligence-gathering activities will remain unnoticed.

Testing and Checking Results:
Making adjustments to the website is not a one-off activity. SEO is a continuous process that involves testing different hypotheses and checking what effect they have on the ranking. Proxies allow you to keep track of such results and be aware of the competitor’s performance (keywords, ranking, clicks, and traffic.)

Some web content can appear differently for users from different locations. Proxies for SEO can help solve this problem by providing a geo-specific IP address, making it possible to analyse local markets and test websites as if local users were seeing them.

No Limits on Promotions:
Advertisement is an essential part of a marketing strategy. However, running frequent promotional campaigns does require lots of bandwidth. Proxies for SEO can help achieve maximum results without the risk of getting your IP blocked.

Competitor Analysis:
Even without employing web scraping techniques, the anonymity of the competitors’ analysis process should be paramount. A thorough review of their keywords, titles, backlinking strategies, and visuals will require numerous and prolonged visits to their websites. By constantly changing your IP, rotating proxies for SEO can ensure a researcher’s presence is not spotted and their real IP remains unblocked.

Social Media Management:
Social media allows brands to build and maintain connections with their audiences, while also convert them into customers. Many businesses require managing several social media accounts and sending out a high volume of messages. That can trigger suspicion and blockage from platforms. SEO proxies can help here as well. They allow access from different geolocations, bypassing any restrictions set by social media platforms.

Creating Quality Backlinks:
Backlinks, sometimes referred to as inbound, incoming, or one way links, are one of the important drivers of SEO. For search engines, such links from one website to another are symbols of valuable endorsement. The more influential the endorser is, the higher value such linkage has. Relevance of the link to the particular topic is also accounted for. Good quality endorsements result in high organic search engine rankings. Proxies can facilitate scraping information for the backlinking opportunities in a safe and efficient manner.

Increasing the Speed of Page Loading:
Nobody likes waiting. Search engines also don’t want that for their users and prioritise ranking for the websites with better loading speed. SEO proxies contribute to better scores on this parameter. Upon first access, they store part of the data using caching techniques. Because of that, any subsequent requests will load faster. And the website can appear higher on the search engine ranks.

Using Geo-based IP Addresses for Your Business Promoting to Specific Audiences:
As mentioned before, SEO proxies allow viewing search engine results and other websites from the perspective of a user located in a specific area. Thus, businesses can ensure their SEO efforts are relevant to the targeted areas. Additionally, companies can specifically target location-specific IP addresses within their campaigns. That allows growing business internationally regardless of the location.

Given how fierce online competition is, figuring out how to make search engines work for your benefit is extremely important for businesses of any scale. That said, companies should pay a lot of effort to improve their search engines ranks, outshine their competitors , and bring high quality traffic to their websites. Proxies can help you with that and drive your SEO optimization efforts to new heights, while also providing you with anonymity.
item.author: dnasedkina

item.title: 在您的 AWS CDK 部署的 Lambda 函数中安全地使用密钥 | Securely Use Secrets In Your AWS CDK Deployed Lambda Function
item.description:
您是否曾经需要安全访问 Lambda 函数中的登录详细信息等内容？ 您可能需要连接到数据库或其他服务，但您不希望对您的凭据进行硬编码。 好吧，让我们通过使用AWS Secrets Manager获取我们的登录详细信息来使用最佳安全实践！

我将使用 Python 风格的AWS CDK部署一个 Lambda 函数，该函数有权访问Secrets Manager中存储的密钥。 我还将向您展示如何在您的 lambda 函数中安全地检索此秘密。

在此处查看 GitHub 存储库。

在进入 CDK 组件之前，让我们使用 AWS CLI 创建一个示例密钥：

 aws secretsmanager create-secret --name secretsExample --secret-string "TestPass123"

堆栈设计

在这里，我们通过创建一个 lambda 函数并授予它读取我们的秘密的权限来构建 CDK 堆栈。 我们可以将秘密的名称作为环境变量传递给 lambda 函数，以节省一些重复。

 import os from aws_cdk import Stack from aws_cdk import aws_lambda as _lambda from aws_cdk import aws_secretsmanager as secrets from constructs import Construct class CdkLambdaSecretsStack ( Stack ): def __init__ ( self , scope : Construct , construct_id : str , ** kwargs ) -> None : super (). __init__ ( scope , construct_id , ** kwargs ) self . build_lambda_func () def build_lambda_func ( self ): secret_name = "secretsExample" self . secrets_lambda = _lambda . Function ( scope = self , id = "LambdaWithSecrets" , runtime = _lambda . Runtime . PYTHON_3_9 , function_name = "LambdaWithSecretsExample" , code = _lambda . Code . from_asset ( path = "lambda_funcs/lambda_with_secrets" ), handler = "lambda_with_secrets.handler" , # We need these env vars to access the secret inside the lambda environment = { "secret_name" : secret_name , "secret_region" : os . environ [ "CDK_DEFAULT_REGION" ], }, ) # Grant permission to the Lambda func to access the secret example_secret = secrets . Secret . from_secret_name_v2 ( scope = self , id = "secretExample" , secret_name = secret_name ) example_secret . grant_read ( grantee = self . secrets_lambda )

在这里，我们使用辅助函数get_secrets()定义了一个 lambda 函数。 当您在 AWS 控制台中查看密钥时，这是 AWS 建议的“示例代码”功能的略微修改版本。 我们可以简单地使用密钥的名称，而不是使用ARN来访问密钥。 我发现我无法使用 CDK 获取密钥的full ARN ，但幸运的是get_secret_value()接受了SecretId参数的密钥名称。

 import base64 import json import os import boto3 from botocore.exceptions import ClientError def get_secret (): """ Gets the secret """ # Get env vars we passed in the stack. secret_name = os . getenv ( "secret_name" ) region_name = os . getenv ( "secret_region" ) # Create a Secrets Manager client session = boto3 . session . Session () client = session . client ( service_name = "secretsmanager" , region_name = region_name ) # In this sample we only handle the specific exceptions for the 'GetSecretValue' API. # See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/apireference/API_GetSecretValue.html # We rethrow the exception by default. try : # We need only the name of the secret get_secret_value_response = client . get_secret_value ( SecretId = secret_name ) except ClientError as e : if e . response [ "Error" ][ "Code" ] == "DecryptionFailureException" : # Secrets Manager can't decrypt the protected secret text using the provided KMS key. # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion. raise e elif e . response [ "Error" ][ "Code" ] == "InternalServiceErrorException" : # An error occurred on the server side. # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion. raise e elif e . response [ "Error" ][ "Code" ] == "InvalidParameterException" : # You provided an invalid value for a parameter. # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion. raise e elif e . response [ "Error" ][ "Code" ] == "InvalidRequestException" : # You provided a parameter value that is not valid for the current state of the resource. # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion. raise e elif e . response [ "Error" ][ "Code" ] == "ResourceNotFoundException" : # We can't find the resource that you asked for. # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion. raise e else : raise e else : # Decrypts secret using the associated KMS key. # Depending on whether the secret is a string or binary, one of these fields will be populated. if "SecretString" in get_secret_value_response : secret = get_secret_value_response [ "SecretString" ] # If you have multiple secret values, you will need to json.loads(secret) here and then access the values using dict keys return secret else : decoded_binary_secret = base64 . b64decode ( get_secret_value_response [ "SecretBinary" ] ) return decoded_binary_secret def handler ( event , context ): secret = get_secret () # Don't do this in production!!! return secret

测试 Lambda 函数

拆除

要删除我们创建的秘密：

 aws secretsmanager delete-secret --secret-id secretsExample

销毁你的堆栈：

享受！



原文:

Have you ever needed secure access to things like login details in your Lambda functions? You might need to connect to a database or other service, but you don’t want to need to hard-code your credentials. Well, let’s use best security practices by getting our login details using AWS Secrets Manager!

I will be using Python flavored AWS CDK to deploy a Lambda function which has permissions to access a secret stored in Secrets Manager. I will also show you how to retrieve this secret securely inside your lambda function.

See the GitHub repository here.

Before getting into the CDK components, let’s create an example secret using AWS CLI:

aws secretsmanager create-secret --name secretsExample --secret-string "TestPass123"

Stack Design

cdk_lambda_secrets/cdk_lambda_secrets_stack.py

Here we build the CDK stack by creating a lambda function and giving it permission to read our secret. We can pass the name of the secret into the lambda function as an environment variable to save ourselves some repetition.

import os from aws_cdk import Stack
from aws_cdk import aws_lambda as _lambda
from aws_cdk import aws_secretsmanager as secrets
from constructs import Construct class CdkLambdaSecretsStack(Stack): def __init__(self, scope: Construct, construct_id: str, **kwargs) -> None: super().__init__(scope, construct_id, **kwargs) self.build_lambda_func() def build_lambda_func(self): secret_name = "secretsExample" self.secrets_lambda = _lambda.Function( scope=self, id="LambdaWithSecrets", runtime=_lambda.Runtime.PYTHON_3_9, function_name="LambdaWithSecretsExample", code=_lambda.Code.from_asset( path="lambda_funcs/lambda_with_secrets" ), handler="lambda_with_secrets.handler", # We need these env vars to access the secret inside the lambda
 environment={ "secret_name": secret_name, "secret_region": os.environ["CDK_DEFAULT_REGION"], }, ) # Grant permission to the Lambda func to access the secret
 example_secret = secrets.Secret.from_secret_name_v2( scope=self, id="secretExample", secret_name=secret_name ) example_secret.grant_read(grantee=self.secrets_lambda)

Lambda Function

lambda_funcs/lambda_with_secrets/lambda_with_secrets.py

Here we define a lambda function with a helper function get_secrets(). This is a very slightly modified version of the ‘Sample code’ function suggested by AWS when you look at the secret in the AWS Console. Rather than use the ARN to access the secret, we can simply use the name of the secret. I found that I was unable to get the full ARN of the secret using CDK, but luckily get_secret_value() accepts the secret name for its SecretId parameter.

import base64
import json
import os import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError def get_secret(): """ Gets the secret """ # Get env vars we passed in the stack.
 secret_name = os.getenv("secret_name") region_name = os.getenv("secret_region") # Create a Secrets Manager client
 session = boto3.session.Session() client = session.client( service_name="secretsmanager", region_name=region_name ) # In this sample we only handle the specific exceptions for the 'GetSecretValue' API.
 # See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/apireference/API_GetSecretValue.html
 # We rethrow the exception by default.
 try: # We need only the name of the secret
 get_secret_value_response = client.get_secret_value( SecretId=secret_name ) except ClientError as e: if e.response["Error"]["Code"] == "DecryptionFailureException": # Secrets Manager can't decrypt the protected secret text using the provided KMS key.
 # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion.
 raise e elif e.response["Error"]["Code"] == "InternalServiceErrorException": # An error occurred on the server side.
 # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion.
 raise e elif e.response["Error"]["Code"] == "InvalidParameterException": # You provided an invalid value for a parameter.
 # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion.
 raise e elif e.response["Error"]["Code"] == "InvalidRequestException": # You provided a parameter value that is not valid for the current state of the resource.
 # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion.
 raise e elif e.response["Error"]["Code"] == "ResourceNotFoundException": # We can't find the resource that you asked for.
 # Deal with the exception here, and/or rethrow at your discretion.
 raise e else: raise e else: # Decrypts secret using the associated KMS key.
 # Depending on whether the secret is a string or binary, one of these fields will be populated.
 if "SecretString" in get_secret_value_response: secret = get_secret_value_response["SecretString"] # If you have multiple secret values, you will need to json.loads(secret) here and then access the values using dict keys
 return secret else: decoded_binary_secret = base64.b64decode( get_secret_value_response["SecretBinary"] ) return decoded_binary_secret def handler(event, context): secret = get_secret() # Don't do this in production!!!
 return secret

cdk deploy this stack and you should have a working example.

Test the Lambda Function

The Lambda function successfully retrieved the secret value from AWS Secrets Manager!

Tear Down

To delete the secret we created:

aws secretsmanager delete-secret --secret-id secretsExample

Destroy your stack:

Enjoy!
item.author: wesleycheek

item.title: 使用这些技巧来建立最佳的全渠道电子商务零售策略 | Use THESE Tips to Build the Best Omnichannel eCommerce Retail Strategy
item.description:
介绍

让我们不要直接跳入“全渠道电子商务零售策略”。 相反，让我们停下来问问自己； 在过去的两年里，是什么以难以想象的方式改变了世界？ 零售购物和运输的方式。 大流行之后，这个领域不再是孩子的游戏（如果以前曾经是的话）：由于竞争和需求，无缝的客户旅程有时会令人生畏。

即使没有世界范围内的医疗紧急情况，人们天生懒惰购物的事实也不是隐藏的事实。 让你的手机为你做这一切也无济于事，因为它让我们在购买东西时更加放松。 只需在智能手机上轻点几下，您就可以在家门口获得想要的东西。 谁不会以这种方式享受无缝的购物体验？

什么是电子商务中的全渠道策略？

大流行加速了许多在电子商务世界中进展缓慢的功能和工具。 它为数字零售营销人员创造了比以往任何时候都更加闪耀的黄金机会。 这就是全渠道电子商务零售策略的用武之地。

全渠道策略是连贯的用户体验，可在任何客户旅程点提供集成的用户体验。 它与传统的多渠道战略方法相反，后者将单个渠道作为单独的实体进行优化。 由于人们越来越倾向于在线购买东西，因此在线和离线产生潜在客户的努力应该相同。 凭借全渠道体验，客户有望得到这一点。

完美的全渠道零售客户体验必须包括确保一致品牌体验的策略。 客户可以快速与品牌建立关系，轻松识别品牌形象——最好的方法是专注于渠道多元化，全面融合数据和系统。

那么，有哪些妙招可以带来更好的凝聚力品牌体验呢？ 以下是制定杀手级零售策略的一些最实用的方法：

审核现有模型

你总是需要确定你站在哪里去一个新的地方。 在制定全渠道零售政策时，这些步骤并没有什么不同。 审计有助于合理化您的成功和失败，以及组织中的每个人如何与客户互动。 您可以在客户反馈和评论中看到这些见解。 一个更好的解决方法是像客户一样思考。

人口狩猎

使全渠道策略发挥最佳作用的是获得准确的客户数据。 它包括客户人口统计数据、在线购物习惯以及可能诱使他们购买您的产品的因素。 您可能已经通过网站 cookie 和社交媒体参与统计记录了您的受众，但要真正获得您想要的结果，您需要汇集这些数据。

专注于正确的渠道

在跨多渠道创造有凝聚力的体验时，经常被忽视的关键方面之一是选择合适的渠道。 您不必全力以赴来瞄准您的受众。 使用适当的渠道可以增加为客户创造有影响力的体验的机会。 一个很好的方法是设计一个与多种设备兼容的网站。

相关内容生成

在规划零售策略时，请始终查看您的受众消费的内容。 确保您的促销信息不会令人讨厌并完成工作。 观察你的观众最活跃在社交媒体上发布内容的时间，因为这会给你带来最好的结果。

让每个接触点都可购物

以大型营销巨头迪士尼为例。 你去他们的商店、公园或移动应用程序，你会得到相同的购买选择和体验。 作为零售策略师，您的目标是通过使每个接触点可销售来确保各个层面的销售额保持不变。 您甚至不必为此花钱，您可以使用社交媒体平台来做到这一点。 如果您的所有渠道都没有强迫人们以一种或另一种方式使用您的产品结帐，那么您可能会失败的全渠道策略。

结论

那些适应全渠道营销风格的企业从长远来看表现良好。 他们在竞争中保持领先，并且相关的时间更长。 如果您不知道如何通过有效的全渠道战略来塑造您的电子商务业务，请联系可以帮助激活您的离线和在线渠道的专家。 他们负责后端基础架构、安全性、支持和托管。



原文:

Introduction

Let’s not jump directly at “omnichannel eCommerce retail strategy.” Instead, let’s pause and ask ourselves; What has transformed the world in ways unimaginable in the past two years? The way retail shopping and shipment are made. After the pandemic, this field is not a child’s play (if it ever was before): a seamless customer journey can sometimes be daunting because of the competition and demand.

Even without a world-blocking medical emergency, the fact that people behave inherently lazily towards shopping is no hidden fact. Having your phone do it all for you is not helping either because it has made us even more relaxed about buying stuff. 39% of the millennials admit to relying on online or eCommerce shopping rather than going out to shop, which says a lot about modern consumerism. With a few taps on a smartphone, you have what you want right at your doorstep. Who wouldn’t enjoy a seamless shopping experience that way?

What is Omnichannel Strategy in eCommerce?

The pandemic accelerated many features and tools that were slow to progress otherwise in the eCommerce world. It has generated this golden opportunity for digital retail marketers to shine brighter than ever before. And this is where omnichannel eCommerce retail strategies come to the rescue.

Omnichannel strategies are coherent user experiences that provide integrated user experience at any customer journey point. It is opposed to a traditional multi-channel strategic approach, which optimizes individual channels as separate entities. Since people are increasingly inclined to buy something online, there should be the same amount of effort to generate leads online and offline. With omnichannel experience, the customers are expected to get just that.

A perfect omnichannel retail customer experience must include strategies that ensure a consistent brand experience. Customers can quickly build relationships with the brand and identify the brand image with ease—the best approach is to focus on channel diversification and merging data and systems comprehensively.

Omnichannel Strategies That Work 100%

So, what are the magic tips which can lead to a better cohesion brand experience? Here are some of the most practical ways you can make a killer retail strategy:

Audit the Existing Model

You always need to determine where you stand to go to a new place. When it comes to making policies for omnichannel retailing, the steps aren’t any different. Auditing helps rationalize your success and failures and how everyone in your organization interacts with the customer. You can see these insights reflected in customer feedback and reviews. A better way to go about it is to think like a customer.

Demographic Hunt

What makes the omnichannel strategy work best is obtaining the exact customer data. It includes customer demographics, online shopping habits, and what is likely to entice them to buy your product. You might already be taking note of your audience via website cookies and social media engagement statistics, but to really get the results you want, you need to pool this data.

Focus on Right Channels

While creating a cohesive experience across multi-channels, one of the critical aspects often neglected is choosing the proper channels. You do not have to go all out to target your audience. Using proper channels can increase your chances of creating impactful experiences for your customers. A great way to do this is to ensign a website compatible with multiple devices.

Relevant Content Generation

When planning a retail strategy, always look at what content your audience consumes. Ensure that your promotional messages are not bothersome and get the job done. Observe the time when your audience is the most active in posting something on social media because that will give you the best results.

Make Each Touchpoint Shoppable

Take the example of Disney, a mega marketing giant. You go to their stores, parks, or mobile app, and you get the same buying options and experience. Your goal as a retail strategist is to ensure the sales remain the same from every level by making each touchpoint sellable. You do not even have to spend money on this, and you can do this using social media platforms. If all your channels aren’t forcing people to checkout with your product in one way or another, you might be failing the omnichannel strategy.

Conclusion

Those businesses that adapt to the omnichannel marketing style fare well in the long run. They remain ahead of the competition and relevant for longer. If you do not know how to shape your eCommerce business with a working omnichannel strategy, contact experts who can help vitalize your offline and online channels. They take care of the back-end infrastructure, security, support, and hosting.
item.author: ropstamsolutions12

item.title: 沉浸式学习的热门话题是什么？ | What’s all the buzz about Immersive Learning?
item.description:
智慧不是学校教育的产物，而是终生尝试获得它——阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦

学习新技能或参加课程可以帮助您在职业生涯中取得重大进展。 或者甚至只是增强你的信心。 对于在学习新技能和成人生活需求之间兼顾的成人学习者来说，电子学习的好处是多方面的。

本周的资源集中在一个新兴的流行语上：“沉浸式学习”。 沉浸式学习使学习者远离知识同化的有意识任务，而是创造沉浸式环境，激发兴趣并触发突触，以更有吸引力和更有效的方式取得成果。

第一个资源是 Memcorp 沉浸式学习首席执行官 Mohsin Memon 关于“通过沉浸式学习实现无限增长”的富有洞察力的演讲的链接。 他谈到了沉浸式学习如何让我们有机会通过新的学习体验过上新的生活。

第二个资源是浣熊帮的链接。 这篇文章解释了沉浸式学习是什么以及创造沉浸式体验的不同方法。

最后一个资源是 Ken Taylor 撰写的一篇文章——“企业学习的未来：进入另一个维度”，揭示了新兴技术如何为学习体验增加价值，以及如何以正确的方式利用技术来帮助提高员工绩效。

您也可以与我联系以获取更多见解，并了解我们 Hornbill FX 一直在努力使学习更加身临其境、引人入胜并最终变得有效。 我们提供交钥匙式沉浸式电子学习解决方案，结合使用 3D 动画、2D 动画、增强现实、虚拟现实和完善的教学设计原则，创造真正引人入胜的学习体验。 最近，Hornbill FX在美国和印度被评为领先的 AR 和 VR 沉浸式学习解决方案公司。

快乐学习！



原文:

Wisdom is not a product of schooling, but the lifelong attempt to acquire it — Albert Einstein

Learning a new skill or taking a course can help you make significant advances in your career. Or maybe even just boost your confidence. For adult learners, who juggle learning new skills with the demands of adult life, the benefits of e-learning are manifold.

The HBX Learning Space aims to help professionals across all walks of life stay informed about the amazing things that are happening in the world of e-learning.

This week’s resources focus on an emerging buzzword: “Immersive Learning”. Immersive learning moves the learner away from the conscious task of knowledge assimilation and instead creates immersive environments that stimulate interest and trigger synapses to achieve outcomes in a more engaging and effective manner.

The first resource is a link to an insightful talk by Mohsin Memon, CEO of Memcorp Immersive Learning on “Limitless Growth Through Immersive Learning”. He talks about how immersive learning allows us an opportunity to live a new life through a new learning experience.

The second resource is a link to Racoon Gang. This post explains what immersive learning is and the different ways to create immersive experiences.

The final resource is an article written by Ken Taylor — “The Future of Corporate Learning: Stepping Into Another Dimension” that reveals how emerging technologies have added value to the learning experience and how leveraging technology in the right way can help improve employee performance.

You can also reach out to me for more insights and to know how we at Hornbill FX are always striving to make learning more immersive, engaging, and ultimately, effective. Hornbill FX is one of the best 3D immersive Learning development companies in India. We offer turnkey immersive eLearning solutions, using a combination of 3D animation, 2D animation, Augmented Reality, Virtual Reality, and well-honed principles of instructional design to create truly engaging learning experiences. Recently Hornbill FX ranked as leading AR and VR immersive learning solutions companies in the USA and India.

Happy Learning!
item.author: arjuntech

item.title: 关于 10K 天的注意事项 | A Note On The 10K Days
item.description:
萨拉姆和大家好！ 对我们所有人来说确实是一段漫长的旅程。 不同的我们，不同的障碍，有着相似的目标——追求幸福和我们最享受的事物。

这篇文章是我个人的记录，但非常欢迎您滚动阅读它，但这不会是一篇简短的文章。 不，这与 PowerLevel10K 无关！

如果你想分享你的旅程，你可以把链接放在下面的评论部分，我会花时间阅读它。

让我们开始吧，好吗？

第一天

圣诞节后 3 天出生在森美兰州，父母有一对医生。 没什么好说的，但我很感激这个卑微的开始。 我的故事年鉴从这里开始——

关于多动症

好吧，在童年时期，我难以入睡并且变得“过度”活跃。 然而，尽管我的父母观察到我有轻度多动症的症状，但他们并没有带我去看儿科，而是决定让我像其他孩子一样正常成长。 好吧，狂野的想象力，我们兄弟姐妹在雷声中欢呼，还有我和我的兄弟姐妹做了很多疯狂的事情。

但你知道吗？ 我也很高兴人们看到它通过它，成为我生活的历史和一部分。

教育的第一个里程碑

在芙蓉 SRI 的 5 年主要教会了我基本的教育知识，比如数数、阅读和孩子们最需要的东西。 但在这里我首先发现了我对数学的兴趣。 嘿嘿，给我一个复杂的加法问题，我可以用我的指尖计算，虽然它不像亚洲孩子那样用那些手指动作计算🤣。 但这一切都从我的数学老师开始，他从在领土军团开始就很严格。

但在第 6 年，我不得不搬到 SK Taman Tasik Jaya Seremban，所以我可以照顾我有学习障碍的妹妹，因为学校有专门的班。 不同的环境。 我设法在 UPSR 中获得了 5 分中的 4 分。

学习继续

对于中学教育，我搬到 SMKA Sheikh Hj Mohd Said 5 年，在那里我主要塑造了现在的我。 思想、哲学、态度的方式。 我在学校以“坏”书呆子的事情而闻名——在那里的 5 年里，我从未完成大部分作业。 当然，我并不为此感到骄傲，但再想想，我觉得这很有趣，因为我的老师只是决定不那么打扰我，因为无论如何我都会在考试中得分。

我在中学期间学到了很多东西。 我主要在这里学习记录，因为我参与了很多活动。 担任活动委员会成员、图书管理员秘书和空手道俱乐部主席。 我是如此活跃，以至于我设法自律并在 3 周内减掉了 15 公斤。 多么疯狂的事情！

好吧，我仍然被视为一个成绩优异的人，有望在考试中取得成绩。 经过努力和汗水，我设法在 PMR 和 SPM 中都获得了一个直分。 真是个书呆子。

我当时的野心得到了疯狂的发展。 最初，我的目标是成为一名医生（特别是骨科，这要归功于我父母的接触），后来成为一名空手道教练，然后是一名精算师，最后是一名计算机科学家。 甚至我的父母甚至曾经要求我成为一名飞行员（我也考虑过）。 但是有了这些雄心壮志，我​​确实学到了一点点，成为我想要的东西，但是情况改变了我对我想要关注的事情的看法。 然而，那时的我很天真。

现在是大学时间

在马来西亚，我们有诸如 A-Level 研究之类的东西，我们称之为基础研究。 所以，中学一毕业，我就在马来亚大学（UM）注册了伊斯兰研究和科学基金会两年。 同时，我也是代表马来亚大学的空手道运动员。 我最喜欢的课程是计算机科学，并且有一位体育讲师。

后来直接就读于伊斯兰研究和信息技术学士学位，这是一个双学位。 在我生命的那个阶段，发生了很多事情。 面对轻度抑郁症，担任 UM 周边活动的图形和动作设计师，连续 2 年空手道比赛获得银牌，我心爱的祖母去世，以及我父母的分居。

当然，我喜欢很多事情，尽管我遇到了一些挑战，比如我已经获得的成功和我有时会后悔的事情。 消除因吃得不好而引起的头痛和胃痛，是我希望我可以做得更好的事情。 这些事情教会了我什么是生活：

  • 了解最重要的事情。 当然，我们会在这里和那里反复犯错误，这只是生活学习的问题。
  • 了解你自己。 有时我们以为我们了解自己，但实际上我们并不了解。 这些障碍将突出您的身份和能力。 在我们尝试之前我们不会知道。
  • 相信自己。 我的自尊心很低，而且一直对自己很苛刻（直到现在）。 我很高兴我周围的人相信我，所以我不想让他们失望。 我现在的成功要感谢身边的人。
  • 站在你的立场上。 坚持你的原则，不要让别人妥协。 与众不同是可以的，因为这就是我们——人类。 变化是一种祝福。
  • 从你的错误中吸取教训。 学习也可以发生在教育环境之外，所以要充分利用它。
  • 渴望知识。 正如我所说，生活就是不停学习的过程，找到自己的道路。 设定目标。
  • 积极学习。 发现很多东西，你就会发现你想成为什么样的人。 曝光对你来说就是一切，无论好坏。

毕业后

我于 2018 年从 UM 毕业。老实说，我还不清楚我想在生活中做什么，因为那时我从事图形和动画设计。 我质疑自己是想走那条路，还是我认为自己最擅长的另一条路。 那是我决定要冒险进入编程世界的那一刻，因为我在编程课程中取得了最高分。 但是，我仍然对自己的技能没有信心，也没有决定要专注于哪种语言或框架。 幸运的是，我的一位讲师正在寻找一名研究助理，特别是在应用程序开发方面。 所以，我抓住机会申请了它。 那个时候最让我害怕的是，我什至不知道我想用什么来构建一个应用程序，所以我“疯了”挑战自己学习 React 2 周而不是别的，就在被任命之前研究助理1年。 我几乎没有看到自己成功完成了应用程序的开发（不要谈论代码质量，我现在查看代码也感到畏缩）。

有了这个，我对编码有了很大的信心，但我发现我喜欢开发 UI 和设计，所以我致力于成为一名前端开发人员。 因此，在结束研究助理的合同后，我寻求前端开发人员的职位。 我收到了 2 个 offer，但我决定去 SPEZA，一家加密货币交易公司，担任初级前端开发人员。

我的第一份工作

由于我的自尊心很低，我总是怀疑自己是否把事情做对了。 值得庆幸的是，我得到了 SPEZA 最好的导师之一，他安慰我并让我确信我有需要的东西。 所以，我利用他们的信任度过了难关，学到了很多东西，享受了大部分的工作，一起面对障碍。

但好吧，美好的事物总是持续的。 经过 5 个月的工作后，世界突然受到了一场臭名昭著的流行病 COVID-19 的袭击。 该公司不得不关闭他们的业务，并且该国进入了封锁（MCO）。 这对我和团队来说都很艰难，甚至给我带来了一些痛苦。 老实说，我在关闭后停止了一段时间的编码，用游戏让自己平静下来。 进入失业生活 7 个月后，我拼命努力让自己和我的技能保持相关性。 我试图重新学习全栈开发并在 MCO 之后找工作。 所以，我设法在一家电子商务平台公司找到了一份全栈开发人员的工作。 我还记得招募我的招聘人员问我是想成为全栈开发人员还是专注于特定的堆栈，例如前端堆栈。 我非常绝望，我说只要我能付得起账单，我愿意接受任何职位。

下一个挑战：金融

作为一名全栈开发人员，我必须开发前端、后端以及任何与它们相关的东西。 然而，一进公司，我就被分配到一个项目，并期望一个人管理它。 当时没有指导（所有其他团队都忙于不同的项目），我不得不从头到尾做。 我的意思是，从项目规划、需求管理、客户管理、设计、开发、测试、部署和交付——所有这些都没有任何明确的经验。 我压力很大，我什至试图为此向管理层发声，但最终，我还是坚持了下来，完成了我开始的事情。 这对我来说可能很难，但一直以来都是一个挑战。

所以，发生的事情是，公司面临资金短缺，我有 5 个月没有拿到全薪。 我以前确实有积蓄，但在那五个月里全都蒸发了。 所以，我决定找到一个退出计划。

我遇到了一些很棒的人，我没想到会认识。 一个著名的 yoyo 家伙，他也是一个著名的游牧开发者 Fajar，他最终成为了我们现在正在从事的项目Jombuat的合作伙伴。 我还遇到了其他一些人，一年后他们成为了我的同事。

下一个挑战：原则

后来在 2021 年底，我加入了一家本地支付网关公司，我的愿景是为支付解决方案创建一个新引擎。 这一点也不坏，但在某些时候，我想我已经偏离了我想要专注的东西——前端开发。 加入后仅一个月就被任命为团队负责人，我不得不监督遗留系统，集思广益新引擎，后来不得不从事不同的项目。 这种事情我以前在以前的公司也发生过，你必须是整个部门。 站在我的原则上，我选择退出，继续寻找另一个机会，特别是前端开发。

所以，像往常一样，我去了不同的职位发布（抱歉，我此时忽略了大多数招聘人员）符合我的喜好，然后有一个职位发布最让我印象深刻 - Xsolla 的中/高级 React 开发人员，一个游戏商业公司。 我很好奇，所以我决定申请并祈祷最好的。 经过 2 个面试阶段后，我设法加入了 Xsolla，在我写这篇文章的时候，我已经在这里待了 1 周，所以没什么好说的——除了要阅读的大量文档😵‍💫。 好吧，我不是书迷。

其他事情

嗯，沿途也发生了很多事情。 规划个人品牌发展，撰写有关 React 和 Javascript 的文章，结识我从中学开始就仰慕的酷人，例如 Afiq Sazlan（HAS 学院，他的书）以及 Fajar Siddiq 和 Anoush Siahkar 等其他人，获得 2 年的通过 Adob​​e Live 活动免费订阅 Adob​​e，加入不同的活动并建立新的联系，管理社区，组织有关 Github 和 React 等内容的课程。 我已经算错了我在经历了这些艰辛之后获得的祝福。 即使在困难时期，比如我的车被偷了——谢天谢地，问题很快得到了解决，得到了全面的保险，胃炎真的很严重（我发抖得发抖），我 10 岁时得了出血性登革热，以及所有这些亲爱的逝去的人，包括和我同龄的朋友和堂兄弟。

所有这些对我来说都是一个教训，并将一直持续到我的末日。 今天是我人生的第10000天，我依然坚强，还在寻找故事中的幸福。 有很多东西要学，还有很多事情要做。 因此，这就是我将在这一点上继续寻求的内容：

  • 教别人。 当我看到自己如何为社会做出贡献时，教学让我感到满足。
  • 职业成长。 在这一点上，我仍然想做一些编码并收集比我需要的更多的经验，这样我可以将很多东西传递给下一代。 同时，我想找到自己的出路。
  • 建立我的个人品牌。 我继续为新学生提供一些课程，并为开发人员提供指导，以提高他们和我自己。 等到我有自己的 Youtube 课程。
  • 改善当前的连接。 我是一个内向的人，所以我并没有真正四处走动并建立很多新的联系，但我尝试接触那些让我成为今天的人。
  • 学会领导。 老实说，我从小就试图避免所有这些领导角色，因为我害怕我会让别人失望。 现在，我需要学习所有这些东西并应对这些事情，这样我才能继续成长。
  • 探索不同的领域。 现在，我可以轻松地说我是前端开发专家（是的，CSS 触手可及），并且能够做一些后端的事情，但我会喜欢探索区块链的东西，也许还有一些新的东西在未来。
  • 照顾我的健康。 投资健康不是浪费。 以前，我的背痛很厉害，所以我买了一把符合人体工程学的椅子，然后我就舒服地工作了。 和照顾饭菜一样（我不能再吃辣的东西了😭）。
  • 改善我的财务管理。 没有拿到 5 个月的薪水真的让我的储蓄减少了，所以现在我想改善我的储蓄习惯（虽然我以前对储蓄很严格）。
  • 照顾我的心理健康。 最重要的是我需要照顾我的心理健康。 由于压倒性的压力，在我以前的公司面临很多倦怠，我需要理清事情的优先级。
  • 最后，要开心。 每个人都值得快乐。 对于身边发生的事情，我一直对自己很苛刻。 寻找幸福对我的意义是我的使命。

结论

每个人都有不同的起源、故事和高潮。 比较这些差异并不是在生活中取得成就的方式，而是回顾你做了什么以及你可以做得更好的事情。 学习的过程应该是永远的，你可能需要注意的是学习的方法——看看学习中什么重要，什么可以在你的生活中实施。 经验教训可以从别人的故事或你自己的故事中获得。

有遗憾没有错，但选择留在里面就错了。 这就是他如何将你提升到你的下一个层次——一个更伟大的你。

嗯，多长啊，嗯？ 最初，我想写一篇10,000字长的文章，但我放弃了，决定就此打住。

但是 - 对于像我这样微不足道的人，我非常高兴你读了这么多。 祝你的生活充满祝福，未来会有很多好事发生在你身上。 所以，这是一个会编码的土豆。

如果您喜欢 React、Javascript 等，也欢迎您阅读我的其他文章。

所以，直到我的文章结束，愿你平安！



原文:

Salam and hello folks! A long journey indeed for all of us. A different us, different obstacles, with a similar goal - a seek for happiness and things we enjoy most.

This article is for my personal record, but you are more than welcome to scroll and read it, but this won't be a short one. And no, it's not about PowerLevel10K!

If you want to share your journey, you can just put the link down below in the comment section, and I will take the time to read it.

Let's begin, shall we?

The First Day

Born 3 days after Christmas festivities at Negeri Sembilan, blessed with a pair of doctors as parents. Not so much to tell, but I am grateful for the humble starting. The almanac of my story starts here -

A Thing About ADHD

Well, during childhood, I was having difficulty having sleep and becoming "overly" active. However, despite my parents observing my symptoms of mild ADHD, they didn't bring me to meet paediatrics but instead decided to grow me up normally like other kids. Well, wild imaginations, us siblings cheering up during thunders, and lots of crazy things my siblings and I did.

But you know what? I only discover that I had mild ADHD in my 20s, so looking back at what happened before is something I always think of, not even bothering about my shortcoming but instead going through everything. I am also glad people see pass through it and just be a history and part of my life.

The First Milestone of Education

Sekolah Rendah Islam Seremban (SRI Seremban) is my first primary education institution, where I was first nurtured. 5 years at SRI Seremban mostly taught me basic education things like counting, reading, and things children mostly need. But here I first discovered my interest in Mathematics. Like hey, give me a complex addition question, and I can calculate with my fingertips, though it's not like the Asian kids that calculate with those finger movements 🤣. But it all begins with my Mathematics teacher, who is strict since he was in Territorial Army Regiment.

But in the 6th year, I had to move to SK Taman Tasik Jaya Seremban, so I could look over my younger sister who has a learning disorder since the school has a special class for her. Different environments. I managed to get 4 out of 5 As in UPSR.

The Learning Continues

For secondary education, I moved to SMKA Sheikh Hj Mohd Said for 5 years, where I was mostly shaped into who I am now. The way of thoughts, philosophy, the attitudes. I was famous in school for "bad" nerd things - I've never completed most of my homework throughout 5 years there. Of course, I am not proud of it, but thinking that again, I found it funny, because my teachers just decide to not bother me that much, for I will score on the exam either way.

I learn a lot of things during my secondary years. I learn to document mostly here because I involved myself in a lot of events. Being the committee member of events, secretary of librarian, and president of the Karate club. I was so active, that I managed to discipline myself and reduce 15kg in 3 weeks. What a crazy thing!

Well, I am still looked up to as a high achiever, expected to score in exams. With struggles and sweats, I managed to score a straight one for both PMR and SPM. What a nerd guy.

My ambition at that time got a wild ride. Initially, I aimed to be a doctor (specifically orthopaedics, thanks to my parents' exposure), and later be a Karate instructor, then an actuarist, and finally a computer scientist. Even my parents even asked me once to be a pilot (and I considered it as well). But having these ambitions, I did learn a little bit to be what I wish for, but the situation changes my mind about things I want to focus on. Yet, I was naive at that time.

It's Varsity Time

In Malaysia, we have things like A-Level studies we called foundational studies. And so, right after secondary school, I enrol the Foundation of Islamic Studies and Science at the University of Malaya (UM) for 2 years. At the same time, I was also a Karate athlete representing the University of Malaya. My favourite course is computer science, and having a sporting lecturer.

And later straight away enrolled on Bachelor of Islamic Studies and Information Technology, a double major degree. During that phase of my life, a lot of things happened. Facing mild depression, being a graphic and motion designer for events around UM, getting silver for Karate competition for 2 years streak, the passing of my beloved grandmother, and my parents' separation.

Of course, I love many things despite having challenges such as the success that I've gained and things that I regret at times. Dismissing headaches and stomach pain from not eating well, are things that I wish that I could've done better. These are things that taught me what life is:

  • Learn what matters most. Of course, we will repeatedly be making mistakes here and there, it is just a matter of life learning.
  • Learn about yourself. Sometimes we thought we know about ourselves, but we are not. These obstacles will highlight what you are and what you are capable of. We won't know until we try.
  • Trust yourself. I have low self-esteem and have always been hard on myself (even until this moment). I am glad that people around me believe in me, so I don't want to let them down. The success that I have right now is thanks to the people around me.
  • Stand on your ground. Be firm on your principles, and don't let others compromise those. It's okay to be different, for that is what we are - human. Variation is a blessing.
  • Learn from your mistake. Learning can also happen outside the education environment, so grab the most out of it.
  • Hunger for knowledge. As I said, life is the non-stop process of learning, find your path. Set the objective.
  • Be active in learning. Discover a lot of things, and you will discover what you want to be. Exposure is everything for you, good or bad.

Post-Graduation

I graduated from UM in 2018. Honestly, it was still unclear to me what I wanted to do in life, given that time I was into graphic and motion design. I question myself whether I want to pursue that path, or maybe another path that I think I am best at. That was the moment that I decide that I want to venture into the programming world since I scored on top in the programming course. However, I still don't have confidence in my skill or decided what language or framework I want to focus on. Luckily, one of my lecturers seeks a research assistant, specifically on app development. So, I took the opportunity and apply for it. What scares me the most at that time is that, I don't even know what I want to build an app with, so I "out of my mind" challenge myself to learn React for 2 weeks and nothing else, right before being appointed as a research assistant for 1 year. Little did I see myself that I managed to complete the development of the app (don't talk about the code quality, I was also cringed looking into the code now).

With this, I gained a lot of confidence in coding, but I found out that I love to develop the UI and designs, so I commit myself to being a front-end developer. So, after ending my contract as a research assistant, I seek a frontend developer position. I got 2 offers, but I decided to go to SPEZA, a cryptocurrency exchange company as a junior frontend developer.

My First Job

Given that I have low self-esteem, I always doubt myself whether I managed to do things right. Thankfully, I got one of the best mentors I had in SPEZA, who comfort me and keep me assured that I have what is needed. And so, I used their trust to make it through and learn a lot of things, enjoy most of the job, facing obstacles together.

But well, good things always last. After 5 months of working, suddenly the world is hit by an infamous pandemic, the COVID-19. The company had to close down their operation, and the country went on lockdown (MCO). It was tough for me and the team, and even cause a bit of distress for me. Honestly, I stopped coding for a while after closure, calming myself with games. 7 months into jobless life, and at the end of that, I desperately try to keep myself and my skills relevant. I tried to relearn full-stack development and seek a job after the MCO. So, I managed to get a job at an e-commerce platform company as a full stack developer. I still remember that the recruiter who recruited me asked me whether I wanted to go for full stack developer or focused on a specific stack such as front end stack. I was so desperate, that I said that I willingly accept any position as long I can pay the bill.

The Next Challenge: The Finance

As a full-stack developer, I had to develop frontend, back end and anything that revolved around them. However, the moment I entered the company, I was assigned to a project and expected to single-handedly manage it. With no guidance at that time (all other teams were occupied with different projects), I had to do it from end to end. And I mean, from project planning, requirement management, managing clients, designing, developing, testing, deploying and delivering it - all without any clear experience. I stressed out, and I even tried to voice out to the management for that, but in the end, I managed to hold out and finished what I started. This might be tough for me, but it was a challenge all along.

So, what happened is that, the company faced a financial shortage, and I didn't get full pay for 5 months. I did have my savings before, but all evaporates for those 5 months. So, I decided to find an exit plan.

In November 2020, I decided to join a local event, JomLaunch, where the developers gather to see the local digital product, the pitches and have some discussions. I met some awesome guys, which I didn't expect to get to know. A famous yoyo guy, who is also a famous nomad developer, Fajar, who eventually be a partner of the project that we worked on right now, Jombuat. I also met some other guys, who then become my colleague a year after.

The Next Challenge: The Principle

Later at the end of 2021, I joined a local payment gateway company, with a vision of me creating a new engine for the payment solution. It is not bad at all, but at some point, I thought a while back that I am strayed away from what I wanted to focus on - the front end development. Being appointed as team lead just a month after joining, I had to oversee the legacy system, brainstorm the new engine, and later on had to work on different projects. This kind of thing happens to me before in the previous company, where you have to be the whole department. Standing on my principle, I choose to just quit and continue to seek another opportunity, specifically in front end development.

So, as usual, I went to different job postings (sorry I ignored most of the recruiters at this point) that match my preferences, and then there is one job posting that strikes me the most - Mid/Senior React Developer at Xsolla, a gaming commerce company. I am so curious, so I decided to apply and pray for the best. After 2 interview phases, I managed to join Xsolla, and at the time I am writing, I am already 1 week here, so not so much to talk about - except for the humongous documentation to read 😵‍💫. Well, I'm not bibliophilic.

Other Things

Well, a lot of things happened as well along the path. Planning for the personal brand growth, writing articles on React and Javascript, meeting cool guys that I looked up to since secondary school such as Afiq Sazlan (HAS Academy, his books) and others such as Fajar Siddiq and Anoush Siahkar, getting 2 years of free Adobe subscription thanks to Adobe Live events, joining different events and create new connections, managing communities, organizing classes on kinds of stuff like Github and React. I have already miscounted the blessing that I've gained after all those hardships that I faced. Even during the hard times, like my car was stolen - thankfully the issue was resolved quickly and get full insurance coverage, a really bad gastritis attack (I was shivering like hell), got haemorrhage dengue when I was 10 years old, and all those beloved people that passed away, including my friends and cousins who are at the same age as me.

All of these serve as a lesson for me and will continue to be until my ending days. Today is the 10,000th day of my life, and I am still strong, and still seeking the happiness in my story. A lot to learn and a lot to do. So here's what I will continue to seek at this point:

  • Teaching others. Teaching gives me satisfaction when I can see how I can contribute to society.
  • Career growth. At this point, I still want to do some coding and gather more experiences than I need, so I can pass on a lot of things to the next generation. At the same time, I want to find my way up.
  • Establishing my personal brand. I continue to offer some courses to the new students and coaching to developers to improve both them and myself. Wait till I have my own Youtube courses.
  • Improving current connections. I am an introvert, so I didn't really go around and make lots of new connections, but I try to reach out to those who make me who I am today.
  • Learn to lead. Honestly, I tried to avoid all those leadership roles since childhood, because I was afraid that I would let others down. Now, I need to learn all those things and cope with things so I can continue to grow.
  • Explore different domains. Right now, I can comfortably say that I am an expert in frontend development (yeah, CSS is just at my fingertips), and am able to do some backend things, but I will love to explore blockchain things and maybe some new things in the future.
  • Taking care of my health. Investing in wellness is not a waste. Previously, I have bad back pain, so I buy an ergonomic chair, and I worked comfortably after that. Same as taking care of the meal (I cannot eat spicy things anymore 😭).
  • Improve my finance management. Not getting a 5-month salary really left a dent in my saving, so now I want to improve my saving habit (though I was strict on saving before).
  • Taking care of my mental health. It matters the most that I need to take care of my mental health. Faced a lot of burnout in my previous company for the overwhelming pressure, I need to sort things out and the priorities.
  • And finally, be happy. Everyone deserves to be happy. I have been harsh towards myself for things happening around me. Seeking the meaning of happiness to me is my mission.

Conclusion

Everyone has a different set of origin, the story and the climax. To compare those differences is not the way to achieve things in life, but to retrospect what you have done and what you could have done better. The learning process should be forever, and the thing that you might need to pay attention to is the method of learning - to see what matters in learning, and what can be implemented in your life. Lessons can be gained either from others' stories or your own.

It's not wrong to have regrets, but it is wrong if you choose to stay in it. This is how He elevated you to the next level of you - a greater you.

Well, what a long one, huh? Initially, I wish to write a 10,000-word long, but I give up, and decided to stop here.

But - I am extremely glad that you read this far, for someone as insignificant as me. I wish your life will be full of blessing, and a lot of good things will happen to you in the future. So, here is a potato who codes.

You are also welcomed to read my other articles as well, if you are into React, Javascript and stuff.

So, till the end of my article, peace be upon ya!
item.author: alserembani

item.title: 如何在 WeTest 上使用 Appium 高效测试 iOS 应用 | How to Efficiently Test iOS Apps with Appium on WeTest
item.description:
为了在开发和测试期间确保我们的 iOS 应用程序的质量，我们决定使用 Jenkins 作业在 WeTest 云设备上对我们的应用程序的每次更新进行测试。 在研究了各种工具之后，我们使用gtest + gmock作为 SDK 的单元测试框架和Appium作为 UI 测试框架。

我们在下面描述 Appium 解决方案，包括 Appium 机制、在本地设置 Appium 的步骤以及将我们的测试集成到 Jenkins 作业中。 在本地设置 Appium 只是为了运行和检查测试用例，而带有WeTest 自动化测试插件的 Jenkins 作业被触发以在整个开发生命周期中对 WeTest 云移动设备上的每个版本进行自动化测试。

一、Appium机制

这是典型的客户端-服务器架构。 此应用接受 HTTP 命令，对其进行解析并调用底层 XCTest.framework 接口来控制被测应用。

自动化脚本是用 Python 或 Java 编写的。 操作系统：MacOS

开发环境：XCode、命令行工具、python、pip、HomeBrew、node、npm、libimobiledevice 工具

安装所有开发环境依赖项。

  • 命令行工具可以在这里下载
  • 家酿
ruby -e "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/install )"
 brew install node node -v # verify node version npm -v # verify npm version
  • 我们使用后一种
#npm config set proxy "http://<proxyip>:<proxyport>" # in case proxy is required npm install appium@latest -g npm install appium-doctor -g appium -v # verify appium version
 brew install libimobiledevice

到目前为止，所有依赖项都已安装。 我们可以运行 appium-doctor --ios 来验证 Appium 的安装。 只需要“必要的依赖”，所以我们目前忽略可选的依赖。

我们可以在以下路径中启动 WebDriverAgent.xcodeproj：

然后，我们将 WebDriverAgentLib 和 WebDriverAgentRunner 更改为“自动管理签名”，如下图所示

在“团队”选择框中选择开发者团队名称。 在“团队”选择框中选择开发者团队名称。

选择目标作为 WebDriverAgentRunner 并选择目标设备。 然后，从 Xcode 菜单项“产品/测试”启动它

您可以从菜单项“查看/调试区域/激活控制台”中启用它

当显示 ServerURLHere 时，WebDriverAgentRunner 已成功启动并等待通过 HTTP 的命令。

然后，停止正在运行的 WebDriverAgent 项目并完全关闭 XCode。 启动 Appium - 只需在终端中运行“appium”。

 Run a simple test script # -*- coding: UTF-8 -*- import unittest import xmlrunner as xmlrunner from appium import webdriver class SimpleAppiumTest(unittest.TestCase): def setUp(self): print('handle driver set up .') desired_caps = { 'platformName': 'iOS', 'automationName': 'XCUITest', 'deviceName': 'iOS', 'newCommandTimeout': 600, 'bundleId': 'com.apple.mobiletimer', 'udid': 'fd3b34c2a7ed5a724168d3af98f694315f3e8611', # remove this line when running on WeTest 'webviewConnectTimeout': 10000, # Following is required when running on WeTest # 'udid': os.getenv("IOS_SERIAL"), # 'webDriverAgentUrl': "http://%s:%s/" % (os.getenv("WDA_SERVER_IP"), os.getenv("WDA_SERVER_PORT")), } self.driver = webdriver.Remote("http://localhost:4723/wd/hub", desired_caps) def tearDown(self): print('tear down') self.driver.quit() def test_open_alarm(self): print('Clicking Alarm tab') self.driver.launch_app() self.driver.find_element_by_name("Alarm").click() if __name__ == '__main__': unittest.main(testRunner=xmlrunner.XMLTestRunner(output="output")) # output=os.getenv("UPLOADDIR") is required when running on WeTest

按照此处的步骤安装和配置 WeTest Jenkins 插件。

准备 Appium 自动化测试脚本
我们按照示例脚本通过运行create-zip.sh创建我们的测试脚本 zip 包。 然后，我们将 zip 文件重命名为“ArchiveTestAppiumDemo1.zip”，如下面的屏幕截图所示。

请注意，生成 TEST-ALL.xml 结果文件需要在 runTest.sh 中有一行“mv $UPLOADDIR/TEST-*.xml $UPLOADDIR/TEST-ALL.xml”，WeTest 利用它来生成报告分析。

向 WeTest 提交测试任务
我们配置 Build 部分并将 ipa 文件路径和脚本 zip 文件路径设置为插件设置。 路径必须是绝对路径。 可以手动配置设备组。 测试将在所选设备组中的这些设备中并行运行。

单击作业的“立即构建”，然后从控制台输出查看结果报告 URL

查看报告
一个视图中的所有测试分析。 转到测试分析并获取测试报告。

导出 Appium 脚本输出
或者，从 REST API“ 获取测试中的设备的结果”中检索它

我们尝试调用（替换：使用从帐户设置页面获取的密钥 id 和密钥）

然后它将返回带有“script_log_url”的 JSON 结果。 下载并解压，Appium 脚本输出（文件名：runTest.log）以及其他日志都驻留在其中。

概括
我们可以将它与其他 CI/CD 系统集成。 由于 WeTest 易于并行运行测试，我们可以在整个开发生命周期中使用基于云的数字质量平台提高质量和速度。

访问WeTest 自动化并立即开始您的 30 天免费试用。

莎拉·莱恩🏴󠁧󠁢 - May 12 5月12日

Ayushi Chaudhuri - Ayushi Chaudhuri - May 12 5月12日



原文:

To ensure the quality of our iOS Apps during development and testing, we decide to use Jenkins jobs to test it on WeTest cloud devices for every update of our app. After investigating various tools, we use gtest + gmock as the unit test framework and Appium as the UI test framework for the SDK.

We describe the Appium solution below, including Appium mechanism, steps to setup Appium locally and integrating our tests into Jenkins jobs. Setting up Appium locally is just for running and checking test cases, while the Jenkins job with the WeTest Automated Test Plugin are triggered to start automation test on WeTest cloud mobile devices for each release during the whole development lifecycle.

1. Appium Mechanism

This is a typical Client-Server architecture. XCode or xcodebuild command installs and launches WebDriverAgentRunner app in the iOS device. This app accepts HTTP command, parse it and call underlying XCTest.framework interfaces to control the app under test.

Automation script was written in Python or Java. WebDriverAgentRunner is the command server accepting HTTP commands and the target app of WebDriverAgent that is an open-source iOS automation tool originally developed by Facebook and now maintained by Appium. Appium server is a bridge accepts automation script requests and then call related WebDriverAgent HTTP commands.

XCTest.framework - It is an Apple official iOS framework provides interfaces to interact with app UI.

2. Setting up Local Appium Test Environment
OS: MacOS

Dev Env: XCode, Command Line Tools, python, pip, HomeBrew, node , npm, libimobiledevice tools

Install all dev env dependencies.

  • XCode can be installed via Mac AppStore or downloaded here
  • Command Line Tools can be downloaded here
  • Homebrew 
ruby -e "$(curl -fsSL 

            https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/install

        )"

brew install node
node -v  # verify node version
npm -v   # verify npm version
  • Appium can be installed via Appium Desktop ( releases page ) or via npm. We use the latter one 
#npm config set proxy "http://<proxyip>:<proxyport>"  # in case proxy is required
npm install appium@latest -g
npm install appium-doctor -g
appium -v # verify appium version

brew install libimobiledevice

So far, all dependencies have been installed. We can run appium-doctor --ios to verify the Appium installation. Appium doctor would check multiple dependencies. Only the "necessary dependencies" are needed, so we ignore the optional dependencies currently.

3. Debugging on the Device
WebDriverAgentRunner is a controlling app running in the iOS device. Appium have already integrated it into its installation folder. We can launch WebDriverAgent.xcodeproj in the following path:

/usr/local/lib/node_modules/appium/node_modules/appium-webdriveragent/WebDriverAgent.xcodeproj

Then, we change WebDriverAgentLib and WebDriverAgentRunner to "Automatically manage signing" as the screenshot below

1) Launch WebDriverAgent.xcodeproj project in XCode 2) Click WebDriverAgent project name as shown in the upper left rectangle of the following screenshot 3) Click the "Signing & Capabilities" tab 4) Switch to target WebDriverAgentLib and click "Automatically manage signing". Choose a developer team name in the "Team" select box. 5) Switch to target WebDriverAgentRunner and click "Automatically manage signing". Choose a developer team name in the "Team" select box.

Select the Target as WebDriverAgentRunner and Select a target device. Then, launch it from Xcode menu item "Product / Test"

Xcode Console will print messages when running. You might enable it from menu item "View / Debug Area / Activate Console"

When ServerURLHere is shown, the WebDriverAgentRunner is succesfully launched and waiting for commands over HTTP.

Then, stop the running WebDriverAgent project and close XCode completely. Appium will internally handle to launch the WebDriverAgentRunner automatically.

Start Appium - Just run 'appium' in the Terminal.

Run a simple test script

# -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-
import unittest

import xmlrunner as xmlrunner
from appium import webdriver


class SimpleAppiumTest(unittest.TestCase):

    def setUp(self):
        print('handle driver set up .')
        desired_caps = {
            'platformName': 'iOS',
            'automationName': 'XCUITest',
            'deviceName': 'iOS',
            'newCommandTimeout': 600,
            'bundleId': 'com.apple.mobiletimer',
            'udid': 'fd3b34c2a7ed5a724168d3af98f694315f3e8611',  # remove this line when running on WeTest
            'webviewConnectTimeout': 10000,
            # Following is required when running on WeTest
            # 'udid': os.getenv("IOS_SERIAL"),
            # 'webDriverAgentUrl': "http://%s:%s/" % (os.getenv("WDA_SERVER_IP"), os.getenv("WDA_SERVER_PORT")),
        }
        self.driver = webdriver.Remote("http://localhost:4723/wd/hub", desired_caps)

    def tearDown(self):
        print('tear down')
        self.driver.quit()

    def test_open_alarm(self):
        print('Clicking Alarm tab')
        self.driver.launch_app()
        self.driver.find_element_by_name("Alarm").click()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main(testRunner=xmlrunner.XMLTestRunner(output="output"))
    # output=os.getenv("UPLOADDIR") is required when running on WeTest

4. Jenkins - Submitting Test Tasks to WeTest Device Cloud
WeTest Automated Test Plugin
Follow the steps here to install and configure the WeTest Jenkins plugin.

Prepare the Appium automated test scrip
WeTest provides an sample script in the Github repository

We follow the sample script to create our test script zip package by running the create-zip.sh . Then, we renamed the zip file to "ArchiveTestAppiumDemo1.zip" as shown in screenshot below.

Please note that there is a line "mv $UPLOADDIR/TEST-*.xml $UPLOADDIR/TEST-ALL.xml" in the runTest.sh is needed for generating the TEST-ALL.xml result file, which WeTest leveraging it to generate the Report Analysis.

Submit test tasks to WeTest
We configure the Build section and set ipa file path and script zip file path to Plugin settings. The paths must be absolute paths. Device groups can be manually configured. Tests would run in parallel in those devices in the selected device group.

Click "Build Now" of the job and and view result report URL from the Console Output

5. Retrieving the Test Result and Analysis
View Reports
All test analysis in one view. Go to Test Analysis and get the test report.

Exporting Appium Script Output
WeTest provides either download script log from the page. Or, retrieve it from the REST API " Get Results of Devices in Test "

We try to call (replace : with the secret id and secret key got from the Account Settings page)

Then it would return the JSON result with "script_log_url". "script_log_url" is a link to the logs zip file. Download it and unzip it, the Appium script output (filename: runTest.log) along with other logs reside in it.

Summary
WeTest provides maximum mobile device coverage and an easy way to automate Appium test using powerful REST APIs and Jenkins plugin. We can integrate it with other CI/CD system. Due to ease of running tests in parallel on WeTest, we can increase quality and velocity with the cloud-based digital quality platform in the whole development lifecycle.

Visit WeTest Automation and start your 30-day free trial today.

Sarah Lean 🏴󠁧󠁢 - May 12

Ayushi Chaudhuri - May 12

item.title: GitHub 历史 | GitHub history
item.description:
我在 2010 年 8 月创建了我的 GitHub 帐户，让我们看看我用它做了什么。

然后在 12 月，我提交了其他一些 php 站点。

在 2010 年 12 月 12 日到 2011 年 9 月 29 日之间，我一定创建了一些自动化流程，这就是为什么每天都有提交。 查看差异，它似乎与跟踪网站的访问者有关。 我想那时我还没有听说过 .gitignore 文件！

几年前什么都没有，直到 2014 年，当我开始编译代码示例集合时，这是我想炫耀的一些代码，我从 2015 年开始添加到这里，所以我真的应该回到这个，因为我的技能已经发展了从那以后有点。

我结束了参加 Advent of Code 的这一年

我去年做了什么？ 好吧，我开始去 York Code Dojo，这意味着他们的代码示例中有很多分支，也是我的第一个拉取请求。 我还查看了其他存储库，试图找到一个好的开源项目来贡献，但还没有找到适合我的。 最后，我以一点代码的出现结束了 2016 年。

不知道这篇博文有多吸引人，但看到不同的提交历史很有趣。



原文:

I created my GitHub account in August 2010, lets look at what I have done with it.

In 2010 I started by committing the code for an old php website I had created. Then in December I committed some other php sites.

Between 12 December 2010 and 29 September 2011 I must have created some automated process which is why there is a commit every day. Looking at the diff it appears to be related to tracking visitors to a site. I assume back then I hadn’t heard of the .gitignore file!

Nothing for a few years until 2014, when I started compiling a code samples collection, this is bits of code that I want to show off, I have added to this since 2015 so I really should go back to this as my skills have developed a bit since then.

In 2015 I really started to think of myself as a developer, I added repositories for Raspberry Pi, and a few C# ideas I had. I ended the year taking part in Advent of Code

What did I do last year? Well I started going to York Code Dojo and this meant lots of forks from their code examples and also my first pull request. I also did a fair bit of looking at other repositories trying to find a good open source project to contribute to, still not found one for me yet. Lastly I ended 2016 with a bit of Advent of Code.

Not sure how enthralling a blog post this is, but fascinating to see the different commit histories.

item.title: 为移动设备优化网站时要避免的 9 个错误 | 9 Mistakes To Avoid While Optimizing Your Site For Mobile Devices
item.description:
在当前的数字化时代，人们通过移动优先的方法推动数字发现。 牢记这一点，来自大企业和小企业的 Web 开发人员都会注意优化网站，使其尽可能适合移动设备。 对网站进行优化以使其在各种移动设备中正确呈现是很重要的。 即使您正在创建一个新网站，与其为移动用户开发一个单独的网站，不如创建一个适合各种设备的响应式网站。 在本文中，我们将讨论在针对移动设备优化您的网站时应避免的错误。

使用单一网站

通常，网站所有者倾向于为移动设备设计一个单独的网站，而不是优化他们现有的网站。 然而，这给用户和所有者都造成了混淆。 使用可在桌面和移动设备中正确呈现的动态布局重新设计现有网站是理想的选择。 毕竟，与拥有多个 URL 相比，具有统一 URL 的网站更容易维护且维护成本更低。

嘿，您现在可以使用Directus 测试在 3000 多种不同的桌面和移动浏览器环境中在最可靠和可扩展的 CMS 测试云上测试 Test Directus 网站。

确保网站是可扩展的

对于响应式网站而言，可扩展性是一项重要功能。 在设计时，您必须记住，手机的屏幕比台式机小。 您需要在不同的移动设备上检查网站的移动视图，并确保响应式网页设计。 元素的排列方式应使用户在浏览网站时不会遇到任何问题。

使用这款面向开发人员的新浏览器，您可以通过测试内容的位置和对齐方式来简化用户界面和用户体验的开发，评估不同屏幕分辨率下视觉上繁重的网站，并生成网站的性能报告。

超链接和按钮的大小

在设计网站时，您应该记住，在桌面上，用户使用鼠标指针单击按钮和链接，而在移动设备中，他们使用拇指或食指，后者要粗得多。 如果链接和按钮的大小保持较小，用户将很难点击它们进行导航或其他目的。 保留大尺寸按钮并进行适当的可用性测试，以确保所有超链接和按钮都可以在移动屏幕上轻松点击。

测试移动友好性

谷歌开发了一种工具来测试您的网站是否适合移动设备。 要检查，您需要访问https://search.google.com/test/mobile-friendly并输入您网站的 URL 并点击测试按钮。 该工具会自动检查您的网站是否满足所有要求的准则。 它检查所有链接和按钮是否可点击、文本是否可读以及内容是否可调整，用户无需放大即可阅读。 您需要进行的更改会在测试执行后计算出来并显示出来。

查看Docusaurus 测试— 一种随您扩展而扩展的自动化测试云。 在最可靠的测试自动化云上的 Docusaurus 网站上大规模运行自动化测试。

不要让它单调

普通用户大约需要半分钟来决定该站点是否值得探索。 为了提高您的网站在 Google 中的排名，请添加丰富的片段、动态和独特的内容、图像以及动画和视频等其他媒体元素。 使您的网站与众不同将有助于提高其在 Google 中的排名并吸引更多受众。

媒体元素应该是可播放的

通常，已观察到嵌入网站内容的视频或 GIF 无法在移动设备上播放。 移动设备不支持需要 Flash 播放器或任何其他许可的内容。 不可播放的媒体元素会让最终用户感到恼火，因此，他们在切换到另一个网站之前不会三思而后行。 一旦您的网站开发完成，请在真实设备上对其进行测试，以确保所有媒体元素都正常工作。

避免冗长的表格

网站通常包含冗长的注册表单。 这些在桌面上更容易填写，但在移动设备上，用户需要滚动很多，在这样做时，他们可能会感到困惑或迷失方向。 当出现问题时，挫败感会增加，他们必须再次向上滚动以纠正它。 避免这些繁琐的工作，并提供不需要用户提供太多信息的简单表单。 让他们的工作更轻松将提高您网站的移动友好性。

您是否检查过Drupal 测试并使用 LambdaTest 在线设备云测试您的 Drupal 网页的响应能力以及与移动设备的兼容性。

跨浏览器兼容性

根据 GS.statcounter 公布的上述统计数据，Chrome、Safari、UC Browser、Samsung Internet 和 Opera 是最常用的移动浏览器。 您必须确保您网站的所有功能在这些浏览器中都能正常运行，并且没有损坏或呈现问题。 使用 Lambdatest 等基于云的测试平台测试您的网站在所有主要浏览器上无缝运行，以确保您的网站跨浏览器兼容。

从用户那里获取反馈

在您的网站上包含一个反馈部分，用户可以在其中输入他们关于在移动设备上进一步优化您的网站以及是否需要改进的建议。 专注于每一个反馈，无论问题多么小，立即解决。 可用性测试是网站成功的关键因素。 因此，请始终注意最终用户提供的建议。

除此之外，请确保您的网站有一个带有轮播和 CTA 的炫酷登录页面，让用户有足够的好奇心去探索里面的内容。 这都是我们这边的。 享受开发您的网站并确保没有这样的重大错误，您很快就会注意到您网站的互联网流量显着增长。



原文:

In the current age of digitalization, people are driven towards digital discovery through a mobile-first approach. Keeping that in mind, web developers from big enterprises as well as small businesses, make a note of optimizing a website as mobile-friendly as possible. It is important for a website to be optimized in such a manner that it gets rendered properly in various mobile devices. Even if you are creating a new website, instead of developing a separate one for mobile users it is ideal to create a responsive website that fits properly in all kinds of devices. In this article, we shall discuss the mistakes to avoid while optimizing your website for mobile devices.

Use a Single Website

Often, site-owners tend to design a separate website for mobile devices instead of optimizing their existing website. This, however, creates confusion for both users as well as the owner. It is ideal to redesign your existing website using a dynamic layout that is rendered properly in both desktop and mobile devices. After all, a website with a uniform URL is easier and less expensive to maintain than having multiple.

Hey, you can now test Test Directus websites on the most reliable and scalable CMS testing cloud across 3000+ different desktop and mobile browsers environments using Directus Testing.

Make Sure the Site is Scalable

Scalability is an important feature when it comes to a responsive website. While designing, you have to keep in mind that the screen of the mobile is smaller than a desktop. You need to check mobile view of website on different mobile devices and ensure the responsive web design. The elements should be arranged in a way that the user does not face any problem while exploring the site.

LT Browser is a mobile website testing tool designed to help you reliably test your website on different mobile resolutions. Using this new browser for developers, you can streamline user-interface and user-experience development by testing the placement of content and alignments, assess visually heavy websites on different screen resolutions, and generate your websites’ performance reports.

Size of Hyperlinks and Buttons

While designing the website, you should keep in mind that in desktop, the user uses the mouse pointer to click on buttons and links while in mobile they use their thumb or index finger which is much thicker. If the size of links and buttons are kept small, it will become difficult for the user to click on them for navigation or other purposes. Keep large-sized buttons and do proper usability testing to make sure that all the hyperlinks and buttons are easily clickable on the mobile screen.

Testing mobile friendliness

Google has developed a tool to test whether your website is mobile friendly or not. To check, you need to visit https://search.google.com/test/mobile-friendly and enter your site’s URL and hit the test button. The tool automatically checks whether your website satisfies all the guidelines required. It checks whether all the links and buttons are clickable, a text is readable and contents are adjustable in a way that users don’t have to zoom in to read. The changes you need to make are figured out and displayed after the test is executed.

Check out this Docusaurus Testing — An automation testing cloud that scales as you scale. Run automation tests at scale on your Docusaurus website on the most reliable test automation cloud.

Don’t Make it Monotonous

An average user takes about half a minute to decide whether the site is worth exploring or not. For increasing your site’s ranking in Google, add rich snippets, dynamic and unique content, images along with other media elements like animations and video. Making your site unique will help to increase it’s ranking in Google and attract more audience.

Media Elements Should be Playable

Often, it has been observed that videos or GIFs embedded in the content of a website are not playable on mobile devices. Contents that require Flash player or any other license are not supported on mobile. Media elements which are not playable are irritating for an end-user, as a result, they don’t think twice before switching on to another website. Once your website is developed, test it on real devices to make sure that all the media elements are working properly.

Avoid Lengthy Forms

Websites often contain lengthy registration forms. Those are easier to fill on a desktop but on mobile, a user needs to scroll a lot and while doing so, they may get confused or lose track. The frustration increases when something goes amiss and they have to scroll up again to correct it. Avoid these tedious jobs and provide easy forms that do not require that much information from the user. Making their job easier will increase your website’s mobile-friendliness.

Did you check Drupal Testing and Test your Drupal webpages for responsiveness and and compatibility with mobile devices with LambdaTest online device cloud.

Cross Browser Compatibility

As per the above-stated statistics published by GS.statcounter, Chrome, Safari, UC Browser, Samsung Internet and Opera are the most used mobile browsers. You must ensure that all the features of your website work properly in those browsers and there are no breakage or rendering issues. Test your website runs seamlessly across all major browsers using cloud based testing platforms like Lambdatest to ensure that your site is cross browser compatible.

Take Feedback from Users

Include a Feedback section on your website where users can enter their suggestions regarding further optimization of your site on the mobile device and whether there is any need for improvement. Concentrate on each and every feedback and no matter how minor the issue is, fix it immediately. Usability testing is a key factor behind a website’s success. So, always pay heed to the suggestions provided by end users.

Apart from these, make sure that your site has a cool landing page with carousels and CTAs that makes a user curious enough to explore what is inside. That’s all from our side. Enjoy developing your site and make sure no such major mistake and you will soon notice a considerable growth in your site’s internet traffic.
