item.title: 网站开发和 Web 应用程序开发有何不同？ | How Do Website Development and Web Application Development Differ?
item.description:
当企业想要建立在线形象时，问题就出现了我们应该开发网站还是网络应用程序。

别担心，我们会让您了解网站和Web 应用程序开发之间的区别。 我们将讨论一些使它们与众不同的重要事情。

所以不要浪费你的时间，让我们开始这篇文章吧！

什么是网站？

网站是网页和相关内容的集合，它们共享一个共同的域名并托管在至少一个 Web 服务器上。 谷歌、Facebook、亚马逊和维基百科是一些知名网站的例子。

网站有什么好处？

其中最重要的原因是：

以有效的方式展示您的产品或服务。 制作你的社交证明。 为您的商品和服务打造品牌。 实现业务目标 提升客户服务 展示作品的平台 优秀的营销渠道

一个好网站的特点是什么？

以下是一个好的网站的一些最重要的特征：

高质量和相关性的内容 用户友好且响应迅速的网页设计 简单的导航 与包括智能手机、平板电脑等在内的各种设备兼容。 快速加载时间 谷歌、必应等搜索引擎可以轻松找到它。 收集线索的表格 SSL证书

什么是 Web 应用程序？

由于这些因素，Web 应用程序变得越来越流行。

它们不需要安装，因为它们在 Web 浏览器中运行。 所有现代浏览器都支持它。 可以在不通知用户他们需要更新应用程序的情况下发布新的更新。 它们易于维护，因为它们在整个应用程序中使用相同的代码。 不存在兼容性问题。 备份既安全又简单。 比移动应用程序开发更具成本效益。

什么特性造就了一个好的 Web 应用程序？

Web 应用程序的主要特征包括：

用户界面响应迅速。 适用于各种屏幕尺寸和像素密度云托管 API 必须是跨平台兼容的，这意味着它们必须在 Linux、macOS 和 Windows 上运行。 必须支持 A/B 测试和分析。 遵循最佳安全实践。 易于实现 对逻辑流、流程流、调试日志等的工具支持非常广泛。

结论：

因此，在本文中，我们区分了Web 开发和 Web 应用程序。 如果您经营家族企业、新创业公司或小型组织，您需要担心的只是您的品牌和用于营销和潜在客户开发的网站。 大多数业务需求都可以通过基本的定制网站设计来处理。 如果您的企业想要随时与客户交流，您将需要一个包含聊天、社交媒体、API 集成和自定义 API 开发的 Web 应用程序。﻿﻿﻿

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— May 12 5月12日



原文:

When businesses want to make their online presence, The issue arises of whether we should develop a Website or a web application.

Don’t worry, we’ll make you understand the difference between a website and a Web App Development. We’ll talk about some important things that make them different.

So without wasting your time let’s get this article started!

What is a Website?

A website is a collection of web pages and related content that share a common domain name and are hosted on at least one web server. Google, Facebook, Amazon, and Wikipedia are a few examples of well-known websites.

What Are the Benefits Of a Website?

Among the most important reasons are: ﻿

Showcase your products or services in an effective manner. Make your social proof. Developing a brand for your goods and services. Achieve business objectives Improve customer service A platform for displaying your work Excellent marketing channel

What are the Characteristics of a Good Website?

The following are some of the most important characteristics of a good website: ﻿

Content of high quality and relevance Web design that is both user-friendly and responsive Simple navigation Compatible with a wide range of devices including smartphones, tablets, and so on. Fast loading time Search engines such as Google, Bing, and others can easily find it. Forms for collecting leads The SSL certificate

What is a Web Application?

A web application is application software that runs on a web browser, as opposed to software programmes that run domestically and naturally on the device's operating system. Web applications are provided to clients with an active network connection via the World Wide Web.

What are the Benefits of a Web Application?

Because of these factors, web applications are becoming increasingly popular. ﻿

They do not require installation because they run in web browsers. All modern browsers support it. New updates can be released without notifying users that they need to update the application. They are simple to maintain because they use the same code throughout the application. There are no compatibility issues. Backups are safe and simple. More cost-effective than mobile application development.

What Characteristics Make a Good Web Application?

A web application's main characteristics include: ﻿

The user interface responds quickly. Suitable for a wide range of screen sizes and pixel densities Cloud-hosted APIs must be cross-platform compatible, which means they must work on Linux, macOS, and Windows. A/B testing and analytics must be supported. Follow best security practices. Simple to implement Tool support for logic flows, process flows, debug logs, and so on is extensive.

Conclusion:

So, in this article we have differentiated between Web Development and web applications. All you need to worry about if you run a family business, a fresh startup, or a small organisation is your brand and a website for marketing and lead generation. The majority of business requirements can be handled with a basic bespoke website design. If your business wants to communicate with customers at any time, you'll need a web application that incorporates chat, social media, API integrations, and customised API development.﻿﻿﻿

Cristian Fernando - May 12
item.author: sdlccorp

item.title: 将 Swashbuckle.AspNetCore 版本从版本“4.0.1”更新为“6.2.3” | Update Swashbuckle.AspNetCore Version from version"4.0.1" to "6.2.3"
item.description:
加琳娜梅尔尼克

发表于你好呀。 我有一个有趣的任务：将 Swashbuckle.AspNetCore 版本从“4.0.1”更新为“6.2.3”。 它看起来很简单，但不是它看起来的样子。

主要问题是从版本 4 传递到版本5时发生的重大更改。 该项目利用 NSwag 生成 httpClient 以从另一个微服务获取数据。 在更新 Swashbuckle.AspNetCore 版本后，任何重新生成自动生成代码的尝试都会改变它。 这些导致了很多构建错误。 我应该减少新代码和旧代码之间的差异。


导致项目不兼容的主要区别是：

  • 自动生成的方法名称已更改。 例如，以前的格式是“AddPersonalTask​​V2Async”（它是控制器类方法名称）。 新的自动生成名称变为“V2Async”。 有很多这样的更改，似乎不可能手动更改所有方法调用。 一个更好的决定是将 CustomOperationIds 添加到 services.AddSwaggerGen。 就我而言：
 services.AddSwaggerGen(c => { c.SwaggerDoc("v1", new OpenApiInfo {Title = "title", Version = "v1"}); c.CustomOperationIds(apiDesc => apiDesc.TryGetMethodInfo(out MethodInfo methodInfo) ? methodInfo.Name : null); });

  • 具有属性 [FromUri] 的参数名称没有变化。 但是所有具有 [FromBody] 属性的参数都将其名称更改为 body。 如规范中所述，[FromUri] 与 Operation Object => 参数对齐，但 [FromBody] 是 Operation Object => requestBody。 请求正文对象中没有字段“名称”。

  • 参数顺序已更改。 之前的顺序与方法声明中的相同。 现在 [FromUri] 比 [FromBody] 早。 您在方法声明中编码的内容并不重要。

  • 如果您使用 JObject 作为请求参数，您会遇到一个令人不快的惊喜。 如果您不同意，最好像这样编写 ISchemaFilter 的自定义实现：

 public class JObjectSchemaFilter : ISchemaFilter { public void Apply(OpenApiSchema schema, SchemaFilterContext context) { if (context.Type != typeof(JObject)) return; schema.Type = "object"; schema.AdditionalProperties = null; } }
  • 另一个变化影响了枚举。 枚举字段名称与您的代码中的名称不同。 它们被更改为数字 1,2, ..., n。 为了解决这个奇点，应该创建另一个 ISchemaFilter 的自定义实现：
 public class EnumSchemaFilter : ISchemaFilter { public void Apply(OpenApiSchema schema, SchemaFilterContext context) { if (!context.Type.IsEnum) return; var fields = context.Type.GetFields(BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags.Public); schema.Enum = fields.Select(field => new OpenApiString(field.Name)).Cast<IOpenApiAny>().ToList(); schema.Type = "string"; schema.Properties = null; schema.AllOf = null; } }
  • 最后要提的一点：自定义请求对象的某些字段突然有了一个属性[Required]。 我不确定我是否编写了最佳决策，但这有效：
 public class SettingsSchemaFilter : ISchemaFilter { public void Apply(OpenApiSchema schema, SchemaFilterContext context) { if (context.Type != typeof(Settings)) return; if (schema.Required == null || schema.Required.Count == 0) { schema.Nullable = true; } } }

这是我面临的所有变化。

托米斯拉夫·克拉利奇 - 埃文·泰潘斯基—— May 3 '21 21 年 5 月 3 日



原文:

Galina Melnik

Posted on May 13

Hi there. I had an interesting task: to update the Swashbuckle.AspNetCore version from "4.0.1" to "6.2.3". It looked very simple but it was not what it looked like.

The main problem was breaking changes which happened when passing from version 4 to version 5. Swashbuckle.AspNetCore began to use Swagger/OpenAPI version v3 instead of OpenAPI v2. The project makes use of NSwag to generate httpClient for getting data from another microservice. Any attempt to regenerate auto generated code changed it after updating Swashbuckle.AspNetCore version. These caused a lot of build errors. I should have decreased differencies between the new code and old one.


Main difference which cause incompatibility in the project were:

  • Autogenerated method names were changed. The previous format was, for example, "AddPersonalTaskV2Async" (it was a controller class method name). New auto generated names became "V2Async". There were a lot of such changes, it seemed impossible to change all method calls by hand. A better decision was to add CustomOperationIds to services.AddSwaggerGen. In my case: 
services.AddSwaggerGen(c =>
            {
                c.SwaggerDoc("v1", new OpenApiInfo {Title = "title", Version = "v1"});
                c.CustomOperationIds(apiDesc =>
                    apiDesc.TryGetMethodInfo(out MethodInfo methodInfo) ? methodInfo.Name : null);
            });

  • There were no changes in names of parameters with attribute [FromUri]. But all parameters with attribute [FromBody] changed their names to body. As described in the spec [FromUri] is aligned with Operation Object => parameters, but [FromBody] is Operation Object => requestBody. There is no field "name" in Request Body Object.

  • Parameter order was changed. Before the order was the same as in method declaration. By now [FromUri] goes earlier than [FromBody]. It doesn't matter what you have coded in method declaration.

  • If you use JObject as a request parameter, you will be in for an unpleasant surprise. NSWag changes JObject to System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary. If you are disagree, it will be better to code custom realization of ISchemaFilter like this:

public class JObjectSchemaFilter : ISchemaFilter
    {
        public void Apply(OpenApiSchema schema, SchemaFilterContext context)
        {
            if (context.Type != typeof(JObject)) return;
            schema.Type = "object";
            schema.AdditionalProperties = null;
        }
    }
  • Another change has affected Enums. Enum fields names don't be the same as in your code. They are changed to figures 1,2, ..., n. To fix this singularity it should be created another custom realization of ISchemaFilter: 
public class EnumSchemaFilter : ISchemaFilter
    {
        public void Apply(OpenApiSchema schema, SchemaFilterContext context)
        {
            if (!context.Type.IsEnum) return;

            var fields = context.Type.GetFields(BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags.Public);

            schema.Enum = fields.Select(field => new OpenApiString(field.Name)).Cast<IOpenApiAny>().ToList();
            schema.Type = "string";
            schema.Properties = null;
            schema.AllOf = null;
        }
    }
  • The last thing I should mention: some fields of custom request objects suddenly have an attribute [Required]. I'm not sure that I coded the best decision, but this worked: 
public class SettingsSchemaFilter : ISchemaFilter
    {
        public void Apply(OpenApiSchema schema, SchemaFilterContext context)
        {
            if (context.Type != typeof(Settings)) return;
            if (schema.Required == null || schema.Required.Count == 0)
            {
                schema.Nullable = true;
            }
        }
    }

It's all changes that I faced.

Tomislav Kraljic - May 8 '21

Evan Typanski - May 3 '21

item.title: 通过本指南释放一流的 UI/UX 设计服务 | Unleash Best-in-Class UI/UX design services with this guide
item.description:
你知道是什么让一个令人难以置信的应用创意变成了一个炙手可热的应用产品吗？
是的，就是把特性、功能和技术融入其中之后的用户体验。

一个令人震惊的事实表明，平均应用程序在安装的前 3 天内损失了 77% 的 DAU。 这背后的原因是令人不快的应用程序用户体验。- Smashing Magazine

对我们中的一些人来说，这可能听起来很荒谬，但它在很大程度上说明了事实。 一个应用程序的成功并不仅仅在于它的功能，它的设计是吸引用户注意力的东西，不能让左眼避开。

归根结底，如果您的应用程序无法提供令人难以置信的UI/UX 设计服务，则只能预期一个结果：大量应用程序流失率。 不要失去你的心。 如果您真的想将您的应用程序从这种恐惧中拯救出来，那么这篇文章将成为您设计用户乐于使用的应用程序的指南。

阅读更多： https ://www.cmarix.com/blog/explore-a-complete-guide-to-attain-best-in-class-ui-ux-design-services/



原文:

Do you Know What Makes an Incredible App Idea into a Sizzling App Product?
Yes, it is the user experience after the features, functionalities, and technology are integrated into it.

A shocking fact suggests that an average app loses 77% of DAU within the first 3 days of installation. And the reason behind this is unpleasant app UX.- Smashing Magazine

It might sound absurd to some of us out there, but it speaks of fact in mass. An app doesn’t become successful just by its features, but its design is something that pulls the users’ attention and must not be given a left eye to be avoided.

It boils down to the conclusion that if you fail to have incredible UI/UX design services for your app, only one outcome can be expected: app churn rate in abundance. Don’t lose your heart. If you truly want to save your app from such horror, then this post will be your guide to designing an app your users would be happy to use.

Read more: https://www.cmarix.com/blog/explore-a-complete-guide-to-attain-best-in-class-ui-ux-design-services/
item.author: joemack

item.title: 如何使用虚拟现实升级您的业务？ | How to Upgrade Your Business with Virtual Reality?
item.description:
技术的进步有助于改善用户体验。 随着增强现实 (AR)、虚拟现实 (VR)、人工智能 (AI) 和物联网 (IoT) 等尖端技术的出现，这一点非常明显。 这些创新始终如一地为移动用户提供无缝的视听体验。

虚拟现实的影响不再局限于游戏和娱乐领域。 身临其境的技术也赢得了其他各个行业的超额响应。 它的 3D 模拟背景有可能非常顺利地实现不同的目标。 意识到重要性，许多商业组织打算与各种VR 应用程序开发公司建立直接联系。 这将帮助他们获得各种优势。 现在是时候回顾以下这些好处了——

对健康的早期见解

以前，很难预测治疗程序对人类健康的影响。 现在，医生可以在开始医疗或手术试验之前及早获得对人类健康状况的准确模拟洞察。 这主要是由于虚拟现实技术的影响，正在帮助临床医生开出适当的药物和补救疗法来彻底治愈患者。

虚拟现实正在成为各种手机游戏应用程序开发公司的重要配件。 使用这种身临其境的 3D 技术，他们可以添加不同的具有挑战性和创新性的游戏元素。 它允许游戏玩家与流行的游戏角色密切互动和竞争，以进入后续关卡。 这将进一步增加参与度，这对于随着时间的推移增加您的业务收入非常必要。

惊人的学习平台

消除学习障碍和获得优质教育不再是不可能实现的目标。 令人惊叹的虚拟现实平台满足了每个学生从任何地方获得最优质教育的愿望。 模拟环境提供了丰富的机会来实践您所学的知识，并随后实时跟踪学习进度。 此外，它还通过提供身临其境的视听数据演示来帮助理解复杂的主题。

更新培训流程

在这个瞬息万变的技术世界中，完善员工培训流程将有助于提高组织的生产力。 实现这一目标的一种可行方法是采用虚拟现实。 模拟 3D 背景可在任何地点提供舒适且安全的培训体验。 它可以帮助员工更加专注于培训过程，而不会遇到语言障碍。 此外，这项强大的技术使工人能够练习和提升他们的技能，而不会遇到任何风险。 毫无疑问，咨询知名的 VR 应用开发公司将指导您非常方便地升级培训过程。

巧妙地影响决策

在竞争激烈的商业环境中，非常有必要不断保持受众的兴趣，并专注于应用最新的技巧来影响购买者的心理以获取新的客户群。 这两个目标都可以使用虚拟现实来实现，它允许您在模拟 3D 环境的支持下非常完美地部署创新的营销策略。 它可以很好地保留现有的客户群，并在全年吸引新的观众群。

更好的体验

在应用程序开发业务中，采用正确的想法和工具来获得预期的结果非常重要。 毫无疑问，虚拟现实有助于通过模拟更好地可视化各种创新想法。 如果结果是积极的，那么您可以敦促开发人员实现这些想法。 这将帮助您更好地为客户服务。

虚拟现实的到来震撼了科技界。 沉浸式 3D 技术在影响业务活动以改善您的服务体验方面具有巨大潜力。 因此，许多企业已经开始聘请 VR 应用程序开发人员来开发能够为移动用户提供模拟视听效果的应用程序。 这将提高参与度并最终增加您的业务收入。

结论

广泛的优势吸引了商业机构采用这种强大的虚拟现实技术。 它帮助卫生组织很好地为患者服务。 该技术的模拟环境使医生可以提前知道哪些治疗可以完全缓解患者的负担。 在教育领域，虚拟现实确保了来自任何地方的每个学生都有惊人的学习机会。 此外，在虚拟现实的帮助下，影响买家的心理现在是小菜一碟。



原文:

The advancement of technology helps to refine the user experience. This is very much evident with the arrival of cutting-edge technologies like augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), artificial intelligence (AI), and the internet of things (IoT). These innovations are providing a seamless audio-visual experience to mobile users quite consistently.

The impact of virtual reality is no longer restricted to the games and entertainment sector. The immersive technology is earning a surplus response from various other industries as well. Its 3D simulated background has the potential to fulfill different objectives pretty smoothly. Sensing the importance, many commercial organisations intend to establish direct contact with various VR app development companies. It will help them to obtain various advantages. It is time to review some of these benefits below-

Early Insights on Health

Previously, it was quite difficult to predict the impact of a treatment procedure on human health. Now, doctors can obtain an accurate and simulated insight into human health conditions at an early stage before initiating a medical or surgical trial. It is mainly due to the impact of virtual reality technology that is helping clinicians to prescribe the appropriate medicines and remedial therapies to completely cure a patient.

*Add Challenging and Innovative Game Elements *

Virtual reality is becoming a crucial accessory among various mobile game app development companies. Using this immersive 3D technology, they can add different challenging and innovative game elements. It allows gamers to closely interact and compete with popular game characters to progress to subsequent levels. This will further increase engagement which is very necessary to grow your business revenue with time.

Amazing Learning Platform

Removing learning barriers and accessing quality education is no longer an impossible goal. The amazing platform of virtual reality has fulfilled the desire of every student to attain the best quality education from anywhere. The simulated environment offers abundant opportunities to implement practically what you have learned and subsequently track the study progress on a real-time basis. Moreover, it also helps to understand complex subject matters by offering an immersive audio-visual data presentation.

Update Training Process

In this ever-changing world of technology, refining the employee training process shall help to boost the productivity of your organisation. A feasible approach to meet this objective is by embracing virtual reality. The simulated 3D background offers a comfortable and secured training experience from any location. It helps employees to focus more on the training process without experiencing language barriers. Moreover, this powerful technology enables workers to practice and upgrade their skills without encountering any risk. No doubt, consulting reputed VR app development companies will guide you to upgrade the training process very conveniently.

Influence Decision Smartly

In a highly competitive business environment, it is very necessary to constantly retain the audience’s interest and also to focus on applying the latest tricks to influence the buyer’s psychology to derive a new customer base. Both these objectives can be accomplished using virtual reality which allows you to deploy innovative marketing strategies very perfectly with the support of a simulated 3D environment. It works very well to withhold the existing customer segment and also to attract new audience chunks throughout the year.

Better Experience

In the app development business, it is very important to proceed with the right ideas and tools to attain the desired outcome. No doubt, virtual reality helps in better visualisation of various innovative ideas through simulation. If the outcome is positive then you can urge developers to materialise such ideas. This will help you to serve the clients better.

The arrival of virtual reality has rocked the technology world. The immersive 3D technology has abundant potential to impact business activities to improve your service experience. As such, many enterprises have started to hire a VR app developer to bring out applications that will deliver a simulated audio-visual impact to mobile users. This has shall hike engagement and eventually grow your business revenue.

Conclusion

A wide range of advantages have tempted business establishments to adopt this powerful virtual reality technology. It helps health organisations to serve patients very well. The simulated environment of this technology allows doctors to know in advance which treatments will offer relief to the patients completely. In the education sector, virtual reality ensures amazing learning opportunities for every student from any place. Besides, influencing the psychology of buyers is a cakewalk now with the help of virtual reality.

item.title: 未验证、部分和验证的提交 | Unverified, Partially and Verified Commits
item.description:
约克维尤斯特

发表于介绍

世界上最好的VCS （版本控制系统）之一。 作为一个分布式版本控制系统，它使您能够与来自当地社区和全球的其他开发人员共享代码。 使用 GitHub，您可以创建存储库、上传文件和提交更改。

借助所有这些功能，为 GitHub 上的项目做出贡献，并且有时可以在同一个项目中包含这么多人，并且必须正确识别贡献者。 什么是 GPG 签名？

如上图所示，提交类型为Partial 、 Unverified和Verified提交。 让我们看看如何验证提交。

验证提交

生成 GPG 密钥

当提交由 GPG 密钥签名时，会验证提交。 转到您各自操作系统的GPG 命令行工具并安装它。

  • 在您的终端上，键入以下命令以生成 GPG 密钥。
    •  $ gpg --full-generate-key
    1. 在提示符处，指定所需的键类型，或按Enter接受默认值。
    2. 在提示符处，指定所需的密钥大小，或按Enter接受默认值。 您的密钥必须至少为4096位。
    3. 输入密钥应该有效的时间长度。 按Enter指定默认选择，指示密钥不会过期或其他内容，具体取决于您的偏好。
    4. 验证您的选择是否正确。
    5. 输入您的用户 ID 信息，即 GitHub 用户名、电子邮件和令牌。
    6. 键入安全密码。
    7. 使用gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long命令列出您拥有公钥和私钥的 GPG 密钥的长格式。 签署提交或标签需要私钥。
     $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
    1. 从 GPG 密钥列表中，复制您想要使用的 GPG 密钥 ID 的长格式。 在本例中，GPG 密钥 ID 为3*4*6*1*5*8... ：
     $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long /Users/hubot/.gnupg/secring.gpg ------------------------------------ sec 4096R/3AA5C34371567BD2 2016-03-10 [expires: 2017-03-10] uid Hubot ssb 4096R/42B317FD4BA89E7A 2016-03-10
    1. 复制您的 GPG 密钥，以-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----开头并以-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----结尾。
    2. 接下来，将 GPG 密钥添加到您的 GitHub 帐户

    将 GPG 密钥添加到您的 GitHub 帐户

    1. 转到您的 GitHub 帐户并单击设置，然后从侧面导航栏转到访问部分。

    2. 单击 SSH 和 GPG 密钥链接后，单击新建 GPG按钮。

    3. 按照生成 GPG 密钥的步骤 10将 GPG 密钥粘贴到文本区域并单击保存按钮。

    1. 要确认操作，请输入您的 GitHub 密码。

    结论

    通过所有这些设置，您可以确保从您的计算机和 GitHub 进行良好的提交并验证提交。 您现在可以提交到您的 GitHub 存储库。 感谢您阅读本教程。



    原文:

    yokwejuste

    Posted on May 13

    Introduction

    GitHub is a pretty awesome platform for sharing code. One of the best VCS(Version control Systems) in the world. Being a distributed version control system, it enables you to share code with other developers from your local communities and across the globe. With GitHub, you can create a repository, upload files, and commit your changes.

    With all these features, contributing to a project on GitHub and can at times include so many people in the same project and proper identification of the contributors is a must. Commits identification on GitHub is is done using a GPG signature.

    What is a GPG signature?

    The GPG(GNU Privacy Guard) signature is a digital signature that is generated using a GPG key. GPG signatures are widely used by Linux package managers such as apt to verify the integrity of downloaded files. GPG signatures are also used by GitHub to verify the integrity of commits. As seen above on the picture above the types of commits Partial, Unverified and Verified commits. Let's see how to verify the commits.

    Verifying the commits

    Generating a GPG key

    A commit is verified when the commit is signed by a GPG key. GitHub Blog explains how to generate a GPG key.

    1. Go to GPG command line tools for your respective operating system and install it.
    2. On your terminal, type the following command to generate a GPG key. 
    $ gpg --full-generate-key
    1. At the prompt, specify the kind of key you want, or press Enter to accept the default.
    2. At the prompt, specify the key size you want, or press Enter to accept the default. Your key must be at least 4096 bits.
    3. Enter the length of time the key should be valid. Press Enter to specify the default selection, indicating that the key doesn't expire or something else depending on your preference.
    4. Verify that your selections are correct.
    5. Enter your user ID information i.e. GitHub username, email and token.
    6. Type a secure passphrase.
    7. Use the gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long command to list the long form of the GPG keys for which you have both a public and private key. A private key is required for signing commits or tags. 
    $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
    1. From the list of GPG keys, copy the long form of the GPG key ID you'd like to use. In this example, the GPG key ID is 3*4*6*1*5*8...: 
    $ gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
/Users/hubot/.gnupg/secring.gpg
------------------------------------
sec   4096R/3AA5C34371567BD2 2016-03-10 [expires: 2017-03-10]
uid                          Hubot 
ssb   4096R/42B317FD4BA89E7A 2016-03-10
    1. Copy your GPG key, beginning with -----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK----- and ending with -----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----.
    2. Next, Add the GPG key to your GitHub account

    Adding a GPG key to your GitHub account

    1. Go to your GitHub account and click on Settings then go to the Access section from the side navbar.

    2. After clicking on the SSH and GPG keys link, click on the New GPG button.

    3. Following the step 10 of the Generating a GPG key Paste the GPG key in the text area and click on the Save button.

    1. To confirm the action, enter your GitHub password.

    Conclusion

    With all this setting you're sure of making good and authenticate commits from both your computer and GitHub. You can now commit to your GitHub repository. Thanks for going through this tutorial.
item.author: yokwejuste_87

item.title: 我的 SDE 实习生在 Hacker Rank 方面的经验（两次） | My SDE intern Experience With Hacker Rank (Twice)
item.description:
大家好👋

我有机会两次采访 HackerRank India，即 9 月的冬季课程和 6 月的 3 月课程，都在校外。 故事很戏剧化🙈。 希望你喜欢它。

选拔状态：❌（排灯节期间，我面试迟到了，即使在完成面试后，座位也已满）

面试经历：✔️

招聘经验：✅(OK)

我该如何申请？

我直接向他们的运营商门户网站申请，并在与招聘人员电话交谈后 4 天内收到了入围邮件，她问了我一些关于我的大学的问题，以便允许实习 6 个月和一些基本的非技术问题。

采访

现在我的第一次面试安排在黑客级别的工程经理身上。 这不是 DSA 回合（Hackerrank 不会在 20 分钟左右只问 DSA 问题，只问你的项目讨论。）在与 CTO 进行第二次面试（大约 20 到 30 分钟）之后，它又是关于项目和过去的实习经历。

之后，在 2 周内我收到了完整的座位邮件。

谢谢
希望你喜欢。



原文:

Hi everyone 👋

I got the opportunity to interview with HackerRank India for Twice i.e in September for winter session and for June session in march profiles, both off campus. The story is quite dramatic 🙈. Hope you enjoy it.

Selection Status : ❌(Even After Clearing interviews ,seat got full because I appeared little late for interview , during festival session of Diwali)

Interview Experience: ✔️

Recruitment Experience: ✅(OK)

How do I apply?

I applied directly to their carrier portal and within 4 days got mail of shortlisted after having a phone conversation with the recruiter, she asked me some questions about my college as to allow intern of 6 months and some basic non-technical questions.

Interviews

Now my 1st interview is scheduled with the engineering manager of hacker rank. It was not a DSA round(Hackerank doesn't ask DSA questions only your Project discussion around 20 min.) After it 2nd interview with CTO (around 20 to 30 min. ), it is about again on projects and past internship experiences.

After it, within 2 weeks I got seat full mail.

Thanks
Hope you like it.
item.author: pawanupadhyay10

item.title: 如何在下一次技术面试中取得好成绩 | How to ace your next tech interview
item.description:
桑佳娜·巴特

发表于技术面试每年都变得越来越难，Covid 大流行只会加剧全球范围内的竞争。

我经常在 LeetCode 上看到帖子，人们分享说他们平均需要一年时间才能获得一份好工作。
我的旅程也很相似，花了一年的准备，大量的工作申请，被拒绝和绝望的感觉，直到我终于得到了两个很棒的工作机会。 对我来说，有很多学习和不学习。

这是一个由 5 部分组成的系列的前身——关于我的准备旅程以及对我有用的东西。 希望它可以帮助与我去年在同一条船上的人。

凡妮莎·阿里斯蒂萨巴尔 - 拉杰什皇家 - 克莱门特·高迪尼埃—— May 8 '21 21 年 5 月 8 日



原文:

Sanjana Bhat

Posted on May 13

Tech interviews have been getting harder each year, the Covid pandemic only increased the competition worldwide.

I constantly see posts on LeetCode, where people shared that it took them an average of one year to get a good job offer.
My journey has been similar, it took a year of prep, lots of job applications, rejections and feeling of sheer hopelessness, until I finally got two great job offers. There have been a lot of learning and un-learning for me.

This is the precursor of a 5 part series - on my preparation journey and what worked for me. Hoping that it helps someone who is in the same boat as I was last year.

Vanessa Aristizabal - May 13 '21

Rajesh Royal - May 10 '21

Clément Gaudinière - May 8 '21
item.author: sanjbhat

item.title: 如何在 .NET 6 中构建 Windows 桌面条码和二维码扫描仪 | How to Build Windows Desktop Barcode and QR Code Scanner in .NET 6
item.description:
几周前，我发布了一个使用Dynamsoft C/C++ Barcode SDK实现的 .NET 6 BarcodeQRCodeSDK包。 本文演示了如何使用 .NET WinForms、OpenCvSharp 和 BarcodeQRCodeSDK 构建 Windows 桌面条码和 QR 码扫描仪。

安装依赖项

  • 用于从网络摄像头捕获视频流。

     dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Windows

  • 用于将 OpenCvSharp Mat 转换为位图。

     dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Extensions

  • 条码QRCodeSDK

    用于从Mat或Bitmap解码条形码和 QR 码。

     dotnet add package BarcodeQRCodeSDK

在 .NET 6 中开发 Windows 桌面条码和二维码扫描器的步骤

让我们从头开始构建应用程序。

第 1 步：创建 Windows 窗体应用程序：

我们可以通过终端中的以下命令快速创建一个新项目并安装依赖项：

 dotnet new winforms -o desktop-barcode-qrcode-scanner cd desktop-barcode-qrcode-scanner dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Windows dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Extensions dotnet add package BarcodeQRCodeSDK

然后在Form1.cs文件中导入相关的命名空间和类：

 using Dynamsoft ; using Result = Dynamsoft . BarcodeQRCodeReader . Result ; using OpenCvSharp ; using OpenCvSharp.Extensions ;

在构造函数中，我们初始化BarcodeQRCodeReader和VideoCapture实例。 您可以申请30 天试用许可证来激活条形码 SDK。

 public Form1 () { InitializeComponent (); BarcodeQRCodeReader . InitLicense ( "DLS2eyJoYW5kc2hha2VDb2RlIjoiMjAwMDAxLTE2NDk4Mjk3OTI2MzUiLCJvcmdhbml6YXRpb25JRCI6IjIwMDAwMSIsInNlc3Npb25QYXNzd29yZCI6IndTcGR6Vm05WDJrcEQ5YUoifQ==" ); // Get a license key from https://www.dynamsoft.com/customer/license/trialLicense?product=dbr reader = BarcodeQRCodeReader . Create (); capture = new VideoCapture ( 0 ); }

第 2 步：添加 UI 组件

将PictureBox和Button添加到表单设计器。 如果您使用Visual Studio Code ，则需要在Form1.Designer.cs中手动添加组件。 而如果您使用Visual Studio ，则可以将组件从工具箱拖到表单中。

 this . pictureBox1 . BorderStyle = System . Windows . Forms . BorderStyle . FixedSingle ; this . pictureBox1 . Location = new System . Drawing . Point ( 13 , 12 ); this . pictureBox1 . Margin = new System . Windows . Forms . Padding ( 4 ); this . pictureBox1 . Name = "pictureBox1" ; this . pictureBox1 . Size = new System . Drawing . Size ( 640 , 640 ); this . pictureBox1 . SizeMode = System . Windows . Forms . PictureBoxSizeMode . Zoom ; this . pictureBox1 . TabIndex = 0 ; this . pictureBox1 . TabStop = false ; this . button1 . AllowDrop = true ; this . button1 . AutoEllipsis = true ; this . button1 . Font = new System . Drawing . Font ( "Microsoft Sans Serif" , 20.25F , System . Drawing . FontStyle . Bold , System . Drawing . GraphicsUnit . Point , (( byte )( 0 ))); this . button1 . Location = new System . Drawing . Point ( 667 , 12 ); this . button1 . Margin = new System . Windows . Forms . Padding ( 4 ); this . button1 . Name = "button1" ; this . button1 . Size = new System . Drawing . Size ( 333 , 79 ); this . button1 . TabIndex = 1 ; this . button1 . Text = "Camera Scan" ; this . button1 . UseVisualStyleBackColor = true ; this . button1 . Click += new System . EventHandler ( this . button_Click );

按钮单击事件处理程序实现如下：

 private void button_Click ( object sender , EventArgs e ) { if (! capture . IsOpened ()) { MessageBox . Show ( "Failed to open video stream or file" ); return ; } if ( button2 . Text == "Camera Scan" ) { StartScan (); } else { StopScan (); } }

为了避免阻塞 UI 线程，我们在后台线程中捕获视频流。 捕获的图像类型为Mat ，不能直接传递给PictureBox 。 所以我们使用BitmapConverter将Mat转换为Bitmap 。

 private void StartScan () { button2 . Text = "Stop" ; isCapturing = true ; thread = new Thread ( new ThreadStart ( FrameCallback )); thread . Start (); } private void StopScan () { button2 . Text = "Camera Scan" ; isCapturing = false ; if ( thread != null ) thread . Join (); } private void FrameCallback () { while ( isCapturing ) { Mat frame = new Mat (); capture . Read ( frame ); pictureBox1 . Image = BitmapConverter . ToBitmap ( frame ); } }

这是用于从Mat解码条形码和 QR 码的代码：

 Result []? results = reader . DecodeBuffer ( mat . Data , mat . Cols , mat . Rows , ( int ) mat . Step (), BarcodeQRCodeReader . ImagePixelFormat . IPF_RGB_888 );

当我们得到结果时，我们在图像上绘制文本和轮廓：

 private Bitmap DecodeMat ( Mat mat ) { Result []? results = reader . DecodeBuffer ( mat . Data , mat . Cols , mat . Rows , ( int ) mat . Step (), BarcodeQRCodeReader . ImagePixelFormat . IPF_RGB_888 ); if ( results != null ) { foreach ( Result result in results ) { string output = "Text: " + result . Text + Environment . NewLine + "Format: " + result . Format1 + Environment . NewLine ; textBox1 . AppendText ( output ); textBox1 . AppendText ( Environment . NewLine ); int []? points = result . Points ; if ( points != null ) { OpenCvSharp . Point [] all = new OpenCvSharp . Point [ 4 ]; int xMin = points [ 0 ], yMax = points [ 1 ]; all [ 0 ] = new OpenCvSharp . Point ( xMin , yMax ); for ( int i = 2 ; i < 7 ; i += 2 ) { int x = points [ i ]; int y = points [ i + 1 ]; OpenCvSharp . Point p = new OpenCvSharp . Point ( x , y ); xMin = x < xMin ? x : xMin ; yMax = y > yMax ? y : yMax ; all [ i / 2 ] = p ; } OpenCvSharp . Point [][] contours = new OpenCvSharp . Point [][] { all }; Cv2 . DrawContours ( mat , contours , 0 , new Scalar ( 0 , 0 , 255 ), 2 ); if ( result . Text != null ) Cv2 . PutText ( mat , result . Text , new OpenCvSharp . Point ( xMin , yMax ), HersheyFonts . HersheySimplex , 1 , new Scalar ( 0 , 0 , 255 ), 2 ); } } } else { textBox1 . AppendText ( "No barcode detected!" + Environment . NewLine ); } Bitmap bitmap = BitmapConverter . ToBitmap ( mat ); return bitmap ; }

更新以下行以显示条形码和 QR 码结果：

 - pictureBox1.Image = BitmapConverter.ToBitmap(frame); + pictureBox1.Image = DecodeMat(mat);

现在，我们可以运行程序来扫描条形码和二维码。

源代码

使用 Subversion 下载源代码：

 svn checkout https://github.com/yushulx/dotnet-barcode-qr-code-sdk/trunk/example/desktop-gui


原文:

A few weeks ago, I released a .NET 6 BarcodeQRCodeSDK package implemented with Dynamsoft C/C++ Barcode SDK. This article demonstrates how to build a Windows desktop barcode and QR code scanner using .NET WinForms, OpenCvSharp, and BarcodeQRCodeSDK.

Install Dependencies

  • OpenCvSharp4.Windows

    Used to capture video stream from webcam.

    dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Windows

  • OpenCvSharp4.Extensions

    Used to convert OpenCvSharp Mat to Bitmap.

    dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Extensions

  • BarcodeQRCodeSDK

    Used to decode barcode and QR code from Mat or Bitmap.

    dotnet add package BarcodeQRCodeSDK

Steps to Develop Windows Desktop Barcode and QR Code Scanner in .NET 6

Let's build the application from scratch.

Step 1: Create Windows Forms App:

We can quickly create a new project and install dependencies via the following commands in terminal:

dotnet new winforms -o desktop-barcode-qrcode-scanner
cd desktop-barcode-qrcode-scanner
dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Windows
dotnet add package OpenCvSharp4.Extensions
dotnet add package BarcodeQRCodeSDK

Then import relevant namespaces and classes in Form1.cs file:

using Dynamsoft;
using Result = Dynamsoft.BarcodeQRCodeReader.Result;
using OpenCvSharp;
using OpenCvSharp.Extensions;

In constructor, we initialize BarcodeQRCodeReader and VideoCapture instances. You can apply for a 30-day trial license to activate the barcode SDK.

public Form1()
{ InitializeComponent(); BarcodeQRCodeReader.InitLicense("DLS2eyJoYW5kc2hha2VDb2RlIjoiMjAwMDAxLTE2NDk4Mjk3OTI2MzUiLCJvcmdhbml6YXRpb25JRCI6IjIwMDAwMSIsInNlc3Npb25QYXNzd29yZCI6IndTcGR6Vm05WDJrcEQ5YUoifQ=="); // Get a license key from https://www.dynamsoft.com/customer/license/trialLicense?product=dbr reader = BarcodeQRCodeReader.Create(); capture = new VideoCapture(0);
}

Step 2: Add UI Components

Add a PictureBox and a Button to the form designer. The PictureBox is used to display the camera video stream, and the Button is used to start and stop scanning barcode and QR code from video frames. If you use Visual Studio Code, you need to manually add the components in Form1.Designer.cs. Whereas if you use Visual Studio, you can drag the components from toolbox to the form.

this.pictureBox1.BorderStyle = System.Windows.Forms.BorderStyle.FixedSingle;
this.pictureBox1.Location = new System.Drawing.Point(13, 12);
this.pictureBox1.Margin = new System.Windows.Forms.Padding(4);
this.pictureBox1.Name = "pictureBox1";
this.pictureBox1.Size = new System.Drawing.Size(640, 640);
this.pictureBox1.SizeMode = System.Windows.Forms.PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom;
this.pictureBox1.TabIndex = 0;
this.pictureBox1.TabStop = false; this.button1.AllowDrop = true;
this.button1.AutoEllipsis = true;
this.button1.Font = new System.Drawing.Font("Microsoft Sans Serif", 20.25F, System.Drawing.FontStyle.Bold, System.Drawing.GraphicsUnit.Point, ((byte)(0)));
this.button1.Location = new System.Drawing.Point(667, 12);
this.button1.Margin = new System.Windows.Forms.Padding(4);
this.button1.Name = "button1";
this.button1.Size = new System.Drawing.Size(333, 79);
this.button1.TabIndex = 1;
this.button1.Text = "Camera Scan";
this.button1.UseVisualStyleBackColor = true;
this.button1.Click += new System.EventHandler(this.button_Click);

The button click event handler is implemented as follows:

private void button_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
{ if (!capture.IsOpened()) { MessageBox.Show("Failed to open video stream or file"); return; } if (button2.Text == "Camera Scan") { StartScan(); } else { StopScan(); }
}

Step3: Display Camera Video Stream in PictureBox

To avoid blocking the UI thread, we capture video stream in a background thread. The type of the captured image is Mat, which cannot be passed to PictureBox directly. So we use BitmapConverter to convert Mat to Bitmap.

private void StartScan() { button2.Text = "Stop"; isCapturing = true; thread = new Thread(new ThreadStart(FrameCallback)); thread.Start();
} private void StopScan() { button2.Text = "Camera Scan"; isCapturing = false; if (thread != null) thread.Join();
} private void FrameCallback() { while (isCapturing) { Mat frame = new Mat(); capture.Read(frame); pictureBox1.Image = BitmapConverter.ToBitmap(frame); }
}

Step4: Decode Barcode and QR Code from Camera Video Stream

Here is the code used for decoding barcode and QR code from Mat:

Result[]? results = reader.DecodeBuffer(mat.Data, mat.Cols, mat.Rows, (int)mat.Step(), BarcodeQRCodeReader.ImagePixelFormat.IPF_RGB_888);

As we get the results, we draw text and contours on the image:

private Bitmap DecodeMat(Mat mat)
{ Result[]? results = reader.DecodeBuffer(mat.Data, mat.Cols, mat.Rows, (int)mat.Step(), BarcodeQRCodeReader.ImagePixelFormat.IPF_RGB_888); if (results != null) { foreach (Result result in results) { string output = "Text: " + result.Text + Environment.NewLine + "Format: " + result.Format1 + Environment.NewLine; textBox1.AppendText(output); textBox1.AppendText(Environment.NewLine); int[]? points = result.Points; if (points != null) { OpenCvSharp.Point[] all = new OpenCvSharp.Point[4]; int xMin = points[0], yMax = points[1]; all[0] = new OpenCvSharp.Point(xMin, yMax); for (int i = 2; i < 7; i += 2) { int x = points[i]; int y = points[i + 1]; OpenCvSharp.Point p = new OpenCvSharp.Point(x, y); xMin = x < xMin ? x : xMin; yMax = y > yMax ? y : yMax; all[i / 2] = p; } OpenCvSharp.Point[][] contours = new OpenCvSharp.Point[][] { all }; Cv2.DrawContours(mat, contours, 0, new Scalar(0, 0, 255), 2); if (result.Text != null) Cv2.PutText(mat, result.Text, new OpenCvSharp.Point(xMin, yMax), HersheyFonts.HersheySimplex, 1, new Scalar(0, 0, 255), 2); } } } else { textBox1.AppendText("No barcode detected!" + Environment.NewLine); } Bitmap bitmap = BitmapConverter.ToBitmap(mat); return bitmap;
}

Update the following line to show barcode and QR code results:

- pictureBox1.Image = BitmapConverter.ToBitmap(frame);
+ pictureBox1.Image = DecodeMat(mat);

Now, we can run the program to scan barcode and QR code.

Source Code

https://github.com/yushulx/dotnet-barcode-qr-code-sdk/tree/main/example/desktop-gui

Download the source code with Subversion:

svn checkout https://github.com/yushulx/dotnet-barcode-qr-code-sdk/trunk/example/desktop-gui
item.author: yushulx

item.title: AWS 云托管服务为您的业务带来的 7 大优势 | The 7 Benefits Of AWS Cloud Managed Services For Your Business
item.description:
尼尔·史密斯

发表于如果您像大多数企业主一样，总是在寻找提高效率和节省资金的方法。 您可能听说过使用 AWS 云托管服务为您的企业带来的好处，但您不确定是否值得投资。 在这篇博文中，我们将讨论使用 AWS 云托管服务的 7 个主要好处。 我们还将提供企业如何从使用这些服务中受益的示例。

通过使用 AWS 云托管服务，企业可以享受许多好处，包括：

  1. 提高效率：使用 AWS 云托管服务的最大好处之一是它可以帮助提高您的业务效率。 通过将 AWS 基础设施的管理外包给专家团队，您可以腾出时间专注于业务的其他方面。
  2. 灵活性：在如何使用 AWS 基础设施方面，使用 AWS 云托管服务还可以为您提供更大的灵活性。 借助管理您的 AWS 基础设施的专家团队，您可以根据需要轻松扩展或缩减，而不必担心管理开销。
  3. 提高性能：使用godaddy migrate的最大好处之一是它可以帮助提高应用程序和系统的性能。 通过将 AWS 基础设施的管理外包给专家团队，您可以确保您的应用程序和系统始终以最佳性能运行。
  4. 提高可靠性：使用 AWS 云托管服务的另一大好处是它可以帮助提高应用程序和系统的可靠性。 当您将 AWS 基础设施的管理外包给专家团队时，您可以确保您的应用程序和系统始终处于正常运行状态。
  5. 降低成本：使用 AWS 云托管服务的最大好处之一是它可以帮助降低 IT 基础设施的成本。 通过将 AWS 基础设施的管理外包给专家团队，您可以在硬件、软件和支持等方面节省资金。
  6. 更快的部署：使用 AWS 云托管服务的最大好处之一是它可以帮助加快应用程序和系统的部署。 通过将 AWS 基础设施的管理外包给专家团队，您可以更快地启动和运行您的应用程序和系统。
  7. 提高安全性：使用托管云 AWS 的另一大好处是它可以帮助提高应用程序和系统的安全性。 通过将 AWS 基础设施的管理工作外包给专家团队，您可以获得最新的安全技术和实践。

结论

通过将您的 IT 需求外包给云，您可以腾出内部资源来专注于更重要的任务。 此外，使用托管服务提供商可以帮助降低与基础设施和人员配备相关的成本。 如果您正在寻找一种方法来提高组织内的效率并降低成本，请考虑 AWS 云托管服务。

法比奥·乔纳森·阿里芬 - May 12 5月12日



原文:

Neil Smith

Posted on May 13

If you are like most business owners, you are always looking for ways to improve efficiency and save money. You may have heard about the benefits of using AWS cloud managed services for your business, but you're not sure if it's worth the investment. In this blog post, we will discuss the 7 main benefits of using AWS cloud managed services. We will also provide examples of how businesses have benefited from using these services. 

By Using AWS Cloud Managed Services, Businesses Can Enjoy A Number Of Benefits, Including: 

  1. Improved Efficiency: One of the biggest benefits of using AWS cloud managed services is that it can help improve your business's efficiency. By outsourcing the management of your AWS infrastructure to a team of experts, you can free up your own time to focus on other aspects of your business. 
  2. Flexibility: Using AWS cloud managed services also gives you more flexibility when it comes to how you use your AWS infrastructure. With a team of experts managing your AWS infrastructure, you can easily scale up or down as needed, without having to worry about the management overhead. 
  3. Improved Performance: One of the biggest benefits of using godaddy migrate is that it can help improve the performance of your applications and systems. By outsourcing the management of your AWS infrastructure to a team of experts, you can ensure that your applications and systems are always running at peak performance. 
  4. Increased Reliability: Another big benefit of using AWS cloud managed services is that it can help increase the reliability of your applications and systems. When you outsource the management of your AWS infrastructure to a team of experts, you can be sure that your applications and systems are always up and running. 
  5. Lower Costs: One of the biggest benefits of using AWS cloud managed services is that it can help lower the costs of your IT infrastructure. By outsourcing the management of your AWS infrastructure to a team of experts, you can save money on things like hardware, software, and support. 
  6. Faster Deployment: One of the biggest benefits of using AWS cloud managed services is that it can help speed up the deployment of your applications and systems. By outsourcing the management of your AWS infrastructure to a team of experts, you can get your applications and systems up and running faster. 
  7. Improved Security: Another big benefit of using managed cloud AWS is that it can help improve the security of your applications and systems. By outsourcing the management of your AWS infrastructure to a team of experts, you can get access to the latest security technologies and practices. 

Conclusion 

AWS Cloud Managed Services can help your business save time and money. By outsourcing your IT needs to the cloud, you can free up internal resources to focus on more important tasks. Additionally, using a managed service provider can help reduce costs related to infrastructure and staffing. If you’re looking for a way to improve efficiency and lower costs within your organization, consider AWS Cloud Managed Services. 

Fabio Jonathan Arifin - May 12

item.title: 事件溯源 - 基本概念 | Event sourcing - basic concepts
item.description:
在 Culture Amp，我们越来越多地采用事件源系统，原因如下：

  • 架构模块化和灵活性
  • 强调领域驱动设计和建模，最能代表业务领域
  • 语义上有意义的仅附加事件流意味着我们永远不必丢弃数据，使我们能够在我们的系统中以新的和有趣的方式利用这些最高保真度的历史数据

我们有两个小型的开源事件溯源微框架，
一个名为Event Framework用 Ruby 编写，另一个用 Kotlin 编写，名为Kestrel 。

随着我们雇用越来越多的人并进行越来越多的事件采购，我们正在寻找让新来者掌握基本知识的简单方法。

示例域

我正在寻找一个可以代表事件溯源基本概念的示例域：

  • 几乎没有文字，偏爱形状、符号、框和箭头，以强调事物如何组合在一起的机制
  • 一个著名的有界域

俄罗斯方块

我意识到俄罗斯方块符合这两个条件，其简约的标志性形状和纯粹的老式无处不在 - 每个人都玩过俄罗斯方块！

这是俄罗斯方块的事件源实现的外观示例，下面我们将逐个解压缩它。

骨料

聚合实例是事情发生的中心实体。 在许多事件源系统中，可能有许多不同类型的聚合的许多实例。 聚合往往有一个生命周期：一个创建事件、许多更新事件，以及通常但不总是一个结束事件。

在俄罗斯方块示例中，每次开始新游戏时都会实例化Tetris Playfield Aggregate 。 在这个实现中，首先发生的事情（创建事件）是一个New Block Appeared在 Playfield 的顶部。 聚合的每个实例都有一个 id，在本例中为 id a 、 b和c 。

命令

一种输入形式是用户命令，它表示用户对聚合做某事的意图，并且可能成功或被拒绝。

在俄罗斯方块示例中，用户可能会尝试向左或向右轻推块，顺时针或逆时针旋转它，或者立即将其发送到 Playfield 的底部。 如果用户试图通过墙壁移动块，用户命令将被拒绝。

活动

当聚合不拒绝命令时，它会将一个或多个事件发送到事件存储。 然后聚合可以使用这些事件来建立“刚刚好”的状态，以便接受或拒绝后续命令。

在俄罗斯方块示例中，当Tetris Playfield Aggregate的当前状态接受移动命令时，“更新”事件Moved Right 、 Moved Left 、 Rotated Clockwise 、 Rotated Anti-clockwise和Sent To Bottom保存为以时间排序的仅追加方式的事件。

非用户演员

除了用户命令，调度命令、反应器或其他参与者可以对聚合进行操作。

预定命令

在事件源系统中具有“预定命令”是一种常见模式。

在俄罗斯方块示例中，我们让Time按计划运行（每秒一次），与聚合进行交互。 正是Time的滴答声允许方块向下移动，当聚合体检测到方块已经到达地面时，聚合体可以在Fell事件之后发出一个额外的Landed事件。

反应堆

反应器具有额外的功能，它们可以侦听迄今为止发生的事件，并通过将新命令发送回聚合来对这些事件做出反应。

在俄罗斯方块示例中，我们希望Landed事件触发 Playfield 顶部New Block Appeared 。

结合能够“着陆”的方块和能够进入 Playfield 的新方块，这意味着聚合现在可以接收Line Cleared或Tetris Cleared事件，当然还有当 Playfield 满时Game Over “结束事件”。

投影仪

到目前为止，我们只讨论了“写入”操作，即尝试更改系统和接收事件的事情。 在事件源系统中，我们通常希望能够以各种特定于上下文的形式查看或“查询”系统的状态。

投影机（和反应器）只需要监听相关类型的事件，可以跳过其余的，如图所示。

在俄罗斯方块示例中，玩家需要能够查看 Playfield 的当前状态，以便他们可以决定他们希望发送哪些进一步的输入命令。 比赛结束后，他们可以看到比赛期间发生的事情的摘要，包括他们的最终得分。

整个画面

通过以下基本构建块，我们拥有完整的端到端事件源系统：

  • 命令
  • 骨料
  • 活动
  • 反应堆
  • 投影仪
  • 查询

俄罗斯方块是一个很好的领域来说明事件溯源的基本原理，但实际上，人们可能不会使用事件溯源来构建视频游戏，因为视频游戏需要针对非常低的延迟吞吐量进行优化。 相反，事件源系统针对领域丰富性、数据保真度、架构模块化和灵活性进行了优化。 在以后的文章中，我们将深入探讨如何使用代码实际构建这些系统。



原文:

At Culture Amp we are increasingly embracing event sourced systems for a few reasons:

  • Architectural modularisation and flexibility
  • The strong emphasis on Domain Driven Design and modelling, best representing the business domain
  • The semantically meaningful append-only event streams mean we never have to throw away data, allowing us to leverage this highest-possible-fidelity historical data in new and interesting ways across our systems

We have two small open-source event-sourcing micro-frameworks,
one named Event Framework written in Ruby, and another written in Kotlin named Kestrel.

As we hire more and more people and do more and more event sourcing, we're looking for easy ways for newcomers to gain a grasp of the basics.

An example domain

I was searching for an example domain that could represent the basic concepts of event sourcing with:

  • Little-to-no text, favouring shapes, symbols, boxes and arrows, in order to emphasise the mechanics of how things sit together
  • A well-known, bounded domain

Tetris

I realised Tetris ticks both of these boxes, with its minimalist, iconic shapes and sheer oldschool ubiquity - everyone has played Tetris!

The Tetris Playfield also has the benefit of being an entity that is subject to forces other than just the Player, it is affected by new blocks coming in from outside (in reaction to the previous block landing), and subject to the ticking of time (gravity moving the blocks down the Playfield).

Here is an example of how an event sourced implementation of Tetris might look, and below we will unpack it, piece by piece.

Aggregates

An aggregate instance is the central entity to which things happen. In many event sourced systems there can be many instances of many different types of aggregates. Aggregates tend to have a life-cycle: a creation event, many update events, and, often but not always, an ending event.

In the Tetris example, the Tetris Playfield Aggregate is instantiated every time a new game is started. In this implementation, the first thing that happens (the creation event) to it is a New Block Appeared at the top of the Playfield. Each instance of the aggregate has an id, in this case, ids a, b and c.

Commands

One form of input are user commands, which represent the user intention to do something to an aggregate, and which may succeed or be rejected.

In the Tetris example, the user may attempt to nudge the block left or right, rotate it clockwise or anti-clockwise, or send it immediately to the bottom of the Playfield. If the user tries to move the block through a wall, the user command will be rejected.

Events

When an aggregate doesn't reject a command, it sinks one or more events to the event store. The aggregate can then use these events to build up "just enough" state in order to accept or reject subsequent commands.

In the Tetris example, when the movement commands are accepted by the Tetris Playfield Aggregate based on the current state of the Playfield, the "update" events Moved Right, Moved Left, Rotated Clockwise, Rotated Anti-clockwise and Sent To Bottom are saved as events in a time ordered append-only fashion.

Non-user Actors

Apart from user commands, scheduled commands, reactors or other actors may act upon an aggregate.

Scheduled commands

It's a common pattern to have "scheduled commands" in event sourced systems.

In the Tetris example, we have Time running on a schedule (once per second), interacting with the aggregate. It's the ticking of Time that allows blocks to move down the playfield, and when the aggregate detects that the block has reached the ground, the aggregate can emit an additional Landed event following the Fell event.

Reactors

Reactors have the additional feature that they can listen to the events that have happened so far, and react to those events by sending new commands back into aggregates.

In the Tetris example, we want the Landed event to trigger a New Block Appeared at the top of the Playfield.

With the combination of blocks being able to "land" and new blocks being able to enter the Playfield, this means the aggregate can now sink Line Cleared or Tetris Cleared events, and of course Game Over "ending event" when the Playfield gets full.

Projectors

So far we've only spoken about "write" actions, things that attempt change the system and sink events. In event sourced systems, we usually want to be able to view or "query" the state of the system in various context-specific forms.

"Projectors" allow us to "project" the events in the event store into different views or "projections". Projectors (and Reactors) only need to listen for events with relevant types and can skip the rest, as indicated in the diagram.

In the Tetris example, the Player needs to be able to view the current state of the Playfield so that they can decide which further input commands they wish to send. Once the game is over, they can see a summary of what happened during the game, including their final score.

The whole picture

With the following basic building blocks we have a full end-to-end eventsourced system:

  • Commands
  • Aggregates
  • Events
  • Reactors
  • Projectors
  • Queries

Tetris is a great domain to illustrate the fundamentals of event sourcing, but in reality, one probably wouldn't build a video game using event sourcing because video games need to optimise for very low latency throughput. Event sourced systems optimise instead for domain richness, data fidelity, architectural modularisation and flexibility. In future articles we will dive into how to to actually build these systems with code.
item.author: williamboxhall

item.title: JavaScript 技术面试题 2 | JavaScript Technical Interview Questions 2
item.description:
问：关于 JavaScript 中的函数，以下哪项陈述不正确？

  • 可以在整个代码中重用函数
  • 函数可以接收可以改变函数输出的参数
  • 函数可以递归。
  • 必须始终为函数分配标识符

答案 - 必须始终为函数分配一个标识符

没有标识符的函数称为匿名函数，在 JavaScript 中使用非常频繁。

问：JavaScript 使用的两种作用域是什么？

  • 全球和本地
  • 国外和本地
  • 外部和内部
  • 周边和内部

答案 - 全球和本地

问：作为开发人员，我希望能够删除数组的最后一个元素，并且还希望能够在数组的开头添加一个新元素。 我应该使用哪两种数组方法？

  • 推（）和排序（）
  • 答案 - pop() 和 unshift()

    问：我们如何访问存储在对象中的值？

    • 周期符号，方括号符号
    • 点表示法、括号表示法
    • 点表示法、大括号表示法
    • 等号，抽象符号

    答案 - 点符号和括号符号

    我们有两种访问对象内部数据的方法，点表示法和括号表示法。

    问：什么是对象方法？

    • 保存在对象内部的数组
    • 对象中分配有数字的键
    • 与对象关联的函数
    • 以对象为参数的函数

    答案 - 与对象关联的函数

    问：“This”运算符的目的是什么？

    • 关键字'This'指的是它所在的对象。'This'根据它在被调用时所在的对象而变化。
    • 这是一个数组，我们可以在其中轻松存储全局变量，以便在需要访问它们时使用。
    • 这个'关键字让我们可以引用我们的窗口，让我们可以访问特殊的对象方法。
    • 答案 - 关键字“This”指的是它所在的对象。“This”会根据它在被调用时所在的对象而变化。

      问：从下面列出的原因来看，JavaScript 不正确。

      • 答案 - JavaScript 比大多数编程语言更好地处理数字。



原文:

Q. Which statement below is not true about functions in JavaScript?

  • Functions can be reused throughout your code
  • Functions can receive arguments that can alter the output of a function
  • Functions are able to be recursive.
  • A function must always be assigned an identifier

Answer - A function must always be assigned an identifier

Functions without identifiers are called anonymous functions which are used quite frequently used in JavaScript.

Q. What are the two types of scope JavaScript uses?

  • Global and Local
  • Abroad and Local
  • Outside and Inside
  • Surrounding and Inner

Answer - Global and Local

Q. As a developer, I want to be able to remove the last element of my array and I want to also be able to add a new element to the beginning of my array. Which two array methods should I use?

  • concat() and shift()
  • forEach() and pop()
  • push() and sort()
  • pop() and unshift()

Answer - pop() and unshift()

The pop array method removes the last element of an array and the unshift method adds an element to beginning of the array.

Q. How do we access a value stored in an object?

  • Period notation, Square bracket notation
  • Dot notation, Bracket notation
  • Dot notation, Curl bracket notation
  • Equal notation, Abstract notation

Answer - Dot Notation & Bracket Notation

We have two ways of accessing data inside of an object, dot notation and bracket notation.

Q. What is an object method?

  • An array saved inside of an object
  • Keys in an object that have a number assigned to it
  • A function associated with an object
  • A function that takes an object for an argument

Answer - A function associated with an object

Q. What is the purpose of the 'This' operator?

  • The keyword 'This' refers to the object it is in. 'This' changes based on which object it is in when being called.
  • This' is an array where we can easily store global variables for when we need access to them.
  • This' keyword lets us make a reference to our window gives us access to special object methods.
  • This' keyword allows us to specify certain variables to it which can be used in the global scope.

Answer - The keyword 'This' refers to the object it is in. 'This' changes based on which object it is in when being called.

Q. From the reason listed below which is NOT true about JavaScript.

  • JavaScript increases interactivity of our websites.
  • Javascript allows developers to create richer interfaces for the users.
  • JavaScript lets provide the user immediate feedback upon an action.
  • JavaScripts handles numbers better than most programming languages.

Answer - JavaScripts handles numbers better than most programming languages.
item.author: aryakris

item.title: React 中 useState() 的一个时髦类比 | A Funky Analogy for useState() in React
item.description:
这些钩子之一是 useState()。 提示和示例：

为了便于理解，建议您以标识您正在使用的内容的方式命名状态变量。 在下面的示例中，我们正在处理某物的“名称”，特别是我们正在处理用户将在“名称”输入字段中键入的文本。 出于这个原因，我们将 useState() 的变量命名为“name”和“setName”。 因为我们也知道用户将输入一个字符串值，所以我们实际上将初始状态设置为空引号，因为我们知道最初输入字段将为空，但将用于评估字符串值。

快速类比

在我们继续解释其余部分之前，我想使用一个类比来帮助我理解 React 中的 useState()。 在尝试将 useState() 概念化时，我最初觉得我的大脑要炸了🤯。

然而，我开始使用 useState() 的次数越多，我开始接受它有点像穿越时空的混乱感觉。 我开始思考，每当我使用 useState() 时，感觉就像是在为自己准备好穿越时空。 具体来说，我在时间机器一集中开始考虑章鱼哥。

如果您是 useState() 的新手，那么我希望您将自己视为 Squidward，就好像您在使用 useState() 时正在穿越时空一样。

也许你在问自己，怎么做？ 让我们回到“名称”的例子。 在这个例子中，我们有一个表单，一旦用户点击“Travel Through Time！”，就会提交该表单。 按钮。 提交后，我们将评估他们在输入框中输入的字符串。 因此，为了注册该更改，我们编写了一个函数，该函数将知道如何处理将在“名称”输入字段中发生的更改。 该功能被相应地标记，可以在下面引用。 如您所见，该函数接受的参数是一个事件（提交），提交后我们将获得输入字段的值。

由于用户正在输入我们想要设置为我们的“名称”的信息字符串，以便它可以显示在我们的浏览器上，我们将使用“setName”作为一种基本上设置“名称”的（未来）当前状态的方法. 在这种情况下，我在括号中说 (future)，因为在提交表单之前，“name”的状态不会反映 e.target.value——它只会在提交之后变成那个状态。 因此，既然我们使用“setName”来获取 e.target.value，那么下次我们使用“name”时，我们将输入“e.target.value”中的任何内容。 例如，如果我们在名称输入字段中输入“bob”后使用 console.log(name) 并单击提交，我们可以在下面看到变量“name”现在设置为“bob”，因为那是我们的“ e.target.value”。

确保我们的 setter 函数正确运行并更新“name”状态的另一种方法是在您键入时跟踪状态。 在下图中，您可以看到当用户在名称输入字段中键入时，状态正在更新为名称输入字段中的任何内容。

如果不使用 setter 函数会发生什么？

请务必记住，您只能使用 setter 函数对当前状态进行实际更改。 因此，例如，如果我们在代码中键入 name(e.target.value)，则状态将不会更新，因此代码将无法工作。 如果我们尝试键入我们的开发人员工具将显示一条错误消息，指出“名称”不是一个函数，因此不能接受“e.target.value”的参数。

在这个代码示例中，我们还将输入字段的值设置为“名称”，输入字段不允许我们输入。 它不允许我们输入，因为没有 setter 函数就无法更新“name”的状态。 因此，输入字段将保持停滞且不可更改。

那么，章鱼哥又是如何融入其中的呢？

回到 Squidward 的时间旅行类比，我认为 useState() 是一种获取某物的当前状态（或值）并以某种方式对其进行更改的方式，以便将来它可以代表不同的东西。 将 useState() 视为 Squidward 乘坐的电梯。 这让他可以接触到海绵宝宝的不同阶段。 章鱼哥转向的时代手柄将是正在发生的事件。

我们将获得的“海绵宝宝”类型取决于我们将获得的事件的值，具体取决于事件（时代句柄）。 如果时间段的值是过去，那么我们将得到 Caveman SpongeBob。

但是，如果时间段的值是未来，我们将得到 FutureBob。

无论手柄落在哪个时代，初始状态都会发生变化，我们将获得穴居人海绵宝宝或未来的海绵宝宝。 海绵宝宝在电梯时代手柄之前的先前状态（初始状态）将是章鱼哥知道（并且喜欢）的常规海绵宝宝。

保持学习！

这个类比可能有点极端，但如果你是像我一样在视觉上思考的人，它可能会帮助你想象一些东西。 这种联系让我能够真正了解正在发生的事情以及如何整体使用 useState()。 如果这个类比没有那么有用，我用我的一个朋友的话来结束这篇博文，他说他喜欢将 setter 函数视为“const variableName =”。这种更简短的解释方式可以直接进入点，当你使用 setter 函数时，你正在更新状态的前一个值。无论是什么让你继续编码并更适应 useState() 都是你继续编码之旅时应该参考的东西！



原文:

React has different hooks that you can use while coding. One of these hooks is useState(). useState() gives you the opportunity to take a variable that you would like to change and preserve the current state to manipulate it in a function.

Tips & An Example:

In order to follow along easily it is recommended that you name the state variables in a way that identifies what you are working with. useState() also needs an initial state which you set in the parenthesis. In the example below we are working with the "name" of something, specifically we are working with the text that the user would type in the “name” input field. For this reason we name the variables for useState() as “name” and “setName”. Since we also know that the user will be typing a string value we actually set the initial state as empty quotes because we know that initially the input field will be empty, but will be used to evaluate a string value.

Quick Analogy

Before we move onto the rest of the explanation, I want to use an analogy that helped me understand useState() in React. When trying to conceptualize useState() I initially felt like my brain was blowing up 🤯 .

However, the more I started working with useState() I started to embrace the chaotic feel of it being somewhat like traveling through time. I started thinking about how whenever I used useState() it felt like I was bracing myself for a ride through time. Specifically I started thinking about the Squidward in the time machine episode.

If you’re new to useState(), then I want you to think of yourself as Squidward as if you were traveling through time whenever you use useState().

...back to our example!

Maybe you’re asking yourself, how? Let’s step back into the example of “name”. In this example we have a form that will be submitted by the user once they click on the "Travel Through Time!" button. Upon submission we will be evaluating the string they have typed into the input box. Therefore, in order to register that change, we write a function that will know what to do with the change that will be occurring in the “name” input field. The function is labeled accordingly and can be referenced below. As you can see the parameter the function is taking in is an event (the submission) and upon the submission we will get the value of the input field.

Since the user is inputting the string of information that we want to set as our "name" so that it can display on our browser, we will use “setName” as a way to essentially set the (future) current state of “name”. In this case I say (future) in parenthesis because before the form is submitted the state of “name” won’t be reflecting the e.target.value– it will only become that after the submission. So now that we used the "setName" to take in e.target.value, then the next time we use “name” we will have whatever was input into “e.target.value”. For example, if we console.log(name) after typing "bob" in the name input field and we click submit, we can see below that the variable "name" now is set to "bob" because that is what was our "e.target.value".

Another way to make sure our setter function is operating correctly and updating the state of "name" is by tracking the state as you are typing. In the image below, you can see that as the user types in the name input field the state is updating to whatever is in the name input field.

What happens if don't use the setter function?

It’s important to remember that you can only use the setter function to actually make the changes you want to the current state. So for example, if we typed name(e.target.value) in our code instead, the state would not be updated and therefore the code would not work. If we try to type our developer tools would display an error message stating that "name" is not a function and therefore cannot take in the parameter of "e.target.value".

In this example of code, we also set the value of our input field as "name" the input field would not let us type. It wouldn’t allow us to type because there is no way to update the state of “name” without the setter function. Thus, the input field would stay stagnant and unchangeable.

So, how does Squidward fit into this again?

Back to the Squidward time traveling analogy, I think about useState() as a way to take the current state (or value) of something and change it in a way so that in the future it can represent something different. Think of useState() as the elevator that Squidward gets on. Which then allows him to be exposed to the different phases of SpongeBob. The era handle that Squidward turns would be the event that is happening.

The initial state of useState() would be SpongeBob as his current self.

The kind of “SpongeBob” we would get would depend on the value of the event which we will get depending on the event (the era handle). The initial state of “SpongeBob” would change based on the time period (our e.target.value) the era handle (the event) lands on. If the value of the time period is the past then we would get Caveman SpongeBob.

However, if the value of the time period is the future we would get FutureBob.

Regardless of what era the handle lands on, there will be a change of the initial state and we will either get Caveman SpongeBob or future SpongeBob. The previous state of SpongeBob before the elevator era handle (the initial state) would be the regular SpongeBob that Squidward knows (and loves).

Keep Learning!

The analogy can be a bit extreme, but if you are someone who thinks visually like me, it may help you to visualize something. This connection allowed me to really picture what was happening and how to use useState() as a whole. If this analogy isn’t as helpful, I end this blog post with the wise words of a friend of mine who said that he likes to think about the setter function as “const variableName = ". This shorter way of explaining gets straight to the point, that when you use the setter function you are updating the previous value of the state. Whatever gets you to continue to code along and get more comfortable with useState() is what you should refer to as you continue your coding journey!
item.author: penelopedurand

item.title: 我需要有关任天堂问题的帮助。 | I need assistance with a Nintendo problem.
item.description:
藤虎39

发表于您好，我一直在尝试登录我的 Nintendo 帐户，但是当我输入我的电子邮件和密码时，它显示“无法查看此页面”。 请从头开始。 请不要使用浏览器的后退按钮。”任何帮助将不胜感激！

fujitora39

Posted on May 13

Hello, I've been attempting to log into my Nintendo account, but when I enter my email and password, it says "this page cannot be viewed." Please start over from the beginning. Please do not utilize your browser's back button." Any assistance would be greatly appreciated!

Cristian Fernando - May 12
item.author: fujitora25

item.title: JavaScript 中的 For 循环 | For-Loop in JavaScript
item.description:
只能根据索引值访问数组是有限制的。 当我们这样做时，我们会重复很多代码。 相反，我们可以使用for-loop多次执行代码。

我们使用 for 循环的主要原因之一是因为我们可以遍历数组中的项目。 我们的循环将运行多少次取决于 for 循环的定义。

让我们更详细地分解一下我们的 for 循环。

每个 for 循环都以 FOR 关键字开头，后跟一组括号。 我们的 if 语句看起来很熟悉。 括号后面是大括号。

 for ( var i = 0;i < 5; i++) { This block of code will run each time }

进入我们的 for 循环，我们得到三个部分。 在上面的代码块中var i = 0是我们的声明性语句 - 我们在这里声明可以在整个过程中使用的变量。 我们将从零位置开始，这是我们在这里定义的。 换句话说，当 i 小于 5 时，我们的条件不再计算为真。这将我们带到i++的最后一部分，我们增加或修改指针以改变我们在循环中移动的方式。 在 for 循环中，我们还利用了数组长度属性。 我们循环直到我们比数组长度少一。 在 for 循环中，我们不是在数组上循环，而是在一个位置上循环，以从头到尾指向数组的每个部分。



原文:

There's a limitation of only accessing arrays based on the index value. We would be repeating a lot of code when we are doing this. Instead we can use a for-loop to execute code more than once.

One of the primary reasons for us to use our for-loop is because we can loop over the items in our array. For-loops are often used to iterate over arrays.For-loops are just iterative repeatable blocks of code that is going to run a certain number of times. How many times our loop is going to run is based on for-loop definition.

Let's breakdown our for-loop a bit more in detail.

Every for loop begins with a FOR keyword followed by a set of parentheses. This looks familiar to our if statements. Followed by parentheses there will be curly brackets.

for (
var i = 0;i < 5; i++) {
This block of code will run each time
}

Going into our for-loop we get three parts. In the block of code above var i = 0 is our declarative statement - where we come and declare our variables which can be used through out the process. We are going to start at position zero is what we are defining here.i < 5 determines when our loop should be broken. In other words our condition no longer evaluates to true when i is less than 5. This brings us to the last part i++ where we increment or modify our pointer to change how we are moving through the loop. i++ means i = i+1 which then brings us back to the condition check.

In for-loop we also take the advantage of arrays length property. We loop until we are one less than the array length. In a for-loop we are not looping over an array but we are looping over a position to point in to each part of our array from start to finish.
item.author: aryakris

item.title: 使用 WebGL 构建 3D 水族馆 | Build a 3D Aquarium Using WebGL
item.description:
这个数字“Equarium”使用 Unity、 echo3D API 和 WebGL 在运行时加载它的装饰物和鱼。 可以通过上传到您的 echo3D 项目来添加新的小动物，而不会增加应用程序的占用空间。 完整的演示也可以在 echo3D 的GitHub 上找到。

因为鱼是随机选择的，缩放和纹理没有两个水族馆会完全相同！

登记

没有 API 密钥？ 确保在echo3D免费注册

设置

需要两个项目，因此在通过控制台登录后创建一个额外的项目（一个项目在注册时创建）。

  1. 从项目根目录打开 Models/Critters 文件夹并将所有文件上传到一个项目。 这将是您的“鱼”项目。
  2. 从模型/装饰中，将所有文件上传到您的其他项目。 这将是您的“静态模型”项目
  1. 在位于 Assets/Scripts/ 的Globals.cs文件中输入每个项目的 API 和安全密钥
  2. 输入锤头鲨和所有静态模型的条目 ID。 您可以通过 echo3D Web 控制台找到 entryID。

完成设置后，使用 Unity 打开项目。 按照我们文档页面上的说明在 Unity 中设置您的 API 密钥。 运行编辑器或构建应用程序（WebGL 是此应用程序唯一经过测试的构建目标！）。 在您与窗口交互之前，不会在浏览器中播放声音。 用“P”增大音乐音量，用“L”减小音乐音量。 重新加载页面/重新运行编辑器应用程序以加载不同的鱼并尝试查看它们！

扫描此二维码或点击此链接在 AR 中查看！

笔记

由于浏览器限制，通过此应用程序加载的模型不会响应通过 echo3D 平台（缩放、旋转等）所做的元数据更改。 刷新应用程序将重新获取具有反映更改的模型。

学分

此演示实现了以下 Unity Asset Store 包中的功能或资源：

  • 非常感谢 mdsn 和他们出色的水族馆咯咯声！

    学到更多

    请参阅我们的文档以了解有关如何使用 Unity 和 echo3D 的更多信息。

    支持

    请随时通过support@echo3D.co与我们联系，或加入我们在 Slack 上的社区频道。

    更多教程

    要了解如何制作其他类似项目，请尝试以下方法之一：

    echo3D ( www.echo3D.co ; Techstars 19') is a cloud platform for 3D/AR/VR that provides tools and network infrastructure to help developers & companies quickly build and deploy 3D apps, games, and content. echo3D ( www.echo3D.co ; Techstars 19') 是一个用于 3D/AR/VR 的云平台，它提供工具和网络基础设施来帮助开发人员和公司快速构建和部署 3D 应用程序、游戏和内容。



原文:

This digital "Equarium" loads its decorations and fish at runtime using Unity, the echo3D API and WebGL. The echo3D platform allows the app to select and display multitudes of different fish types without requiring any asset bundling or inclusion in the app build itself. New critters can be added by uploading to your echo3D project without increasing the app footprint. The full demo can also be found on echo3D's GitHub.

Because fish are randomly selected, scaled and textured no two aquariums will ever be exactly the same!

Register

Don't have an API Key? Make sure to register for FREE at echo3D

Setup

Two projects are required so create one additional project after signing in via the console (one project is created upon sign up).

  1. From the project root, open the Models/Critters folder and upload all files to one project. This will be your 'fish' project.
  2. From Models/Decor, upload all files to your other project. This will be your 'Static models' Project
  1. Enter the API and security keys for each project within the Globals.cs file located in Assets/Scripts/
  2. Enter the entry IDs for the hammerhead shark and all static models. You can find the entryIDs via the echo3D web console.

After completing setup, open the project with Unity. Follow the instructions on our documentation page to set up your API key in Unity. Run the editor or build the app (WebGL is the only tested build target for this app!). Within a browser sounds will not play until you interact with the window. Increase the music volume with "P" and decrease with "L". Reload the page / re-run the editor app to load different fish and try to see them all!

Scan this QR code or click this link to see it in AR!

Notes

Due to browser limitations models loaded via this app will not respond to changes in metadata made via the echo3D platform (Scale, rotation etc.). Refreshing the app will re-fetch models with reflected changes.

Credits

This demo implements features or assets from the following Unity Asset Store packages:

  • Thalassophobia: Stylized Oceans
  • Everloop

A big thanks to mdsn and their terrific aquarium gurgle sound!

Learn More

Refer to our documentation to learn more about how to use Unity and echo3D.

Support

Feel free to reach out at support@echo3D.co or join our community channel on Slack.

More Tutorials

To learn how to make other similar projects, try one of these:

echo3D (www.echo3D.co; Techstars 19') is a cloud platform for 3D/AR/VR that provides tools and network infrastructure to help developers & companies quickly build and deploy 3D apps, games, and content.
item.author: _echo3d_

item.title: 仅 14 行 CSS 中的明暗模式 | Light and dark mode in just 14 lines of CSS
item.description:
我喜欢暗模式所有的东西。 但我也知道很多人喜欢灯光模式！ 为了尊重个人偏好并从一开始就考虑可访问性，我在开始一个新的 Web 项目时添加了对原生明暗模式的支持。

该解决方案不使用 JavaScript ，因此我们没有构建明暗模式切换。 相反，它使用 CSS 媒体查询检测用户的系统设置，并使用两个自定义 CSS 属性来确定基本配色方案。 这是它是如何完成的。

声明 2 个 CSS 自定义属性

您可以在 CSS 文件中的任何位置定义 CSS 自定义属性，它们遵循与其他 CSS 规则相同的级联和特异性模式。 例如，您可以在文档根目录定义 CSS 变量，并在更具体的 CSS 类中覆盖它们。 很棒的是，您还可以在浏览器开发工具中检查和调试声明的 CSS 变量，这些变量显示在样式表规则下方。

您还可以将第二个参数传递给var()函数，如果您尝试使用它时自定义属性不存在，该参数将充当备用值。

 .element { color : var ( --my-new-color , #ff0000 ); }

对于这种明暗模式解决方案，在文档根处定义两个颜色变量——一个用于前景色，一个用于背景色。 我倾向于默认选择深色模式，因此我将背景颜色设置为黑色（ --color-bg ），将前景色设置为白色（ --color-fg ）。

 :root { --color-bg : #000000 ; --color-fg : #ffffff ; }

使用 prefers-color-scheme 媒体查询

接下来，我们将使用prefers-color-scheme CSS 媒体查询挂钩系统设置，并翻转背景和前景色的变量声明。 当检测到浅色主题设置时，以下代码将--color-bg设置为白色，将--color-fg设置为黑色。

 @media ( prefers-color-scheme : light ) { :root { --color-bg : #ffffff ; --color-fg : #000000 ; } }

添加身体样式

最后，使用 CSS 自定义属性，在 HTML body元素上设置background-color （用于页面颜色）和color （用于文本），如果没有被覆盖，所有子元素都将继承它们。

 body { background-color : var ( --color-bg ); color : var ( --color-fg ); }

就是这样——只用 14 行 CSS 就支持原生的明暗模式偏好！

在 CodePen 上查看代码

这是CodePen 的完整示例，它将根据您的系统偏好显示亮或暗模式。 切换您的系统设置以观看 CodePen 切换主题！

你如何实现暗模式和亮模式？ 在推特上告诉我。



原文:

I like to dark mode all the things. But I also know lots of people who prefer light mode! To respect personal preferences and consider accessibility from the start, I add support for native light and dark mode as soon as I begin a new web project.

This solution uses no JavaScript, so we're not building a light and dark mode toggle. Instead, it detects the user's system settings with a CSS media query, and uses two custom CSS properties to determine a basic colour scheme. Here's how it's done.

Declare 2 CSS custom properties

CSS custom properties are also referred to as CSS variables or cascading variables. You can define CSS custom properties anywhere in your CSS files, and they follow the same cascading and specificity patterns as other CSS rules. For example, you can define CSS variables at the document root, and override them in more specific CSS classes. What's great is you can also inspect and debug declared CSS variables in browser dev tools, which appear below the stylesheet rules.

CSS custom properties are declared by words prefixed with two dashes (--), and accessed using the var() function.

:root { --my-color-variable: #000000;
} .element { /* This is calculated as #000000! */ color: var(--my-color-variable);
}

You can also pass a second parameter into the var() function, which acts as a fallback value if the custom property doesn't exist when you try to use it.

.element { color: var(--my-new-color, #ff0000);
}

For this light/dark mode solution, define two colour variables at the document root — one for the foreground colour, and one for the background colour. I tend to choose dark mode by default, so I set the background colour to black (--color-bg) and the foreground colour to white (--color-fg).

:root { --color-bg: #000000; --color-fg: #ffffff;
}

Use the prefers-color-scheme media query

Next, we're going to hook into system settings using the prefers-color-scheme CSS media query and flip the variable declarations for the background and foreground colours. The following code sets the --color-bg to white and the --color-fg to black when a light theme setting is detected.

@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { :root { --color-bg: #ffffff; --color-fg: #000000; }
}

Add body styles

Finally, using the CSS custom properties, set the background-color (for the page colour) and color (for the text) on the HTML body element, which all child elements will inherit if they're not overwritten.

body { background-color: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-fg);
}

And that's it — support for native light and dark mode preferences in just 14 lines of CSS!

View the code on CodePen

Here's the full example on CodePen, which will display light or dark mode depending on your system preferences. Toggle your system settings to watch the CodePen switch themes!

How do you implement dark mode and light mode? Let me know on Twitter.
item.author: whitep4nth3r

item.title: 你能在合并冲突发生之前解决它们吗？ 💥 💥 | Can You Resolve Merge Conflicts Before They Happen? 💥
item.description:
解决合并冲突很有趣！ 没有人喜欢他们，但这并不能改变他们是工作的一部分的事实。 在这篇简短的文章中，我想简要解释它们的来源，展示如何解决它们，并作为额外的奖励，提出一个可能首先避免它们的解决方案！

合并冲突——它们是什么？

合并冲突是当 Git 无法自动解决两次提交之间的代码差异时发生的事件。 它们可能是由于合并分支、rebase 期间或您在 Git 中挑选樱桃时发生的。

好消息是 Git 非常擅长弄清楚如何集成更改，所以大多数时候你是安全的。 当完全相同的行被修改并且 Git 无法确定哪个版本是正确的时，困难的部分就开始了。 它会在终端通知您并让您负责修复它。

以正常方式解决合并冲突

好的，那么我们如何解决冲突呢？ 我们可以手动解决它们，也可以使用 Git GUI，例如内置于 VS Code 或 Jetbrains IDE 中的 GUI，或单独的桌面应用程序，如Tower或Sourcetree 。

让我们看看手动解决冲突。

冲突的更改很容易发现，因为它们将在文件中由<<<<<<<和>>>>>>>标记。 删除不需要的行（包括冲突标记）并保留正确的行。

早期合并冲突检测

合并冲突并不像看起来那么可怕，但事实是解决它们可能是一个漫长而乏味的过程，尤其是在大型项目中。

这就是 GitLive 派上用场的地方 - 一个强大的工具，当您在编辑器中进行与任何其他分支上的更改发生冲突的更改时，它会在您的 IDE 中警告您。 警告向您显示潜在的冲突，并让您有机会在合并冲突发生之前解决它们。

从 GitLive 开始

安装最新的 GitLive VS Code 扩展或JetBrains 插件以开始使用。

当您打开文件时，您会在编辑器的装订线中看到更改指示器，显示您的队友在哪里进行了与您的文件版本相比的更改。 当您和您的队友进行编辑时，这些内容会实时更新。

如果您进行了冲突更改，您将看到亮红色的冲突指示器。 这些冲突可能是您尚未推送的未提交的本地更改，也可能是您的分支上与您的队友的更改发生冲突的现有更改。

在 JetBrains 中，单击作者的头像（或者您可以在 VS Code 中滚动受影响的行）来检查更改并查看差异、它们来自哪个分支，甚至可以直接在本地文件中挑选更改。

就是这样，希望您会发现我的关于如何处理合并冲突的迷你指南很有用，并且下次遇到合并冲突时您不会惊慌。 让我知道您在评论中的想法！



原文:

Solving merge conflicts is fun! - said no one ever. Nobody likes them, but it doesn’t change the fact that they are, well, part of the job. In this short post, I would like to briefly explain where they come from, show how to solve them and as an added bonus, suggest a solution to potentially avoid them in the first place!

Merge conflicts - what are they?

A merge conflict is an event that occurs when Git is unable to automatically resolve differences in code between two commits. They can happen as a result of merging branches, during a rebase or when you’re cherry-picking in Git.

The good news is that Git is quite good at figuring out how to integrate changes, so most of the time you’re safe. The hard part begins when the exact same line has been modified and Git can’t decide which version is correct. It will notify you about it in the terminal and hold you responsible for fixing it.

Solving merge conflicts the normal way

Ok, so how do we resolve conflicts? We can either solve them manually or use a Git GUI such as the ones built into VS Code or Jetbrains IDEs or a separate desktop application like Tower or Sourcetree.

Let’s look at solving conflicts manually.

The conflicting changes are easy to spot as they will be marked in a file by <<<<<<< and >>>>>>>. The part before ======= is your version of the conflicting lines and the part after is their version. Delete the lines you don’t want (including the conflict markers) and keep those that are correct.

Early merge conflict detection

Merging conflicts are not as scary as they seem, but the truth is that solving them can be a long and tedious process, especially on big projects.

That is where GitLive comes in handy - a powerful tool that will warn you inside your IDE the moment you make a change in the editor that conflicts with a change on any other branch. The warnings show you potential conflicts and give you the chance to solve them before the merge conflict occurs.

Starting with GitLive

Install the latest GitLive VS Code extension or JetBrains plugin to get started.

When you open a file you’ll see the change indicators in the gutter of your editor that shows you where your teammates have made changes compared to your version of the file. These update in real-time as you and your teammates are editing.

If you've made a conflicting change you will see the bright red conflict indicator. These conflicts can be uncommitted local changes you have not pushed yet or existing changes on your branch that conflict with your teammates’ changes.

In JetBrains click on the author’s avatar (or you can rollover the affected lines in VS Code) to inspect a change and see the diff, what branch they're from and even cherry-pick changes straight into your local file.

That’s it, hope you’ll find my mini-guide on how to deal with merge conflicts useful and next time you encounter a merge conflict you won’t panic. Let me know what you think in the comments!
item.author: benjamincohen123
