item.title: 有了这个建议，不要错过一次无故障的旅行 | Stop Missing Out On A Trouble-Free Trip With This Advice
item.description:
旅行是大多数人都想做的事情。 少数幸运者可以前往世界各地的异国情调的地方。 尝试节省一点钱并设定目标。 如果您打算去国内或国际的某个地方，这并不重要。 存钱罐是一个很好的开始方式。 备用零钱加起来很快！ 如果你没有多余的钱，不要担心。 在树林里远足或短途游览当地景点，就像去卢顿机场一样充实的迷你出租车

到陌生地区进行公路旅行时，请确保您的汽车得到保养并且油箱已加满油。 您需要做的最后一件事就是崩溃，不知道最近的加油站在哪里。 在您的汽油用完的情况下，在您的汽车后备箱中放置一个空汽油罐。 您将能够前往最近的服务站并给罐子加满油，而不是叫别人为您加油。

研究您要去的地区并采取预防措施保护自己免受犯罪侵害。 一些旅游区的盗窃率特别高。 在你去之前了解这些事情是非常重要的。 您通常可以采取一些简单的预防措施来减少自己的攻击目标。

计划一些小舒适，例如靠过道的座位、安静的房间或其他能让您旅行更好的小东西。 靠窗的座位可以看到风景，但靠过道的座位可以让您更自由地使用头顶行李箱、洗手间、引起服务人员的注意以及您一侧的空闲空间。

计划出国旅行时，请务必检查是否有任何必需或推荐的疫苗接种，并尽早与您的医生讨论接种疫苗。 如果您未能获得所需的镜头，您可能会在旅行时病得很重。 或者，即使您没有生病，您也可能难以重新进入该国，并可能被迫进入一段隔离期，以确保您没有携带任何疾病。

在选择旅行地点时，没有比同路人更好的信息来源了。 其他有类似需求和计划的旅行者可以告诉您哪些目的地是必游之地以及您应该尽量避开哪些区域。 没有任何指南可以取代另一个人或家人的第一手经验。

如果您要长时间旅行，例如五到七天或更长时间，请轻装上阵并计划洗衣服。 带上一点额外的钱，去自助洗衣店或干洗店。 您可以通过清洗和重新穿衣橱中的一些物品来为自己节省大量宝贵的行李箱空间。

在预订之前，请仔细查看所有旅行套餐优惠。 许多航空公司和网站将航班、酒店住宿、餐饮和活动整合在一起，并以一个价格提供。 这是为了让公司受益，因为他们可以在不提及他们的底线的情况下预订航班和房间。 其中一些套餐可能会被盗，但您应该始终查看每笔交易的成本，以确保您省钱。

在飞机上携带您自己的旅行物品。 这包括枕头、毯子、娱乐设备和耳机。 携带这些物品可以让您自给自足，而不是被迫依赖航空公司来满足您的需求。 还可以考虑自己带食物。 航空公司的食品价格可能是合理的，但他们没有很好的选择。

如果旅行包括跨越时区，则通常会出现时差反应。 虽然无法避免，但在飞行前几天​​多睡会有助于减轻影响。 您还应该尝试在飞行期间睡觉。

了解您的手机和计划在您旅行时的功能。 不要因为在拨打漫游电话之前没有与供应商核实而在不知情的情况下去度假并最终收到超过每分钟 5 美元的账单。 您最好使用酒店的固定电话拨打电话。

度假旅行时不要害怕在外面吃饭。 从酒店拿一张床单； 您可以将它用作野餐毯或桌布。 选择一个您一直想去的地方，然后从当地餐厅外卖。 与家人在美丽的地方享受悠闲的午餐或晚餐。

租车旅行时，请尝试从远离机场的地方租车。 很多时候，航空公司的汽车租赁包括税收和额外费用等项目，这些项目确实会抬高他们的价格。 通常机场外的租车会派专车来接你，这可能是更快更方便的minicabstratford

为您的旅行打包背包时，请尝试巧妙而安全地打包。 尝试将较轻的物品放在底部，将较重的物品放在顶部。 这将使您的背包在您的背部和肩膀上感觉更轻。 将您将使用或需要的东西放在顶部也是一个好主意。 脏衣服也可以很容易地放在底部。

如果带着婴儿旅行，您也可以使用他们的尿布袋作为随身行李。 它不仅适合您所有的婴儿用品，而且是收拾贵重物品的好方法。 这些类型的包通常不太可能成为盗窃的目标。 您还可以将垃圾袋装在这些袋子里，这些袋子非常适合小孩子使用，并且可以在您不在可以冲水的马桶时使用。

无论预算如何，旅行都很容易，而且适合所有人。 需要很少努力但令人满意的事情通常是最好的方式，旅行可以满足这个要求！ 因此，计划一次旅行，设定时间表并实现它。 你可以找到适合你的有趣的东西！



原文:

Traveling is something that most people would like to do. A lucky few get to travel to exotic places around the world. Try saving a little bit of money and set a goal. It doesn't matter if you plan on going somewhere domestic or international. A piggy bank is a great way to start. Spare change adds up fast! If you have no money to spare don't fret. A hike in the woods or a short trip to view local sites, can be just as fulfilling minicab to luton airport

When taking a road trip to an unfamiliar area, be sure that your car is serviced and you have a full tank of gas. The last thing you need is to break down, unaware of where the nearest gas station is. Keep an empty gas can in the trunk of your car in the event that you run out of gas. You will be able to make your way to the nearest service station and fill up the can, rather than calling someone out to do that for you.

Study up on the area you are going to and take precautions to protect yourself from crime. Some tourist areas have especially high theft rates. It is very important to know about these things before you go. There are often simple precautions you can take to make yourself less of a target.

Plan for such small comforts as an aisle seat, a quiet room or other little things that make you trip better. A window seat has a view, but a seat at the aisle allows you more freedom in accessing your overhead bags, restrooms, getting attention of the service staff, and free space on one side of you.

When planning a trip overseas, be sure to check on any required or recommended vaccinations and speak to your doctor early about getting them. If you fail to get the shots that are required, you could become very ill while traveling. Alternatively, even if you don't get sick, you may have difficulty re-entering the country and may be forced into a period of quarantine to ensure you aren't carrying any diseases.

When selecting a location to travel to, there is no better source of information than a fellow traveler. Other travelers with similar needs and plans, can tell you what destinations are must-sees and what areas you should try to avoid. No guidebook can replace the first-hand experience of another person or family.

If you will be traveling for an extended period of time, such as five to seven days or more, pack lightly and plan on doing laundry. Take a little extra money with you and visit a laundromat or a dry cleaner. You can save yourself a lot of precious suitcase space by washing and re-wearing some items of your wardrobe.

Carefully check out any travel package deals before you book them. Many airlines and websites put together flights, hotel accommodations, meals and activities and offer them at one price. This is designed to benefit the companies, because they can book flights and rooms without mentioning their bottom line. Some of these packages can be steals, but you should always look into the cost of each piece of the deal to make sure you are saving money.

Take your own travel items on the flight. This includes a pillow, blanket, entertainment device and headphones. Bringing these items allows you to be self-sufficient rather than being forced to rely on the airline for your needs. Also consider taking your own food. Airline food prices can be reasonable, but they don't have a very good selection.

Jet lag typically occurs if travel includes crossing time zones. While it cannot be avoided, getting extra sleep in the days leading up to the flight can help to lessen the effects. You should also try sleeping during the flight.

Educate yourself on how your cellphone and plan will function when you are traveling. Do not go on vacation unawares and end up with a bill that exceeds five dollars a minute because you did not check with your provider before you made roaming calls. You may be better off making calls from a land-line in your hotel.

Don't be afraid to eat outside while traveling on vacation. Grab a sheet from the hotel; you can use it as a picnic blanket or a table cloth. Pick a location that you have been wanting to visit and get take-out from a local restaurant. Enjoy having a leisurely lunch or dinner in a beautiful place with your family.

When renting a car for a trip, try renting one from a location away from the airport. Many times the car rentals at an airline include items like taxes and additional fees that can really jack up their prices. Usually the off-airport car rentals will send a shuttle to pick you up in, which may be faster and more convenient minicab stratford

When packing a backpack for your trip, try to pack it smartly and securely. Try placing lighter items at the bottom and the heavier ones at the top. This will cause your backpack to feel lighter on your back and shoulders. It is also a good idea to place things that you will use or need on the top. Dirty clothes can be easily placed on the bottom too.

If traveling with a baby, you can use their diaper bag as a carry-on too. It's not only great for all of your baby gear, but it is a great way to pack your valuables. These types of bags are generally less likely to be targeted for theft. You can also carry waste bags in these that are great for little ones and for use when you are not at a toilet that can flush.

Traveling can be easy and something that works for everybody, no matter what the budget looks like. Things that require little effort, yet are satisfying, are often the best way to go and traveling can fit that bill! So plan a trip, set a timeline and make it happen. You can find something enjoyable to suit you!
item.author: topcarsminicab

item.title: 使用 Closure 实现 JS 缓存的简单方法。 (JS developer need to know) （JS开发者需要知道） | Simple way to Implement JS Cache using Closure. (JS developer need to know)
item.description:
暴风雨天赋

发表于在 React 中，有一些使用 React 钩子的缓存功能，例如 React.memo、useCallback、useMemo 等。 这次，我们尝试使用 JS(ES6+) 中的闭包来实现这些缓存操作。 闭包意味着函数被声明的词法上下文和它可以访问的变量。 

 function fnGenerator(str) { const strLog = 'The string I was given is "' + str + '"'; return function() { console.log(strLog); } } const fnRet = fnGenerator("BatMan"); fnRet(); // -> The string I was give is "BatMan"

为了参与这个理论，我们可以在 JS 中实现缓存功能。 这是我为斐波那契做的一个例子。

 const originalFibo = (n) => { let res = [1, 1]; if (res.length >= n ) return res; while(res.length <= n) res.push(res[res.length - 1] + res[res.length - 2]); return res; } const fbGenerator = (n) => { let res = [1, 1]; return function() { if (res.length >= n) return res; while(res.length <= n) res.push(res[res.length - 1] + res[res.length - 2]); return res; } } const fibo = fbGenerator(100); console.time('cache started'); for(let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) fibo(); console.timeEnd('cache ended'); console.time('original started'); for(let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) originalFibo(100); console.timeEnd('original ended');

您会注意到，与原始代码快照相比，此代码快照显着减少了运行时间。

阿纳布查特吉 - May 12 5月12日



原文:

StormyTalent

Posted on May 13

In React, there are some functionality for cache using React hooks such as React.memo, useCallback, useMemo and so on. In this time, we try to implement those cache action using closure in JS(ES6+). Closure means the lexical context a function was declared in and the variables it has access to.

function fnGenerator(str) {
  const strLog = 'The string I was given is "' + str + '"';
  return function() {
    console.log(strLog);
  }
}
const fnRet = fnGenerator("BatMan");
fnRet();  // -> The string I was give is "BatMan"

To engage this theory, we can implement Cache functionality in JS. Here is an example I did for fibonacci.

const originalFibo = (n) => {
  let res = [1, 1];
  if (res.length >= n )
    return res;
  while(res.length <= n)
    res.push(res[res.length - 1] + res[res.length - 2]);
  return res;  
}

const fbGenerator = (n) => {
  let res = [1, 1];
  return function() {
    if (res.length >= n)
     return res;
    while(res.length <= n)
       res.push(res[res.length - 1] + res[res.length - 2]);
    return res;
  }
}

const fibo = fbGenerator(100);
console.time('cache started');
for(let i = 0; i < 1000; i++)
  fibo();
console.timeEnd('cache ended');

console.time('original started');
for(let i = 0; i < 1000; i++)
  originalFibo(100);
console.timeEnd('original ended');

You will notice that this code snap dramatically decreases the runtime compared to original codesnap.

Arnab Chatterjee - May 12
item.author: stormytalent

item.title: JS(ES6+) 开发者必须知道的 50 条提示（第一部分） | 50+ hints JS(ES6+) developer must know (first part)
item.description:
暴风雨天赋

发表于如果你认为自己是一名 JS 工程师，我建议你至少读一遍这篇文章。

使用文字语法创建对象

// bad const item = new Object(); // good const item = {};

在创建具有动态属性名称的对象时使用计算的属性名称。

 function getKey(k) { return `a key named ${k}`; } // bad const obj = { id: 5, name: 'San Francisco', }; obj[getKey('enabled')] = true; // good const obj = { id: 5, name: 'San Francisco', };

使用对象方法的简写。

 // bad const atom = { value: 1, addValue: function (value) { return atom.value + value; }, }; // good const atom = { value: 1, addValue(value) { return atom.value + value; }, };

使用属性值简写

const lukeSkywalker = 'Luke Skywalker'; // bad const obj = { lukeSkywalker: lukeSkywalker, }; // good const obj = { lukeSkywalker, };

在对象声明的开头对您的速记属性进行分组。

 const anakinSkywalker = 'Anakin Skywalker'; const lukeSkywalker = 'Luke Skywalker'; // bad const obj = { episodeOne: 1, twoJediWalkIntoACantina: 2, lukeSkywalker, episodeThree: 3, mayTheFourth: 4, anakinSkywalker, }; // good const obj = { lukeSkywalker, anakinSkywalker, episodeOne: 1, twoJediWalkIntoACantina: 2, episodeThree: 3, mayTheFourth: 4, };

仅引用无效标识符的属性。

 // bad const bad = { 'foo': 3, 'bar': 4, 'data-blah': 5, }; // good const good = { foo: 3, bar: 4, 'data-blah': 5, };

不要直接调用 Object.prototype 方法。

 // bad console.log(object.hasOwnProperty(key)); // good console.log(Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(object, key)); // best const has = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty; // cache the lookup once, in module scope. console.log(has.call(object, key)); /* or */ import has from 'has'; // https://www.npmjs.com/package/has console.log(has(object, key));

优先使用对象扩展语法而不是 Object.assign 到浅拷贝对象。 使用 object rest 参数语法获取省略某些属性的新对象。

删除副本.a; 谢谢你的时间。
下一次，我将发布 Array 在 ES6+ 中的重要用法



原文:

StormyTalent

Posted on May 13

If you think you are a JS engineer, I recommend you to read this at least one time.

1. REFERENCE

// bad
var a = 1;
var b = 2;
// good
const a = 1;
const b = 2;

// bad
var count = 1;
if (true) {
  count += 1;
}
// good, use the let
let count = 1;
if (true) {
  count += 1;
}

2. Objects

Use the literal syntax for object creation

// bad
const item = new Object();
// good
const item = {};

Use computed property names when creating objects with dynamic property names.

function getKey(k) {
  return `a key named ${k}`;
}

// bad
const obj = {
  id: 5,
  name: 'San Francisco',
};
obj[getKey('enabled')] = true;

// good
const obj = {
  id: 5,
  name: 'San Francisco',

};

Use object method shorthand.

// bad
const atom = {
  value: 1,

  addValue: function (value) {
    return atom.value + value;
  },
};

// good
const atom = {
  value: 1,

  addValue(value) {
    return atom.value + value;
  },
};

Use property value shorthand

const lukeSkywalker = 'Luke Skywalker';

// bad
const obj = {
  lukeSkywalker: lukeSkywalker,
};

// good
const obj = {
  lukeSkywalker,
};

Group your shorthand properties at the beginning of your object declaration.

const anakinSkywalker = 'Anakin Skywalker';
const lukeSkywalker = 'Luke Skywalker';

// bad
const obj = {
  episodeOne: 1,
  twoJediWalkIntoACantina: 2,
  lukeSkywalker,
  episodeThree: 3,
  mayTheFourth: 4,
  anakinSkywalker,
};

// good
const obj = {
  lukeSkywalker,
  anakinSkywalker,
  episodeOne: 1,
  twoJediWalkIntoACantina: 2,
  episodeThree: 3,
  mayTheFourth: 4,
};

Only quote properties that are invalid identifiers.

// bad
const bad = {
  'foo': 3,
  'bar': 4,
  'data-blah': 5,
};

// good
const good = {
  foo: 3,
  bar: 4,
  'data-blah': 5,
};

Do not call Object.prototype methods directly.

// bad
console.log(object.hasOwnProperty(key));

// good
console.log(Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(object, key));

// best
const has = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty; // cache the lookup once, in module scope.
console.log(has.call(object, key));
/* or */
import has from 'has'; // https://www.npmjs.com/package/has
console.log(has(object, key));

Prefer the object spread syntax over Object.assign to shallow-copy objects. Use the object rest parameter syntax to get a new object with certain properties omitted.

// very bad const original = { a: 1, b: 2 };

const copy = Object.assign(original, { c: 3 }); // this mutates original ಠ_ಠ

delete copy.a; // so does this

// bad
const original = { a: 1, b: 2 };
const copy = Object.assign({}, original, { c: 3 }); // copy => { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 }

// good
const original = { a: 1, b: 2 };
const copy = { ...original, c: 3 }; // copy => { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 }

const { a, ...noA } = copy; // noA => { b: 2, c: 3 }

Thanks for your time.
Next time, I will post about the important usage of Array in ES6+
item.author: stormytalent

item.title: 使用 WSL 在 Windows 上为 Laravel 设置 LEMP | Set Up LEMP for Laravel on Windows using WSL
item.description:
唐卡达沃纳

发表于一、简介
二、 安装 WSL Ubuntu
三、 安装 Nginx
四。 安装 MySQL
五、安装PHP
六、 创建一个 Laravel 应用程序
七。 管理多个 Laravel 应用程序和 PHP 版本
八。 设置 Windows 终端
九。 附加功能

一、简介

最后，我们可以使用 Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) 在我们的 Windows 机器上设置一个真正的 Linux、Nginx、MySQL 和 Php (LEMP) 环境。

如果您是 Linux 或 Mac 用户，并且由于公司规定而像我一样被迫使用 Windows 机器，或者，如果您只是想在 Windows PC 上开发 Web 应用程序同时仍在 Linux 或类 Unix 上没有双启动的环境，使用 WSL 设置 LEMP。

为什么不直接使用XAMPP、WAMP 或 MAMP ？ 好吧，这些都是有用的，直到它们不起作用。 它们非常笨重，它们无法使您的本地项目在诸如myproject.test 之类的域中看起来不错。

为什么不使用宅基地？ 好吧，我希望我做到了，直到我放弃尝试让它在我的 Windows PC 上运行。

为什么不使用Laragon ？ 我确实使用拉拉贡！ 我喜欢它。 它是轻量级的，可配置的，提供漂亮的本地域，例如myproject.test ，还支持使用Ngrok将您的本地项目共享到互联网。
但是，它仍然不是真正的 Unix/类 Unix。

二、 安装 WSL 和 Ubuntu

首先，按照微软官方指南在此处安装 WSL：
安装 WSL Ubuntu 后，在 Windows 终端中打开一个新的 Ubuntu 选项卡。

三、 安装 Nginx

四。 安装 MySQL

要安装 MySQL，请运行：

连接到 MySQL：

在 MySQL 中，为我们的示例 Laravel 应用程序创建一个新数据库：

五、安装PHP

在一个命令中安装 PHP 和推荐的扩展：

安装作曲家

要安装 Composer，请参阅如何在 Ubuntu 20.04 上安装和使用 Composer 。

六、 创建一个 Laravel 应用程序

首先，在我们的 home 中为我们的 Laravel 项目创建一个目录：

通过 Composer 创建一个新的 Laravel 应用程序：

我们的新 Laravel 应用程序现已准备就绪：
要查看我们正在运行的 Laravel 应用程序，请在本地机器的 Web 浏览器中访问http://127.0.0.1:8000 。

要设置 Laravel 应用程序的数据库，请更新环境配置文件，生成加密密钥，然后运行数据库迁移：

七。 管理多个 Laravel 应用程序和 PHP 版本

最终，您将需要使用多个 Laravel 应用程序，每个应用程序都可能需要其他 PHP 版本。 这是通过为每个 Laravel 应用程序添加虚拟主机配置来管理的。 然后，我们将每个域添加到 Windows 的host文件 ( C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\host )。 这样，我们将不再需要每次都为每个应用程序运行 $ php artisan serve ，并且我们将为每个应用程序拥有更多可读的域，例如laravel.local 、 myblog.test 、 todos.test等。

例如，我们将在http://laravel.local上创建我们的第一个名为“ laravel ”的 Laravel 应用程序。 然后我们将创建两个额外的 Laravel 应用程序，名为“ todo ”和“ blog ”。 每个应用程序都需要PHP 5.6和PHP 7.3 ，并且可以在浏览器中分别访问http://todo.local和http://blog.local 。 当然，您可以根据需要更改应用程序名称。

通过 Composer 在~/codes中创建待办事项和博客Laravel 应用程序，然后设置每个应用程序。 参见第六章。 创建一个 Laravel 应用程序：

见第六章。

进入你的 Nginx 的虚拟主机目录并为你的laravel 、 todo和blog Laravel 应用程序创建三个虚拟主机配置文件：

为每个文件保存以下推荐的 Nginx 配置，仅更新server_name和root变量：

 server { listen 80; # UPDATE BELOW: Use appropriate server_name for each Laravel application. server_name laravel.local; #server_name todo.local; #server_name blog.local; # UPDATE BELOW: Use appropriate root directory for each Laravel application. root /codes/joserizal/laravel/public; #root /codes/joserizal/todo/public; #root /codes/joserizal/blog/public; add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"; add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block"; add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"; index index.html index.htm index.php; charset utf-8; location / { try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string; } location = /favicon.ico { access_log off; log_not_found off; } location = /robots.txt { access_log off; log_not_found off; } error_page 404 /index.php; location ~ \.php$ { fastcgi_pass unix:/var/run/php/php7.4-fpm.sock; fastcgi_index index.php; fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $realpath_root$fastcgi_script_name; include fastcgi_params; } location ~ /\.(?!well-known).* { deny all; } }

要激活新的虚拟主机配置文件，请在sites-enabled中创建指向laravel 、 todo和blog的符号链接：

测试 Nginx 是否有错误，然后重新启动：

将三个新域添加到 Windows 的C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\host文件中：

 127.0.0.1 laravel.local 127.0.0.1 todo.local 127.0.0.1 blog.local

在浏览器中，我们可以访问我们新的 Laravel 应用程序：

八。 附加功能

在 Sublime Text 3/4 中打开一个 Laravel 应用程序源代码。

首先，将以下代码块添加到您的 Bash 配置文件（ ~/.bashrc 、 ~/.bash_profile或~/.zshrc ）：

 alias subl='"/mnt/c/Program Files/Sublime Text 3/subl.exe"'

重新加载 Bash 脚本后重新启动 Windows 终端：
转到 Laravel 应用程序并在 Sublime Text 3/4 中打开项目。
在一个命令中运行多个服务：

将以下代码块添加到您的 Bash 配置文件（ ~/.bashrc 、 ~/.bash_profile或~/.zshrc ）：

 # Function to start|stop|restart LEMP services in one command. # Example: $ sudo lemp start|stop|restart lemp() { echo "$1 Nginx, MySQL and PHP for Linux (LEMP)" sudo service nginx "$1" sudo service mysql "$1" sudo service php7.4-fpm "$1" echo "Done..." }

您现在可以在一个命令中启动、停止或重新启动 LEMP 服务：
要激活新的 Bash 配置，请在重新加载 Bash 脚本后重新启动 Windows 终端：
亚历克斯 M. Schapelle - Apr 23 4月23日



原文:

Don Cadavona

Posted on May 13

I. Introduction
II. Install WSL Ubuntu
III. Install Nginx
IV. Install MySQL
V. Install PHP
VI. Create a Laravel Application
VII. Manage Multiple Laravel Applications and PHP Versions
VIII. Set Up Windows Terminal
IX. Extras

I. Introduction

Finally, we can set up a true Linux, Nginx, MySQL and Php (LEMP) environment on our Windows machine using Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL).

If you're a Linux or Mac user and is forced to use a Windows machine, just like me, due to company provisions, or, if you simply want to develop web applications on your Windows PC while still on a Linux or Unix-like environment without dual-booting, set up LEMP using WSL.

Why not just use XAMPP, WAMP or MAMP? Well, these are functional until they aren't. They are very clunky, they can't make your local projects look good with domains like myproject.test.

Why not use Homestead? Well, I wished I did, until I gave up trying to make it work on my Windows PC.

Why not use Laragon? I do use Laragon! I love it. It's lightweight, configurable, gives beautiful local domains like myproject.test and also supports sharing your local project to the internet using Ngrok.
But, it is still not a true-Unix/Unix-like.

II. Install WSL and Ubuntu

First, follow the official Microsoft guide to install WSL here:
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install

Once WSL Ubuntu is installed, open a new Ubuntu tab in Windows Terminal.

$ cd ~
$ sudo apt update

III. Install Nginx

$ sudo apt install nginx

IV. Install MySQL

To install MySQL, run:

$ sudo apt install mysql-server

Connect to MySQL:

$ sudo mysql or

$ sudo mysql -u root -p

Inside MySQL, create a new database for our example Laravel application:

mysql > create database laravel;
mysql > exit;

V. Install PHP

Install PHP and the recommended extensions in one command:

$ sudo apt install php php-fpm php-mysql php-mbstring php-xml php-bcmatch php-zip php-curl

Install Composer

To install Composer, see How To Install and Use Composer on Ubuntu 20.04.

VI. Creating a Laravel Application

First, create a directory for our Laravel projects inside our home:

$ cd ~ <-- Go to our home directory.
$ mkdir codes <-- Make a new directory called "codes".
$ cd codes <-- Go to the new "codes" directory.

Create a new Laravel application via Composer:

$ composer create-project laravel/laravel
$ cd laravel

Our new Laravel application is now ready to be served:
$ php artisan serve

To see our running Laravel application, visit http://127.0.0.1:8000 in a web browser in our local machine.

To set up the Laravel application's database, update the environment configuration file, generate an encrytion key then run the database migration:

$ cp .env.example .env <-- Create a .env file based on .env.example.
$ vim .env <-- Open the .env and update the necessary configurations:

DB_NAME=laravel
DB_USERNAME=root
DB_PASSWORD=secret

$ php artisan key:generate
$ php artisan migrate

VII. Manage Multiple Laravel Applications and PHP Versions

Eventually, you will need to work with multiple Laravel applications, each may require other PHP versions. This is managed by adding virtual host configurations for each Laravel application. Then, we add the each domain to the Windows' host file (C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\host). This way, we will no longer need to run $ php artisan serve for each application every time, and, we will have more readable domains for each application, eg laravel.local, myblog.test, todos.test, etc.

As an example, we will make our first Laravel application called "laravel" accessible at http://laravel.local. We will then create two additional Laravel applications called "todo" and "blog". Each application requires PHP 5.6 and PHP 7.3 and can be visited in a browser at http://todo.local and http://blog.local respectively. Of course, you may change the application names as needed.

Create the todo and blog Laravel applications in ~/codes via Composer, then setup each application. Refer to Chapter VI. Create a Laravel Application:

$ composer create-project laravel/laravel todo
$ composer create-project laravel/laravel blog

... Setup each application. See Chapter VI.

Go to your Nginx' virtual hosts directory and create three virtual host configuration files for your laravel, todo and blog Laravel applications:

$ cd /etc/nginx/sites-available
$ sudo vim laravel.conf
$ sudo vim todo.conf
$ sudo vim blog.conf

Save the following recommended Nginx configuration for each files, updating only the server_name and root variables:

server {
    listen 80;

    # UPDATE BELOW: Use appropriate server_name for each Laravel application.
    server_name laravel.local;
    #server_name todo.local;
    #server_name blog.local;

    # UPDATE BELOW: Use appropriate root directory for each Laravel application.
    root /codes/joserizal/laravel/public;
    #root /codes/joserizal/todo/public;
    #root /codes/joserizal/blog/public;

    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN";
    add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block";
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff";

    index index.html index.htm index.php;

    charset utf-8;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;
    }

    location = /favicon.ico { access_log off; log_not_found off; }
    location = /robots.txt  { access_log off; log_not_found off; }

    error_page 404 /index.php;

    location ~ \.php$ {
        fastcgi_pass unix:/var/run/php/php7.4-fpm.sock;
        fastcgi_index index.php;
        fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $realpath_root$fastcgi_script_name;
        include fastcgi_params;
    }

    location ~ /\.(?!well-known).* {
        deny all;
    }
}

To activate the new virtual host configuration files, create symbolic links to laravel, todo and blog in sites-enabled:

$ sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/laravel.conf/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/todo.conf/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/blog.conf/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/

Test Nginx for errors then restart:

$ sudo nginx -t
$ sudo service nginx restart

Add the three new domains to Windows' C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\host file:

127.0.0.1 laravel.local
127.0.0.1 todo.local
127.0.0.1 blog.local

In a browser, we can visit our new Laravel applications:

http://laravel.local
http://todo.local
http://blog.local

VIII. Extras

Open a Laravel application source code in Sublime Text 3/4.

First, add the following block of code below to your Bash configuration file (~/.bashrc, ~/.bash_profile or ~/.zshrc):

alias subl='"/mnt/c/Program Files/Sublime Text 3/subl.exe"'

Restart Windows Terminal after reloading your Bash script:
$ source ~/.bashrc or
$ source ~/.bash_profile or
$ source ~/.zshrc

Go to a Laravel application and open the project in Sublime Text 3/4.
$ cd ~/codes/todo
$ subl .

Run multiple services in one command:

Add the following block of code below to your Bash configuration file (~/.bashrc, ~/.bash_profile or ~/.zshrc):

# Function to start|stop|restart LEMP services in one command.
# Example: $ sudo lemp start|stop|restart
lemp() {
    echo "$1 Nginx, MySQL and PHP for Linux (LEMP)"
    sudo service nginx "$1"
    sudo service mysql "$1"
    sudo service php7.4-fpm "$1"
    echo "Done..."
}

You may now start, stop or restart your LEMP services in one command:
$ sudo lemp start
$ sudo lemp stop
$ sudo lemp restart

To activate the new Bash configuration, restart Windows Terminal after reloading your Bash script:
$ source ~/.bashrc or
$ source ~/.bash_profile or
$ source ~/.zshrc

Alex M. Schapelle - Apr 23
item.author: doncadavona

item.title: 介绍 Tailwinder - Tailwind 粉丝的家 - 由 Appwrite 提供支持 🚀 | Introducing Tailwinder - Home for Tailwind Fans - Powered with Appwrite 🚀
item.description:
我想首先说复杂的后端开发不再是痛苦！ 作为一名 Laravel 开发人员，我真的不知道 Appwrite 可以完成所有这些令人惊奇的事情并为我节省数小时的编码时间。

当这个黑客马拉松宣布时，我不知道什么是Appwrite或如何使用它。 在阅读了文档并在帮助线程、Twitter 和任何我能找到信息的地方提出了许多问题之后，我很清楚我可以使用这项伟大的技术做出一些独特的事情。

我不想重新创建已经存在的东西或克隆任何东西。 我真正想做的是考虑一个复杂的结构化网站，它可以解决问题或为社区增加价值。 自从我开始编码以来，我最喜欢的事情之一就是我的 CSS 工作的TailwindCSS 。 它使我们的生活变得更加轻松，即使对于后端开发人员来说也非常简单。 所以我想为什么不为 Tailwind 用户创建一个 COMPLETE 社区，让他们可以创建、分享、学习、评论和更新。 使用 appwrite 的Web SDK和Node SDK ，这成为可能。

从此 👆 我创建了Tailwinder 🚀：

起初，我只是想创建 TailwindCSS 在线工具。 但是在发现 Appwrite 一段时间后，我发现它可能比我对这个项目的想法要多得多。 它有动态的 URL、图片、按钮和动态的一切！ 它拥有您通过添加自己的内容来获得灵感或启发他人所需的一切。

让我们看看 Tailwinder 的功能以及我如何利用 Appwrite 创建完美的社区后端。

如果您有兴趣观看该项目的快速演示视频，我有您需要的东西👉 就在这里👈我试图尽可能快地制作它，但该项目有很多细节。

动态头像（Appwrite 功能）

导航栏中的个人资料图片是动态的，Appwrite 为该特定用户获取图片并将其显示在整个网站的导航栏中。
它还检测用户是否没有个人资料图片并显示带有用户名和姓的第一个字母的头像。 当我们进入 Userprofiles 部分时，我们将看到这一点。

从 Appwrite 使用：
获取文件预览Web SDK。
获取用户姓名缩写Web SDK。

  • 动态暗/亮主题切换（Appwrite 功能）

暗/亮主题切换不只是在一般情况下更改主题，它使用 Appwrite 将用户的选择注册到用户偏好中，以便在此特定用户登录时自动更改主题🌞🌙。

从 Appwrite 使用：
更新帐户首选项Web SDK。
获取 Account Prefrences Web SDK。

导航栏在向下滚动时淡出，但在向上滚动时再次出现。 它包括徽标、重要链接和用户下拉列表。

  • 酷 Tailwinder 徽标视图

这是 Claire Larsen 于 2015 年 10 月 21 日创建的开源组件， codepen可用。 我添加了一些调整以使其成为 VueJS 组件并更改一些内容以适应主题。

动态用户 URL （Appwrite 功能）

用户可以插入用户名，该用户名将用于为此特定用户创建动态唯一 URL。 该代码捕获用户名并将其保存为用户首选项，然后获取 URL 的用户名。

  • 用户登录/注销（Appwrite 功能）

使用 appwrite 登录和注销用户的简单身份验证。

从 Appwrite 使用：
创建帐户Web SDK。
创建帐户会话Web SDK。
更新帐户首选项Web SDK。
获取帐户Web SDK。
删除帐户会话Web SDK。

动态头像（Appwrite 功能）

再次，用户配置文件是动态的。 由 Appwrite 为该特定用户获取，与用户下拉导航栏中的个人资料图片相同。 它检测用户是否没有添加图片，然后添加头像。

从 Appwrite 使用：
获取文件预览Web SDK。
获取用户姓名缩写Web SDK。
更新帐户首选项Web SDK。

  • 动态用户信息（Appwrite 功能）

如您所见，每个用户都可以更新他们的信息，并使用 Appwrite Node-SDK 显示在他们的个人资料中。 当然，我可以为它的显示方式提出一个更好的设计，但我认为这没关系。

从 Appwrite 使用：
获取用户首选项节点 SDK。

  • 用户信息更新（Appwrite 功能）

在上面的视频中，您可以注意到我正在更改用户的头像、姓名、电子邮件和首选项。

该代码检查用户是否已经有个人资料图片，如果没有，则显示头像。

用户可以：

  • 更新一张新的头像，代码检查是否已经有，如果是，则 Appwrite 删除包含头像的先前存储桶，创建新存储桶，创建新文件并显示它。 如果用户没有添加个人资料图片，它只是为用户更新它。
  • 可以更新他们的名字。
  • 用户可以更新电子邮件，如果用户对电子邮件输入进行了任何更改，将显示新的密码输入。 这是通过 Appwrite Node-SDK 完成的，因为它显示了已经存在的信息并允许用户更新它们。

    从 Appwrite 使用：
    列出用户节点 SDK。
    获取用户节点 SDK。
    删除桶节点 SDK。
    创建存储桶节点 SDK。
    创建文件Web SDK。
    列出文件节点 SDK。
    获取文件预览Web SDK。
    获取用户姓名缩写Web SDK。
    更新帐户名称Web SDK。
    更新帐户电子邮件Web SDK。
    更新帐户首选项Web SDK。
    获取用户首选项节点 SDK。

    • 动态用户贡献（Appwrite 功能）

    上面的 GIF 演示了 Appwrite 如何获取用户的贡献和类别，并使用指向实际贡献的动态 URL 显示它们。 代码获取所有集合和文件，并循环遍历它们，仅过滤用户创建的集合和文件，并提供动态 URL。

    从 Appwrite 使用：
    列出集合节点 SDK。
    列出文档Web SDK。

    动态组件获取（Appwrite 功能）

正如您在上面的 GIF 中看到的，appwrite 正在动态获取用户提交的所有组件，每个组件都有一个唯一标识的 URL，每个用户都有一个唯一的 URL，如前所述。 组件缩略图也被从他们的桶中获取并显示给用户。

组件类别也可以通过 appwrite 动态识别和显​​示。

组件提交者用户的个人资料图片也被动态获取。

从 Appwrite 使用：
列出集合节点 SDK。
列出文档Web SDK。
列出文件节点 SDK。
获取文件预览Web SDK。
获取用户姓名缩写Web SDK。
获取文件预览Web SDK。

  • 添加新组件（Appwrite 功能）

用户可以向社区添加新组件。 每个组件都有类别、示例图片、名称、描述和组件代码。

该代码正在捕获组件名称并将其转换为 slug 以用作组件的动态 URL。

然后 appwrite 使用集合名称、id、组件名称、用户名称、用户 ID、组件代码和描述创建新文档。

代码自动动态地将用户重定向到提交的组件，并显示一条成功消息。

从 Appwrite 使用：
创建存储桶节点 SDK。
创建文件Web SDK。
创建文档Web SDK。

  • 组件视图（Appwrite 功能）

如上所述，我还为组件 URL 以及用户创建了一个唯一的 slug。 我还使用 Swiper JS和 Appwrite 来显示组件示例图片。

用户可以查看组件示例图片、名称、描述、组件提交者的详细信息，还可以复制代码并在TailwindCSS Playground中进行测试。

评论也可以在表格上找到，并将显示给所有访问此特定组件的用户。 创建评论的用户也可以删除该组件。

从 Appwrite 使用：
获取文档Web SDK。
获取用户首选项节点 SDK。
列出文档Web SDK。
列出文件节点 SDK。
删除文档节点 SDK。
获取用户姓名缩写Web SDK。

动态资源获取（Appwrite 功能）

这个部分是我最喜欢的。 用户可以在这里与社区共享学习资源。 我的代码使用与Components几乎相同的结构，所以这里没有必要再重复一遍。

  • 向学院添加新资源（Appwrite 功能）
  • 查看资源（Appwrite 功能）

将Youtube API 与 Appwrite 结合起来非常有趣！ 用户可以添加新的学习资源，其他人可以查看它们、发表评论并学习新知识。 结构几乎相同，这里不再赘述。

这可以被视为 Tailwinders 的博客。 用户可以查看其他人提交的帖子或添加自己的帖子。

设计结构是相同的，但我通过允许用户用他们的声音写文章来添加Deepgram触摸。

提交类别：

链接到代码

其他资源/信息

演示视频：

我希望我没有在这里错过任何东西😅我真的为这个感到自豪，并且尽一切努力学习 Appwrite 绝对值得❤️ 感谢您查看此内容，请让我知道您的想法。



原文:

I would like to start this by saying that complex backend development is not a pain anymore! As a Laravel developer, I really had no idea that Appwrite could do all these amazing stuff and save hours of coding for me.

When this hackathon was announced, I didn't know what is Appwrite or how to use it. After reading the DOCs and asking many questions in the Help Thread , Twitter and anywhere I could find information, It became clear to me that I can make something unique using this magnificent technology.

I didn't want to recreate something that already exists or to make a clone of anything. What I really wanted to do is to think of a complex structured website that solves an issue or adding value to the community. And one the things I love the most since I started coding is TailwindCSS for my CSS work. It makes our lives much easier and it's pretty simple even for backend developers. So I thought why not creating a COMPLETE community for Tailwind users where they can create, share, learn, comment and update. Using appwrite's Web SDK and Node SDK, that became possible.

From that 👆 I created Tailwinder 🚀 :

At first, I just thought of creating TailwindCSS online tools. But after awhile of discovering Appwrite, I figured that it can be much more than what I have ever had in mind for this project. It has dynamic URLs, pictures, buttons and dynamic everything! 😍

Tailwinder is an open source community for creative Tailwind lovers. It has everything you need to get inspired or to inspire others by adding your own content.

Let's have a look at Tailwinder features and how I utilized Appwrite to create the perfect backend of the community.

If you are interested more to watch a quick demo video of the project, I have what you need 👉Right here👈 I tried to make it as quick as I could but the project has so many details.

1- Homepage:

  • Dynamic Profile Picture (Appwrite Feature)

The profile picture in the Navbar is dynamic, Appwrite fetches the picture for this specific user and display it in the Navbar across the website.
It also detects if the user doesn't have a profile picture and display an Avatar with the first letter of the user's first and last name. We will see that in action when we get to Userprofiles section.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Get File Preview Web SDK.
Get User Initials Web SDK.

  • Dynamic Dark/Bright Theme Toggle (Appwrite Feature)

The dark/bright theme toggle isn't just changing the theme in general, It registers the the user's choice to the user preferences with Appwrite to automatically change the theme when this specific user is logged in 🌞🌙.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Update Account Preferences Web SDK.
Get Account Prefrences Web SDK.

The Navbar fades out while scrolling down but appears again on scroll up. It includes the logo, important links and user dropdown.

  • Cool Tailwinder Logo view

This is an opensource component by Claire Larsen created in October 21, 2015 and the codepen is available. I have added few adjustments to make it a VueJS component and change few things to fit the theme.

2- Signup/Signin:

  • Dynamic User URL (Appwrite Feature)

The user is able to insert username which will be used to create a dynamic unique URL for this specific user. The code catches the username and saves it as a user preference and then fetches the username for the URL.

  • User Login/Logout (Appwrite Feature)

Simple auth to login and logout the user using appwrite.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Create Account Web SDK.
Create Account Session Web SDK.
Update Account Preferences Web SDK.
Get Account Web SDK.
Delete Account Session Web SDK.

3- User Profile:

  • Dynamic Profile Picture (Appwrite Feature)

The user profile again, is dynamic. Fetched by Appwrite for this specific user same as the profile picture in the user dropdown Navbar. It detects if the user didn't add a picture then it adds an Avatar instead.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Get File Preview Web SDK.
Get User Initials Web SDK.
Update Account Preferences Web SDK.

  • Dynamic User Information (Appwrite Feature)

As you can see, every user has the ability to update their info and it's being displayed in their profile with Appwrite Node-SDK. Of course I could come up for a better design for the way it's displayed but I think that's okay.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Get User Preferences Node SDK.

  • User Information Update (Appwrite Feature)

In the above video, you can notice that I'm changing the user's profile picture, name, email and preferences.

The code checks if the user already has a profile picture, if not it displays an avatar.

The User can:

  • update a new profile picture, the code checks if there is already one, if yes then Appwrite deletes the previous bucket that contains the profile picture, create new bucket, create new file and display it. If the user didn't add a profile picture, it just updates it for the user.
  • The can update their name.
  • The user can update email, if the user made any changes in the email input, a new input for password will be displayed. -The user can update prefrences.

This is being done with Appwrite Node-SDK as it displays the already existing information and allows the user to update them.

Utilized from Appwrite:
List Users Node SDK.
Get User Node SDK.
Delete Bucket Node SDK.
Create bucket Node SDK.
Create File Web SDK.
List Files Node SDK.
Get File Preview Web SDK.
Get User Initials Web SDK.
Update Account Name Web SDK.
Update Account Email Web SDK.
Update Account Preferences Web SDK.
Get User Preferences Node SDK.

  • Dynamic User Contributions (Appwrite Feature)

The above GIF demonstrates how Appwrite can fetch the user's contributions and categories and display them with dynamic URL that leads to the actual contribution. The code gets all collections and files and loop over them filtering only the ones created by the user and provides a dynamic URL.

Utilized from Appwrite:
List Collections Node SDK.
List Documents Web SDK.

4- Components:

  • Dynamic Components Fetching (Appwrite Feature)

As you can see in the above GIF, appwrite is dynamically fetching all submitted components by users, and every component has a unique identified URL and every user has a unique URL as explained before. Also component thumbnails are being fetched from their buckets and displayed to the user.

Components category are being identified and displayed as well dynamically with appwrite.

Components submitters user's profile picture is being dynamically fetched as well.

Utilized from Appwrite:
List Collections Node SDK.
List Documents Web SDK.
List Files Node SDK.
Get File Preview Web SDK.
Get User Initials Web SDK.
Get File Preview Web SDK.

  • Add New Component (Appwrite Feature)

The user is able to add new components to the community. Every component has category, sample pictures, name, description and component code.

The code is catching the component name and transform it into a slug to be used as a dynamic URL for the component.

Appwrite creates new bucket and then add the pictures to this specific bucket with ID same as the component slug.

Then appwrite creates new document with collection name, id, name of component, name of user, id of user, component code and description.

The code automatically and dynamically redirects the user to the submitted component and shows a flash message of success.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Create bucket Node SDK.
Create File Web SDK.
Create Document Web SDK.

  • Component View (Appwrite Feature)

As explained above, I have created a unique slug for the component URL along with the users as well. Appwrite fetches the component details and dynamically inject them in the URL. Vue Router is being used for this piece of action.

I also used Swiper JS with Appwrite to display the component sample pictures.

The user is able to view the component sample pictures, name, description, component submitter's details and also is able to copy the code and test it in TailwindCSS Playground.

Comments are available on the table too, and will be displayed to all users who visits this specific component. The user who created the comment is able to delete the component as well.

Utilized from Appwrite:
Get Document Web SDK.
Get User Preferences Node SDK.
List Documents Web SDK.
List Files Node SDK.
Delete Document Node SDK.
Get User Initials Web SDK.

5- Tailwinder Academy:

  • Dynamic Resources Fetching (Appwrite Feature)

This section is my favorite. It's where users are able to share learning resources with the community. My code uses almost the same structure as Components ,So there is no point of repeating the same here again.

  • Add New Resources to The Academy (Appwrite Feature)
  • View Resources(Appwrite Feature)

Combining Youtube API with Appwrite turned out to be super fun! The user is able to add new learning resources and others can view them, leave a comment and learn something new. The structure is almost the same so there is no need to repeat it here.

6- Tailwinder Community:

This can be considered as a blog for Tailwinders. The user is able to view posts submitted by others or add his own.

The design structure is the same but I added Deepgram touch to it by allowing the user to write articles with their voice.

Submission Category:

Web2 Wizards

Link to Code

Additional Resources / Info

Demo Video:

I hope I didn't miss anything here 😅 I'm really proud of this one and every effort was put to learn Appwrite was absolutely worth it ❤️ Thank you for checking this out and please let me know what you think.
item.author: moose_said

item.title: 处理 JavaScript 中的错误 | Handling Errors In JavaScript
item.description:
费索比奥米米

发表于错误处理是您编写的任何代码的重要组成部分。 例如，如果您正在开发一个供其他开发人员使用的库，他们不想处理可能出现的错误。 这些错误要么导致库崩溃，要么需要他们付出额外的努力才能解决。
首先在编写软件之前计划好如何处理错误是一个好主意，但更重要的是在编写软件后实际跟进并有效处理错误。 在这篇文章中，我们将检查一个（人为的）假设场景，并研究我们可以在 JavaScript 中处理错误的方法。

错误语法

发生异常时，浏览器会停止执行您的脚本并显示错误消息。 一般来说，JavaScript 错误语法如下所示：

 try { code block to try } catch ( err ) { code to handle the catched error } finally { Block of code to be executed regardless of the try / catch resul t }

无论是否抛出异常，都会执行 finally 块。

如果您的程序中没有 catch 块，那么只要出现错误，程序就会终止并显示错误消息。

使用错误对象

错误对象是一个特殊的错误处理程序，用于处理 JavaScript 中的运行时错误。 此对象允许您识别发生的错误类型，以及它的来源和其他相关信息。
运行时错误可以是逻辑错误、未定义变量或函数或被零除。

考虑以下场景：如果您在软件中使用外部脚本，则需要在代码中使用 try 和 catch 来处理更新外部脚本时可能发生的任何错误，如下所示。

 try { username = doument . getElementById ( " username " ). value password = doument . getElementById ( " password " ). value if ( validate ( username , password ) == true ){ //login successfully } else { // username/password not correct } } catch ( e ) { console . error ( e . name + ' : ' + e . message ) alert ( " an error occur, we are working to fix it!!! " ) }

如果外部脚本中的函数 validate 被重命名或有错误，并且它没有在主脚本中更新，它将为用户生成一条自定义消息。 而不是抛出错误并终止程序执行。

如果在外部脚本中修改了 Validate() 并且您没有

利用错误事件

错误事件是一个函数，可让您处理在程序中加载外部 HTML 文件时可能发生的错误。 它将处理程序附加到窗口对象的错误事件。 这样，只要外部资源失败，就可以通知脚本。 当资源加载失败时，错误事件用于处理 HTML 元素，例如 img、video、audio、object、frame 等。 每个元素都必须有 onerror 属性，其中包含一个处理错误的函数，如下所示。 

 < img src = " image.jpg " onerror = " error_function() " >

每当 image.jpg 加载失败时，会触发 onerror 属性中的函数，以便我们处理代码中的错误，如下所示：

 function error_function () { console . log ( " The image could not be loaded. " ); }

我们还可以使用 HTML DOM 将 onerror 事件分配给元素。 以视频元素为例，如果视频加载失败或 URL 无效，我们可以向用户显示自定义消息，如下所示：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <body> <p> Handling javascript errors </p> <img id= "myImg" src= "image.png" > <span id= "demo" ></span> <script> document . getElementById ( " myImg " ). onerror = function () { error_function ()}; function error_function () { document . getElementById ( " demo " ). innerHTML = " The image could not be loaded. " ; } </script> </body> </html>

创建自定义错误

您可以通过在 try 语句中使用 throw 状态来控制程序流程并生成自定义错误消息。 这使您有机会定义自己的错误类型。

当执行到达 throw 时，将抛出自定义异常消息。 考虑一个如何使用它的例子。

 function myFunction () { const message = document . getElementById ( " p01 " ); message . innerHTML = "" ; let x = document . getElementById ( " demo " ). value ; try { if ( x == "" ) throw " empty " ; if ( isNaN ( x )) throw " not a number " ; x = Number ( x ); if ( x < 5 ) throw " too low " ; if ( x > 10 ) throw " too high " ; } catch ( err ) { message . innerHTML = " Input is " + err ; } }

悉达多文卡特什—— Apr 29 4月29日



原文:

Fesobi omoyemi

Posted on May 12

Error handling is an important part of any code you write. For example, if you are developing a library to be used by other developers, they don't want to deal with errors that might crop up. Those errors would either cause the library to break or would require extra effort on their part to resolve.
It's a good idea to plan for how you will handle errors before writing your software in the first place, but it's even more important to actually follow through and handle errors effectively once the software has been written. In this post, we'll examine a (contrived) hypothetical scenario and look at ways that we can handle errors in JavaScript.

Error syntax

When an exception occurs, the browser stops executing your script and displays an error message. The try...catch statement catches and handles these exceptions so that they don't cause the browser to stop running your program.

In general JavaScript error syntax is as shown below:

try { code block to try
}
catch(err) { code to handle the catched error
}
finally { Block of code to be executed regardless of the try / catch result
}

The try block executes first and if an exception occurs or throws during the execution, the catch block is executed. The Finally block is executed whether an exception is thrown or not.

If there is no catch block in your program, then the program will terminate with an error message, whenever there is an error.

Using the Error Object

The Error Object is a special error handler that is used to handle runtime errors in JavaScript. This object allows you to identify the type of error that occurred, as well as its source and other information about it.
The runtime error can be a logic error, undefine variable or functions, or division by zero.

Consider the following scenario: if you're utilizing an external script in your software, you'll need to use try and catch in your code to handle any errors that may occur when the external script is updated, as shown below.

try { username = doument.getElementById("username").value password = doument.getElementById("password").value
if(validate(username, password)== true){ //login successfully 
}else{ // username/password not correct 
}
} catch (e) { console.error(e.name + ': ' + e.message) alert("an error occur, we are working to fix it!!!")
}

If the function validate in the external script is renamed or has an error, and it was not updated in the main script, it will generate a custom message for the user. Instead of throwing errors and terminating the program execution.

If the Validate() is been modified in the external script and you have not

Utilize Error Events

Error Events is a function that lets you handle errors that may occur when loading external HTML files in your program. It attaches a handler to the window object's error event. That way the script can be informed whenever an external resource fails. Error events are used for handling HTML elements such as img, video, audio, object, frame, etc when their resource fails to load. Each element must have onerror attribute, containing a function to handle the error, as demonstrated below.

<img src="image.jpg" onerror="error_function()">

Whenever the image.jpg fails to load, the function inside the onerror attribute will be triggered for us to handle the error in our code as shown below:

function error_function() { console.log("The image could not be loaded.");
}

We can also use the HTML DOM to assign an onerror event to an element. let's take for instance, a video element, if a video fails to load or its URL is invalid, we can display a custom message to the users as shown below:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body> <p>Handling javascript errors</p> <img id="myImg" src="image.png"> <span id="demo"></span> <script>
document.getElementById("myImg").onerror = function() {error_function()}; function error_function() { document.getElementById("demo").innerHTML = "The image could not be loaded.";
}
</script> </body>
</html>

Create Custom Errors

Javascript allows developers to throw an error exception if certain conditions are not met or to throw an exception on a particular line.

You can control your program flow and generate custom error messages by using throw state inside try statement. This gives you the opportunity to define your own error types.

When the execution reaches throw, the custom exception message is thrown. Consider an example of how to use it.

function myFunction() { const message = document.getElementById("p01"); message.innerHTML = ""; let x = document.getElementById("demo").value; try { if(x == "") throw "empty"; if(isNaN(x)) throw "not a number"; x = Number(x); if(x < 5) throw "too low"; if(x > 10) throw "too high"; } catch(err) { message.innerHTML = "Input is " + err; }
}

Siddharth Venkatesh - Apr 29
item.author: omoyemi

item.title: 两个以上的边距可以折叠 | More than two margins can collapse
item.description:
顶级编码器

发表于保证金崩溃不仅限于两个保证金！ 在此示例中，4 个单独的边距占用相同的空间：

很难看出发生了什么，但这本质上是前面规则的组合：

  • 兄弟姐妹可以组合相邻的边距（如果第一个元素有 margin-bottom，而第二个元素有 margin-top）
  • 父母和孩子可以组合相同方向的边距每个兄弟姐妹都有一个贡献相同方向边距的孩子。

在这里，在代码中。 使用 devtools 单独查看每个边距：

我们的<header>和<section>之间的空间有 4 个独立的边距竞争占据该空间！

最终，段落的累积边距最大，所以它获胜，并且 40px 将header和section分开。

Juri Strumpflohner - Juri Strumpflohner - May 11 5月11日



原文:

TopCoder

Posted on May 13

Margin collapse isn't limited to just two margins! In this example, 4 separate margins occupy the same space:

It's hard to see what's going on, but this is essentially a combination of the previous rules:

  • Siblings can combine adjacent margins (if the first element has margin-bottom, and the second one has margin-top)
  • A parent and child can combine margins in the same direction Each sibling has a child that contributes a same-direction margin.

Here it is, in code. Use the devtools to view each margin in isolation:

The space between our <header> and <section> has 4 separate margins competing to occupy that space!

The header wants space below itself
The h1 in the header has bottom margin, which collapses with its parent
The section below the header wants space above itself
The p in the section has top margin, which collapses with its parent
Ultimately, the paragraph has the largest cumulative margin, so it wins, and 40px separates the header and section.

Juri Strumpflohner - May 11
item.author: topcode007

item.title: 边距可以朝同一个方向塌陷 | Margins can collapse in the same direction
item.description:
到目前为止，我们看到的所有示例都涉及相邻的相对边距：一个元素的底部与下一个元素的顶部重叠。

令人惊讶的是，即使在相同的方向上，边距也会崩溃。

这是代码中的样子：

 <style> .parent { margin-top: 72px; } .child { margin-top: 24px; } </style> <div class="parent"> <p class="child">Paragraph One</p> </div>

您可以将其视为先前规则的扩展。 子边距被“吸收”到父边距中。 两者正在结合，并且遵循我们迄今为止看到的相同的保证金崩溃规则（例如，最大的一方获胜）。

这可能会带来很大的惊喜。 例如，看看这个常见的挫败感：

在这种情况下，您可能希望两个部分相互接触，并在每个容器内应用边距：

这似乎是一个合理的假设，因为<section>根本没有边距！ 目的似乎是增加每个盒子顶部的空间，给段落一点喘息的空间。

问题是0px 边距仍然是可折叠的边距。 每个部分都有 0px 的上边距，它与段落的 32px 上边距相结合。 由于 32px 是两者中的较大者，因此它获胜。



原文:

So far, all the examples we've seen involve adjacent opposite margins: the bottom of one element overlaps with the top of the next element.

Surprisingly, margins can collapse even in the same direction.

Here's what this looks like in code:

<style>
  .parent {
    margin-top: 72px;
  }
  .child {
    margin-top: 24px;
  }
</style>
<div class="parent">
  <p class="child">Paragraph One</p>
</div>

You can think of this as an extension of the previous rule. The child margin is getting “absorbed” into the parent margin. The two are combining, and are subject to the same rules of margin-collapse we've seen so far (eg. the biggest one wins).

This can lead to big surprises. For example, check out this common frustration:

In this scenario, you might expect the two sections to be touching, with the margin applied inside each container:

This seems like a reasonable assumption, since the <section>s have no margin at all! The intention seems to be to increase the space within the top of each box, to give the paragraphs a bit of breathing room.

The trouble is that 0px margin is still a collapsible margin. Each section has 0px top margin, and it gets combined with the 32px top margin on the paragraph. Since 32px is the larger of the two, it wins.
item.author: topcode007

item.title: MySQL 万岁：好的旧 MySQL 应该重新焕发活力 | Long Live MySQL: Good Old MySQL Should Be Rejuvenated
item.description:
作者： Ed Huang （PingCAP 联合创始人兼 CTO）

这篇文章是我们系列的第一部分。 您可以在这里看到第二部分：* MySQL 万岁：向生态系统创新者致敬***。

想象一下，您是一名应用程序开发人员，您的经理告诉您在一周内制作一个新应用程序的原型。 你会为你的数据库选择什么？ 可能是MySQL。

许多工程师喜欢 MySQL。 但是，其他一些数据库（例如 SQLite 和 IBM Db2）的受欢迎程度正在下降。

根据 DB-Engines 的数据库流行度排名

在Stack Overflow 2021 年开发者调查的 73,317 份回复中，MySQL 被评为最受欢迎的数据库。

我使用最常用数据库的关键字在Indeed上搜索了职位，“mysql”返回了 31,245 个职位。 需要“mysql”技能的职位空缺在顶级数据库中排名第二。

事实上，在 PingCAP，MySQL 是我们的许多工程师在加入 PingCAP 之前就知道如何使用的少数数据库之一。 我就是其中之一。

我使用 MySQL 的 20 年

我第一次使用 MySQL 是在 2003 年我还是一名高中生的时候。当时我想用 PHP 为编程竞赛爱好者开发一个 Web 应用程序（Online Judge），并帮助他们运行他们的代码并检查它是否正确。 为了存储用户提交的数据，我需要一个数据库。 我可以使用 MySQL 客户端编写 SQL 并直接在 PHP 中操作数据库，然后 Apache 呈现动态页面结果。

我是为数不多的基础设施工程师之一。 公司业务增长如此之快，其数据量很快飙升至超过 MySQL 的单机容量。 于是我开始寻求解决方案。 例如，我将 MySQL binlog 用于高可用的主层和辅助层，并应用了水平分片。 当我离开那家公司时，它的数据库集群有 30 多个分片，可以容纳数十 TB。 对我来说，解决这些问题是一次非常充实的经历。

由于这些 MySQL 相关的经历，我有机会结识了许多优秀的工程师，他们在 MySQL 方面有着相似的回忆。 为什么这么多人喜欢 MySQL

它受欢迎的秘诀在于 MySQL 简单易用。 许多用户友好的特性，如 Binlog 也很早就被引入 MySQL。 因此，MySQL 用户的数量在接下来的三年中急剧增长。 此外，许多用户对 MySQL 的使用提出了反馈或直接为 MySQL 做出了贡献，所有这些都导致了 MySQL 生态系统的更加繁荣。 在鼎盛时期，MySQL 成为许多企业的自然选择，因为：

  • 许多工程师都在使用它，为数据库公司提供了一个庞大的人才库。
  • 它是开源的，易于获取和使用。
  • 您可以在互联网上找到几乎所有 MySQL 问题的答案。
  • 它有一个充满活力的生态系统。 大量的开源项目，例如 WordPress 和 phpMyAdmin，严重依赖 MySQL 并使其更加流行。

事情变了

在过去的 20 年里，世界瞬息万变，我们见证了许多重大的技术变革，例如移动互联网的爆炸式增长和当今的数字化转型。 我们的应用场景也发生了巨大变化。 这些变化是什么？ 让我们从数据的角度来探索它们。

数据量呈指数级增长，使得分布式技术成为几乎所有现代数据库的必需品。 它们提供透明的可扩展性，同时保持一致的使用，以便开发人员可以扩展和扩展而无需担心应用程序更改。 然而，NoSQL 数据库为用户提供的功能比 SQL 数据库更有限。 这在 Web 2.0 早期并不是什么大问题，因为虽然数据量增加了，但商业模式相对简单。 以 Twitter 为例：在其最早的版本中，它仅提供一个不超过 140 个字符的巨型共享笔记服务。

如果企业打算构建复杂的应用程序，尤其是那些需要强一致性和复杂关联分析的应用程序，例如 JOIN 和 GROUP BY，SQL 仍然是他们的最佳选择。 不幸的是，传统的关系型数据库，包括 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL，尽管采用了分布式技术，但在很长一段时间内几乎没有什么创新。 他们为单机开发数据库，​​使分片成为处理分布式需求的唯一主流解决方案。 然而，最近我们看到了一些新的数据库项目，可以称为真正的分布式 SQL 数据库。

起初，数据模型从复杂演变为简单，然后变得更加复杂。 这种演变伴随着移动互联网爆炸等技术的重大变化。 当时 NoSQL 和分片数据库的盛行使数据建模受制于一组有限的功能。 有一段时间，数据应用建模不得不简化为键值对、文档和其他灵活简单的模型。

然而，当低垂的果实被采摘时，真正棘手的问题变得更加棘手。 随着金融服务、电子商务等更多行业开始数字化转型以加速在线业务，数据库的应用场景变得更加复杂。 所以，关系模型加 SQL 几乎总是我们最好的数据库解决方案。 在过去的 10 年中，我们看到了许多使用新的领域特定语言 (DSL) 对数据进行建模的尝试——GraphQL 就是一个例子——但我认为它们都无法撼动关系模型和 SQL 组合的主导地位。 对更强大的一致性和实时事务处理和分析的需求一直在增加。 早期，MySQL 的默认存储引擎是 MyISAM，但最终 InnoDB 取而代之。 我认为主要区别之一是 InnoDB 支持强一致性事务。 数据库层提供的语义越一致，应用层必须承担的复杂性就越低。 在处理故障转移时尤其如此。 如果数据库不支持强一致性，那么灾难恢复时就很难保证数据的完整性。 这在大多数金融场景中是不可接受的。

在过去的十年中，数据库管理技术出现了许多创新。 然而，一个明显的权衡是在线事务处理 (OLTP) 和在线分析处理 (OLAP) 的分离。 虽然这种方法在许多情况下都有效，但引入的架构复杂性是一个难以破解的难题。 在这个令人头疼的技术堆栈上保持数据的一致性也很困难。 这给许多需要分钟甚至毫秒级别的实时洞察力的企业带来了巨大的挑战。 由于技术壁垒，公司可能不得不压制他们的业务需求并转向更传统但更容易驯服的解决方案。 近年来，随着数据量和实时分析需求的增加，OLTP和OLAP正在融合的轨道上。

数据的生成和消费方式正在变得多方面。 过去，写入数据库很简单：客户端通过 SQL 语句直接连接到数据库。 如果你仔细看看今天数据是如何进入数据库的，你会发现上游更加多样化； 它可能是来自 ETL 作业的消息队列或批量写入。 批量写入的挑战在于，用户通常更关心吞吐量性能，而不是是否满足 ACID 隔离要求。 在这种情况下，传统的事务机制（例如锁定）将是不必要的。 另一方面，实时性要求的急剧上升导致需要将数据变化同步到下游消息队列以供消费。 变更数据捕获 (CDC) 已成为现代数据库的主流实现。 比如 MySQL 有 Binlog，Oracle 有 GoldenGate，PG 有 pg 复制。

对数据质量的要求越来越高，可观察性的范围从执行面板扩展到数据面板。 随着数据量的急剧增加和分布式数据库的出现，我们对基本的系统信息并不满意； 我们想观察数据是如何被访问的，并从中获得业务洞察力。 以 TiDB 的 KeyVisualizer 为例。 实时反映数据访问的分布情况，让您一目了然地知道哪些数据被频繁访问，访问的特点是什么。

但是，这些可观察性维度仍然仅限于数据库系统本身。 最近，一个新趋势开始出现：数据可观察性，它试图衡量数据的质量。 硬件基础设施正在发生巨大变化。 随着云变得越来越流行，托管服务正在成为主流。 硬件的进步缓解了很多问题。 比如B+Tree解决了传统机械盘读写慢的问题； 但是，这不是现代 NVMe SSD 的问题。 随着现代云环境的广泛采用，云上的共享存储解决方案（例如 AWS EBS）的性能即使不是更好，也比本地磁盘好。 这将深刻改变数据库存储引擎的设计。 另一方面，数据库厂商和云提供商可以在云上提供完全托管的体验，让用户不必担心运维，可以专注于业务发展。 对于在云上提供数据库服务的厂商来说，这也是一个合理的商业模式。

好的旧 MySQL 过时了吗？

目睹了科技界发生的所有这些变化，我不禁想：MySQL已经过时了吗？ 作为一名数据库内核开发人员，我非常担心 Oracle 对 MySQL 的贡献正在减少。

我不知道甲骨文内部发生了什么，他们向社区做出解释会很棒。

即便如此，我仍然喜欢 MySQL 及其生态系统。 但是我们需要更现代的 MySQL 吗？ 绝对没错。 在我的下一篇文章中，我将与您分享现代 MySQL 生态系统中的一些主要参与者。 敬请关注。

这篇文章是我们系列的第一部分。 您可以在此处查看第二部分：* MySQL 万岁：向生态系统创新者致敬***。



原文:

Author: Ed Huang (Co-Founder & CTO at PingCAP)

This post is the first part in our series. You can see the second part **here: *Long Live MySQL: Kudos to the Ecosystem Innovators***.

Imagine you’re an application developer, and your manager tells you to prototype a new application in a week. What would you choose for your database? Probably MySQL.

MySQL is ubiquitous

Many engineers love MySQL. DB-Engines reports MySQL is the second most popular database; however, some other databases, such as SQLite and IBM Db2, are decreasing in popularity.

Database popularity ranking according to DB-Engines

From the 73,317 responses of Stack Overflow’s 2021 Developer Survey, MySQL ranked as the most popular database.

Database popularity ranking according to Stack Overflow’s 2021 Developer Survey

MySQL is also popular when it comes to hiring. I searched for jobs on Indeed with the keywords of top used databases, and there were 31,245 jobs returned for “mysql.” Job openings that require a “mysql” skillset are the second highest among the top databases.

MySQL is ubiquitous. In fact, at PingCAP, MySQL was one of the few databases a lot of our engineers knew how to use before they joined PingCAP. I was one of them.

My 20 years with MySQL

I used MySQL for the first time when I was a high school student in 2003. Back then, I wanted to develop a web application (Online Judge) with PHP for programming competition hobbyists and help them run their code and check whether it was correct. To store the data users submitted, I needed a database. MySQL was my best choice because it was so straightforward and user friendly. I could use MySQL client to write SQL and operate the database in PHP directly and then Apache rendered the dynamic page results.

In 2012, I joined a startup company whose infrastructure was based on MySQL. I was one of the few infrastructure engineers. The company business grew so fast, its data volume soon spiked beyond MySQL’s single-machine capacity. So I began to seek solutions. For example, I used MySQL binlog for highly available primary and secondary layers and applied horizontal sharding. When I left that company, its database cluster had more than 30 shards which could hold dozens of terabytes. For me, solving these issues was a very fulfilling experience.

Because of these MySQL-related experiences, I had the opportunity to meet many excellent engineers who had similar memories working with MySQL. In 2015, when I decided to start my own business and build a brand new database, MySQL was the first ecosystem that popped into my mind.

Why so many people love MySQL

MySQL’s popularity is unquestionable, although its earlier versions were not as advanced as what we see today. The secret sauce behind its popularity is that MySQL is simple and easy to use. Many user-friendly features like Binlog were introduced to MySQL early as well. As a result, the number of MySQL users grew dramatically in the following three decades. In addition, many users gave their feedback on using MySQL or contributed to MySQL directly, all of which led to a more prosperous MySQL ecosystem. In its heyday, MySQL became a natural choice for many businesses because:

  • Many engineers are using it, making a large talent pool available for database companies.
  • It is open-sourced, making it easy to obtain and use.
  • You could find the answers to almost any MySQL question on the internet.
  • It has a vibrant ecosystem. A large number of open source projects, such as WordPress and phpMyAdmin, heavily relied on MySQL and made it even more prevalent.

Things have changed

During the past 20 years, the world has been changing rapidly and we have witnessed many significant changes in technology such as the explosion of the mobile internet and today’s digital transformation. Our application scenarios have changed enormously as well. What are these changes? Let’s explore them from a data perspective.

Data volume increased exponentially, making distributed technology a must for almost all modern databases. NoSQL databases do a very good job of building a distributed architecture. They provide transparent scalability while maintaining consistent usage, so that developers can scale in and out without worrying about application change. However, NoSQL databases provide users with an even more limited set of functions than SQL databases. This was not a big problem in the early days of Web 2.0 because although data volume increased, business models were relatively simple. Take Twitter for example: in its earliest versions, it provided nothing more than a giant shared note service within a limit of 140 characters.

If enterprises plan to build complicated applications, especially those that require strong consistency and complex association analysis such as JOIN and GROUP BY, SQL is still their best option. Unfortunately, traditional relational databases including MySQL and PostgreSQL made few innovations for a long time even though they embraced distributed technology. They developed their databases for a single machine, making sharding the only mainstream solution to deal with distributed requirements. Recently, however, we have seen some new database projects that can be called real distributed SQL databases.

At first, data models evolved from complicated to simple, and then became even more complicated. This evolution comes with the significant change in technology like the mobile internet explosion. The then-prevalence of NoSQL and sharded databases made data modeling subject to a limited set of functions. For a while, data application modeling had to be simplified to key-value pairs, documents, and other flexible and simple models.

However, when the low-hanging fruit is picked, the really hard problems get trickier. As more industries such as financial services and e-commerce begin their digital transformation to accelerate their online business, the databases’ application scenarios are becoming more complicated. So, the relational model plus SQL is almost always our best database solution. In the last 10 years, we have seen many attempts to model data with new domain-specific languages (DSL)—GraphQL is one example—but I think none of them can shake the dominant place of a relational model plus SQL combination. Google described this situation succinctly in its research paper “F1: A Distributed SQL Database That Scales”: 

"When we sought a replacement for Google’s MySQL data store for the AdWords product, that option was simply not feasible: the complexity of dealing with a non-ACID data store in every part of our business logic would be too great, and there was simply no way our business could function without SQL queries."

The demand has been increasing for stronger consistency and real-time transactional processing and analytics. In the early days, MySQL’s default storage engine was MyISAM, but eventually InnoDB took its place. I think one of the main differences is that InnoDB supports strongly consistent transactions. The more consistent semantics the database layer provides, the less complexity the application layer has to bear. This is especially true when you are dealing with failovers. If the database does not support strong consistency, it is difficult to guarantee data integrity when performing a disaster recovery. This is not acceptable in most financial scenarios.

In the last decade, there have been many innovations in database management technologies. However, one obvious trade off is the separation of Online Transactional Processing (OLTP) and Online Analytical Processing (OLAP). Streaming technologies such as Apache Kafka, Apache Storm, and Apache Spark have emerged to form the so-called “lambda architecture,” which combines the two systems and eliminates the latency of extract, transform, load (ETL). While this approach works in many situations, the architectural complexity introduced has been a tough nut to crack. It is also difficult to keep the data consistent on this head-scratching technology stack. This has caused great challenges for many businesses that require real-time insights at the minute or even millisecond level. Due to the technical barriers, companies might have to suppress their business needs and turn to solutions that are more traditional but easier to tame. As data volume and the demand of real-time analytics have risen in recent years, OLTP and OLAP are on the track of integration.

The way data is generated and consumed is becoming multifaceted. In the past, writing to a database was simple: the client connected directly to the DB through SQL statements. If you take a closer look at how data enters the DB today, you can see that the upstream is much more diversified; it could be a message queue or batch writing from an ETL job. The challenge for batch writing is that users normally care more about the throughput performance than whether the ACID isolation requirements are met. In such scenarios, traditional transaction mechanisms such as locking would be unnecessary. On the other hand, the dramatic rise in real-time requirements lead to the need for data change synchronization to a downstream message queue for consumption. Change Data Capture (CDC) has become a mainstream implementation for modern databases. For example, MySQL has Binlog, Oracle has GoldenGate, and PG has pg replication.

Data quality is increasingly required and the scope of observability extends from the execution panel to the data panel. Twenty years ago, most of our focus was on simple database system metrics such as queries per second (QPS), transactions per second (TPS), latency, and query plan level. With the dramatic increase in data volume and the emergence of distributed databases, we are not satisfied with basic system information; we want to observe how data is accessed, and from that, gain business insights. Take TiDB’s KeyVisualizer as an example. It reflects the distribution of data access in real time so that you can tell at a glance which data is frequently accessed and what are the characteristics of the access.

TiDB’s KeyVisualizer

However, these observability dimensions are still limited to the database system itself. Recently, a new trend has started to emerge: data observability, which tries to measure the quality of data. Anomalo is a good example, and I think this will be a very promising direction for database products.

The hardware infrastructure is changing dramatically. As the cloud is becoming more prevalent, managed services are becoming the mainstream. When MySQL was released, there were no SSDs or 20G NICs. The advances in hardware have alleviated many problems. For example, B+Tree solved the problem of the slow reads and writes of traditional mechanical disks; however, this is not an issue with modern NVMe SSDs. With wide adoption of modern cloud environments, shared storage solutions on the cloud, such as AWS EBS, perform as well, if not better, than local disks. This will profoundly change the design of database storage engines. On the other hand, database vendors and cloud providers can offer fully managed experience on the cloud, so that users won’t have to worry about operations and maintenance and can focus on the business development. This is also a reasonable business model for vendors providing database services on the cloud.

Is good old MySQL outdated?

Having witnessed all those changes happening in the technology world, I could not help but think: is MySQL outdated? As a database kernel developer, I am more than concerned that the contribution from Oracle to MySQL is dwindling.

The growth trend of the number of MySQL contributors

MySQL’s commits trend during past 3 years

I don’t know what’s going on inside Oracle, and it would be great for them to make an explanation to the community.

Even so, I still love MySQL and its ecosystem. The MySQL ecosystem will not go away. MySQL applications and developers are still the foundations of the database market. But do we need a more modern MySQL? Absolutely yes. In my next post, I will share with you some major players in the modern MySQL ecosystem. Stay tuned.

This post is the first part in our series. You can see the second part here: *Long Live MySQL: Kudos to the Ecosystem Innovators***.
item.author: yuxi903

item.title: 使用 Keda 和 Kubernetes 实现事件驱动的 Auto Scaling | Implementing Event Driven Auto Scaling with Keda and Kubernetes
item.description:
使用 Keda、Spring 和 RabbitMQ 实现 Kubernetes 事件驱动的 Auto Scaling

科达

使用 KEDA，您可以明确映射您想要使用事件驱动规模的应用程序，而其他应用程序继续运行。 这使得 KEDA 成为与任何数量的其他 Kubernetes 应用程序或框架一起运行的灵活且安全的选择。

自动缩放是 Kubernetes 最喜欢的功能之一。 当我们谈论 Kubernetes 自动缩放功能时，会自动想到 Horizo​​ntal Pod Autoscaler (HPA)。 与容器外的不同组件集成的复杂和分布式应用程序。 这些组件基本上是事件源，可以是 MongoDB、Azure 服务总线、RabbitMQ 队列长度、Kafka 主题滞后、Redis 流等。HPA 无法根据这些组件的指标扩展 pod。 为了缓解这种情况，KEDA 在 Horizo​​ntal Pod Autoscaler 之上提供了扩展功能。 使用 KEDA，您可以明确映射您想要使用事件驱动规模的应用程序，而其他应用程序继续运行。 它还允许您将 Scaler 集成到 Kubernetes 集群中。 它将帮助您根据来自这些事件源的不同指标来扩展您的 pod。
科达的架构

操作员/代理：激活和停用 Kubernetes 部署以在没有事件的情况下从零扩展到零。

  • 指标：充当 Kubernetes 指标服务器，向 Horizo​​ntal Pod Autoscaler 公开丰富的事件数据，如队列长度或流延迟，以推动横向扩展。

    • 运行和实施科达

    执行该项目时使用了以下技术，需要安装一些项目：

    • 码头工人
    • 爪哇 17
    • 马文
    • 兔MQ

    准备环境

    在 Kubernetes 集群中安装 Keda

    使用 minikube 启动我们的集群。 按照以下命令启动 Kubernetes 集群：

    运行该命令后，您还需要另一个命令来在我们的集群中安装 Keda，执行命令：

     kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kedacore/keda/releases/download/v2.0.0/keda-2.0.0.yaml

    您使用此命令来咨询您的 Keda 安装：

    在 Kubernetes 集群中安装 RabbitMQ

    为此，我使用了 RabbitMQ 集群运算符。 按照以下命令在集群中安装 RabbitMQ：

     kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml

    为您检查集群上的 RabbitMQ 部署：

     kubectl get all -n rabbitmq-system

    在项目event-service-sender中，您有 k8s 路径，并且您正在运行配置 Rabbit 的命令：

     kubectl apply -f k8s/rabbitmq-keda-cluster.yaml

    为您验证 Replica 和所有配置到 Rabbit：

     kubectl describe RabbitmqCluster rabbitmq-keda-cluster kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rabbitmq

    要在 localhost 中完成兔子管理，您需要执行以下命令：

     kubectl port-forward service/rabbitmq-keda-cluster 15672

    现在您可以使用 User:guest 和 Password:guest 访问此端点http://localhost:15672来控制您的 RabbitMQ 并创建您的队列。

    在 Kubernetes 中部署发布者和消费者

    配置 Kubernetes 集群和 Rabbit 后，我​​们的环境就可以运行我们的应用程序并使用 Keda 进行概念验证。

    四个您部署事件发送器和事件接收器，您需要在终端上导航到两个项目的路径并访问您需要执行此命令：

     kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    四、您部署需要执行此命令的事件接收器：

     kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    您需要为这两种服务提供咨询 pod：

    为在终端的两个不同实例中运行两个命令的两个应用程序启用 localhost：

     kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-receiver 9095 kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-sender 9096

    现在，您可以使用 jmeter 进行压力测试，并遵循消费者服务中的可伸缩性



    原文:

    Implementing Kubernetes Event Driven Auto Scaling with Keda, Spring and RabbitMQ

    Keda

    KEDA is a single-purpose and lightweight component that can be added into any Kubernetes cluster. KEDA works alongside standard Kubernetes components like the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler and can extend functionality without overwriting or duplication. With KEDA you can explicitly map the apps you want to use event-driven scale, with other apps continuing to function. This makes KEDA a flexible and safe option to run alongside any number of other Kubernetes applications or frameworks.

    Autoscaling is one the favorite features of Kubernetes. When we talk about Kubernetes autoscaling features, then the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) will automatically come into mind. HPA is responsible for scaling of pods based on CPU or RAM usage.

    Complex and distributed applications which integrate with different components outside of the containers. Those components are basically event sources and can be anything like MongoDB, Azure Service Bus, Length of RabbitMQ queue, Kafka topic lag, Redis Stream, etc. The HPA is not able to scale the pods based on metrics from these components. To mitigate this kind of situation, KEDA comes with extended functionalities on top of the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler. With KEDA you can explicitly map the apps you want to use event-driven scale, with other apps continuing to function. It also allows you to integrate a Scaler into your Kubernetes cluster. It will help you to scale your pods based on the different metrics from those event sources.
    KEDA is a single-purpose and lightweight component that can be added into any Kubernetes cluster. KEDA works alongside standard Kubernetes components like the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler and can extend functionality without overwriting or duplication.

    Architecture of KEDA

    The principal layer in KEDA architecture is:

    • Operator/Agent: Activates and deactivates Kubernetes Deployments to scale to and from zero on no events.
    • Scalers: Connects to an external event source and feeds custom metrics for a specific event source. A current list of scalers is available on the KEDA home page.
    • Metrics: Acts as a Kubernetes metrics server that exposes rich event data like queue length or stream lag to the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler to drive scale out.

    Running and Implementing Keda

    The following technologies were used to carry out the project and it is necessary to install some items:

    • Docker
    • Java 17
    • Maven
    • SpringBoot
    • Kubernetes(Minikube)
    • RabbitMQ

    Preparing Environment

    Install Keda in Kubernetes Cluster

    Keda is a solution for running in kubernetes cluster. Use minikube to start our cluster. Follow the command below to start the kubernetes cluster:

    After running the command, you need another command to install Keda in our cluster, follow the command:

    kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kedacore/keda/releases/download/v2.0.0/keda-2.0.0.yaml

    You used this command for consulting your installation of Keda:

    Installing RabbitMQ in Kubernetes Cluster

    RabbitMQ improves Operator approach. For that I have used the RabbitMQ cluster operator. Follow the command to install RabbitMQ in your cluster:

    kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml

    For you check RabbitMQ deployment on the cluster:

    kubectl get all -n rabbitmq-system

    In the project event-service-sender, you have the k8s path and you are running the command for configure Rabbit:

    kubectl apply -f k8s/rabbitmq-keda-cluster.yaml

    For you to verify Replica and All Configuration to the Rabbit:

    kubectl describe RabbitmqCluster rabbitmq-keda-cluster
kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rabbitmq

    To finalize the rabbit management in the localhost, you need to execute this command:

    kubectl port-forward service/rabbitmq-keda-cluster 15672

    Now you can access this endpoint http://localhost:15672 using User:guest and Password: guest to control your RabbitMQ and Create your Queue.

    Deployment Publisher and Consumer in Kubernetes

    After you configure Kubernetes cluster and Rabbit, our environment is ready for running our applications and proof of concept with Keda.

    Four you deploy Event Sender and Event Receiver, you need navigate on terminal to the path for both project and access the you need execute this command:

    kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    Four you deploy the Event Receiver you need execute this command:

    kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    You need consulting pods for both services:

    For enabling localhost to both applications running the two commands in two different instances to the terminal:

    kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-receiver 9095
kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-sender 9096

    Now, you can use jmeter for stress test and you follow the scalability in your consumer service

item.title: 更大的利润获胜 | The bigger margin wins
item.description:
顶级编码器

发表于当边距不对称时怎么办？ 比如说，顶部元素需要下面 72px 的空间，而底部元素只需要 24px？

较大的数字获胜。

如果您将保证金视为“个人空间”，这会让人感觉很直观。 在历史的这一刻，保持 6 英尺的距离是社会责任。 如果有人想要更多的空间——比如 8 英尺——我们需要保持 8 英尺的距离才能满足两个个人空间的要求。

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - May 5 5月5日



原文:

TopCoder

Posted on May 13

What about when the margins are asymmetrical? Say, the top element wants 72px of space below, while the bottom element only needs 24px?

The bigger number wins.

This one feels intuitive if you think of margin as "personal space". In this moment in history, it's socially responsible to keep 6 feet apart. If someone wants even more space — say, 8 feet — we'll need to keep 8 feet apart in order to satisfy both personal-space requirements.

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - May 5
item.author: topcode007

item.title: 嵌套并不能防止折叠 | Nesting doesn't prevent collapsing
item.description:
好吧，这就是它开始变得奇怪的地方。 考虑以下代码：

 <style> p { margin-top: 48px; margin-bottom: 48px; } </style> <div> <p>Paragraph One</p> </div> <p>Paragraph Two</p>

我们将第一段放入包含<div>的内容中，但边距仍会塌陷！

怎么会这样？ 好吧，事实证明，我们中的许多人对利润的运作方式存在误解。

边距是为了增加兄弟姐妹之间的距离。 它_不是_意味着增加孩子与其父母边界框之间的差距； 这就是填充的用途。

边距总是会尝试增加兄弟元素之间的距离，即使这意味着将边距转移到父元素！ 在这种情况下，效果与我们将边距应用于父<div>而不是子<p>的效果相同。

必须满足一些条件才能将保证金转移到父级（并折叠）：

  • 中间没有其他元素（参见前面关于<br>的规则）。
  • 父元素没有设置height 。
  • 父元素沿相关边缘没有任何填充或边框。 最后一种情况很常见，所以让我们看一个简单的例子。 在这种情况下，我们的嵌套子级不能将边距与下一段结合起来，因为父级有一些填充：

您可以将填充/边框视为一种墙； 如果它位于两个边距之间，它们就不会塌陷，因为有一堵墙挡住了路。 宽度也无关紧要； 即使是 1px 的填充也会干扰边距折叠。



原文:

Alright, here's where it starts to get weird. Consider the following code:

<style>
  p {
    margin-top: 48px;
    margin-bottom: 48px;
  }
</style>
<div>
  <p>Paragraph One</p>
</div>
<p>Paragraph Two</p>

We're dropping our first paragraph into a containing <div>, but the margins will still collapse!

How can this be? Well, it turns out that many of us have a misconception about how margins work.

Margin is meant to increase the distance between siblings. It is _not _meant to increase the gap between a child and its parent's bounding box; that's what padding is for.

Margin will always try and increase distance between siblings, even if it means transferring margin to the parent element! In this case, the effect is the same as if we had applied the margin to the parent <div>, not the child <p>.

“But that can't be!”, I can hear you saying. “I've used margin before to increase the distance between the parent and the first child!”

There are some conditions that must be satisfied in order for the margin to be transferred to the parent (and collapsed):

  • No other elements in-between (see earlier rule, about the <br>).
  • The parent element doesn't have a heightset.
  • The parent element doesn't have any padding or border along the relevant edge. That last condition is really common, so let's look at a quick example. In this case, our nested child can't combine margin with the next paragraph, because the parent has some padding in the way:

You can think of padding/border as a sort of wall; if it sits between two margins, they can't collapse, because there's a wall in the way. The width doesn't matter, either; even 1px of padding will interfere with margin collapse.

item.title: 只有相邻元素折叠 | Only adjacent elements collapse
item.description:
顶级编码器

发表于使用<br />标签（换行符）来增加块元素之间的空间有些常见。

 <style> p { margin-top: 32px; margin-bottom: 32px; } </style> <p>Paragraph One</p> <br /> <p>Paragraph Two</p>

遗憾的是，这对我们的利润率产生了不利影响：

元素需要在 DOM 中相邻才能使它们的边距折叠*。

Mahmoud Harmouch - Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 24 4月24日

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22 4月22日



原文:

TopCoder

Posted on May 13

It is somewhat common to use the <br /> tag (a line-break) to increase space between block elements.

<style>
  p {
    margin-top: 32px;
    margin-bottom: 32px;
  }
</style>
<p>Paragraph One</p>
<br />
<p>Paragraph Two</p>

Regrettably, this has an adverse effect on our margins:

The <br /> tag is invisible and empty, but any element between two others will block margins from collapsing. Elements need to be adjacent in the DOM for their margins to collapse*.

Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 24

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22
item.author: topcode007

item.title: InfoAbout.me | InfoAbout.me
item.description:
这个应用程序的主要目的是让用户使用这个应用程序在各种社交媒体平台上查看自己上传的内容，这就是为什么应用程序被命名为InfoAbout.me的原因。

大多数时候，全球各地的人们必须在一天内访问各种应用程序或社交媒体平台，以检查他们在社交媒体平台上上传的内容，因此牢记这个问题，我制作了这个 Web 应用程序，人们可以在其中查看上传的内容他/她使用这个单一应用程序在 twitter 和 Dev 上发布的内容。

由于时间限制，我只集成了两个应用程序（Twitter + Dev）简单的功能，所以使用InfoAbout.me的用户只能看到他们上传的内容，不能对他们上传的内容进行任何操作。

将来这个应用程序将由我增强，并且像 auth2 等 appwrite 服务也将在其中实现。

目前，此应用程序允许用户使用 InfoAbout.me 登录，如果用户没有帐户，则用户需要创建一个帐户。

成功登录后，用户需要通过 Twitter 和 Dev 授权 InfoAbout.me 应用程序，以便 infoAbout.me 可以成功地从这些应用程序中获取用户数据。

提交类别：

链接到代码

这个项目是使用Redux和Redux Toolkit模板通过Create React App引导的。

你想在你的系统上本地运行这个应用程序吗？

要为本地开发设置前端，请执行以下提到的每个步骤：

  1. 安装运行此应用程序所需的所有依赖项。
  2. 从https://appwrite.io/在您的系统上安装 appwrite。
  3. 然后在 src/utils/config.js 文件的 'endpoint' 变量中设置你的 appwrite 的本地域名。
  4. 通过本地 appwrite 中的 localhost:port 创建一个项目。
  5. 并获取项目 ID 并将其保存到 src/utils/config.js 的“项目”中。
  6. 并通过 localhost:port 在您的 appwrite 中创建一些数据库集合。 集合必须如下所示

  1. 将 UsersInfo 集合的集合 ID 放在 src/utils/config.js 的 collectionID 中。
  2. 并将 Dev-Credential 的集合 ID 放在 src/utils/config.js 的 devCredential 中。
  3. 并在最后一次运行npm start在根文件夹中。

这里…

演示视频

Management of Appwrite's Database collections Appwrite 数据库集合的管理
